description: White in center to Pink on outside. Small, single.

name: Asakura
description: White shaded Pink. Medium, semidouble.

name: Ashley Black

name: Ashley Variegated

name: Ashton's High Rise

name: Ashton's Red Sunset

name: Ashton's Shooting Star
description: Bright Red (RHS 54A) stripes over White background with Golden Yellow anthers and Creamy White filaments. Large, single. Average, spreading, dense growth. M. (Japonica 'Mrs. Bertha A. Harris' x Oleifera 'Plain Jane'). (U.S. 2010 - Ackerman).

name: Ashton's Snow
description: White. Medium, single to semidouble form. Average, dense, spreading growth. E-L. Cold hardy to -15°F. (Japonica 'Billie McCaskill'× Oleifera 'Plain Jane'). (U.S. 2002 - Ackerman).

name: Aspasia Macarthor
description: (‘Paeoniaeflora’; ‘Flore Celeste’). White to Cream White with a few Rose Red lines and dashes. Medium, full peony form. Slow, upright growth. E-M. (For other forms of this cultivar see Japonicas ‘Camden Park’; ‘Jean Clere’; ‘Lady Loch’; ‘Margaret Davis’; ‘Otahuhu Beauty’; ‘Strawberry Blonde’). (Aus. 1850 - Macarthur).

name: Aspasia Macarthur
description: ('Paeoniaeflora'; 'Flore Celeste'). White to Cream White with a few Rose Red lines and dashes. Medium, full peony form. Slow, upright growth. E-M. (For other forms of this cultivar see Japonicas 'Camden Park'; 'Jean Clere'; 'Lady Loch'; 'Margaret Davis'; 'Otahuhu Beauty'; 'Strawberry Blonde'). (Aus. 1850 - Macarthur).

name: Astra

name: Astro Nova

name: Atago

Leaves small, elliptic. Upright growth. Breeding unknown, released by Soshin Hirai, Kurume, Saitama Pref., Japan.

name: Athelyne Variegated
description: Red blotched White variation of "Athelyne'.

name: Athena

name: Aubrey Harris
description: Orange Pink to Red moiré White. Large, semidouble with some upright center petals. Slow, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1959 - Judice).

name: Auburn Moonbright
description: White with Yellow anthers and Yellow filaments. Very large, semidouble. Average, upright growth. E. (Japonica 'Moonlight Bay' × Japonica 'Joshua Youtz'). (U.S. 2017 - Kenneth Rogers, Auburn, AL).

name: Auburn Sunrise
description: Orchid Pink with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Large, semidouble form. Vigorous, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 2020 - James Walker and Leo Brown, Mobile, AL).

name: Auburn White

name: Audrey Claire

name: Auguste Delfosse (Belgium)

name: Augusto Leal De Gouveia Pinto

('Another variation of this cultivar includes Japonica 'Shepherdess'; 'Sport of Japonica 'Grand Sultan'). (Portugal 1980 - Da Silva).

name: Aunty Lola
description: White. Medium, single. Average, upright growth. E-L. (N.Z. 1992 - B. Simmons, Blenheim).

name: Autumn Carnival

name: Autumn Dawn

name: Autumn Daze

name: Autumn Delight

name: Autumn Glory

name: Autumn Herald

name: Autumn Mist
description: Pure White with Pink edges gradually fading to the center. Large, single. E-M. (U.S. 2008 - Parks). M. Cold hardy. (U.S. 1811 - Originator unknown; 1990 - Registered by

name: Autumn Moon
description: White. Medium, single with very thick petals.

name: Autumn Rocket
description: White. Medium, anemone to rose form double.

name: Autumn Sunrise
description: White with a Red tip on each petal. Large, single-cup-shaped. Vigorous, dense and upright growth. (U.S. - Parks).

name: Avalanche

description: White. Medium, semidouble.

name: Awa-Yuki

name: Ayer Maid

name: Azora

name: Babette

name: Babs Alsip

name: Baby Angel
description: White. Miniature, semidouble. Average, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 1968 - E. Pieri, San Gabriel, CA).

name: Baby Brother

name: Baby Willow

name: Ballerina Belle

name: Barbara Morgan

Leaf Characteristics: Dark green. Originator: Tea Gardens. Place of Origination: U.S. - Summerville, South Carolina. Year of Registration / Origination: 1890 - Europe to U.S..

name: Beatrice Hooper
description: White. Large, formal double to rose form double. Compact growth. M. (Aus. 1960 - G. Hooper, Bexley).

description: Red heavily variegated White. Medium, peony form. Average growth. M. (U.S. 1957).

name: Betty Goodwin

description: Bright Red with occasional White blotches. Medium, semidouble to anemone form. Slow, compact growth. M. (Not same as 'Elegans (Chandler)'). (England 1825 - Chandler).

name: Chandleri Elegans Improved
description: Rose Pink with center petaloids often spotted White. Large to very large, anemone form. Average, spreading growth; blossoms and leaves are larger than parent and growth is more vigorous. E-M. (U.S. 1985 - Nuccio's).

name: Chapel Bells
description: White. Medium to large, peony form. (U.S. 1965 - Tammia).

name: Charlean Variegated
description: Color: Translucent pink blotched white with pink filaments and lemon yellow anthers. Form: Semidouble. Size: Large. Bloom Season: M-L. Growth Rate: Vigorous. Growth Characteristics: Spreading, upright growth. Leaf Characteristics: Light green. Place of Origination: U.S.. Heritage: Virus variegated of Non-reticulata hybrid 'Charlean'.

name: Charlene Whiddon
description: Medium Light Pink with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Very Large, semidouble to loose peony form. Average, upright, dense growth. E-M. (U.S. 2021 - Pat B. Johnson, Cairo, GA).

name: Charles Augustus Jones
description: Edge of petals Light Pink, shading to Pure White at center of flower. Medium, semidouble. Vigorous, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 2005 - H. Finley Jones, Lake Charles, LA).

name: Charles Henty
description: China Pink, veined deeper and shading to White edge. Large, semidouble to loose peony form. Vigorous, open, upright growth. M. (For a variation of this cultivar see Japonica 'Robert Henty'). (Aus. 1967 - Henty).

name: Charles Kahn
description: White with each petal bearing several bright Red streaks. Medium, loose peony form. Vigorous, willowy growth. E-M. (U.S. 1951 - J. L. Kahn, Pensacola, FL).

name: Charles Minarik
description: White. Medium, single. Vigorous, upright growth. L. Cold hardy. (U.S. 1991 - C. and D. Minarik, West Harwich, MA).

name: Charles S. Tait Sr.
description: Light Pink variegated White. Medium, semidouble with rosette of petaloids. Average, compact growth. (U.S. 1956 - Tait).

name: Charles Zerkowsky
description: Soft Pink flecked White. Large, peony form to rose form double. Average, upright growth. E-M. (U.S. 1982 - Tammia).

name: Charley V
description: Red and White with Yellow anthers. Medium, loose peony form. Vigorous, upright growth. M. (U.S. 2009 - Hal Vanis, Henderson, TX).

name: Charlie Adams
description: Bright Red with White variegation with Yellow anthers and Yellow filaments. Large, semidouble. Average, upright, open growth. M-L. (U.S. 2013 - Robert A. Stroud, Slidell, LA).

name: Charlie Bettes
description: White with Deep Yellow stamens. Large to very large, semidouble. Vigorous, compact growth. E. (U.S. 1960 - C. Bettes, Jacksonville, FL).

name: Charlie's Jeanne
description: Medium Red with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Very large, semidouble. Average, upright, open growth. M-L. (U.S. 2018 - Pat Johnson, Cairo, GA).

name: Charlotte Blount Variegated
description: Color: Strawberry red marbled white with white anthers and white filaments. Form: Semidouble to peony form with 85 to 94 petals. Size: Large. Bloom Season: M. Growth Rate: Average. Growth Characteristics: Upright Growth. Leaf Characteristics: Dark green. Place of Origination: U.S.. Year of Registration / Origination: 1980. Heritage: Virus variegated form of Japonica 'Charlotte Blount'.

name: Charlotte Bradford
description: Phlox Pink variegated White in varied degrees. Medium, semidouble. (Sport of Japonica Mrs. Baldwin Wood'). (U.S. 1950 - Bradford).

name: Charlotte Grace
description: Dark Pink at the petal margins fading to Light Pink with some striping with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Small, semidouble to rose form double. Average, upright, open growth. M-L. (U.S. 2020 - Pat B. Johnson, Cairo, GA).

name: Charlotte Hoak
description: Porcelain White. Medium, single with irregular fluted petals and unique stamen formation. Vigorous, compact, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1955 - McCaskill).

name: Charlotte Johnson Variegated
description: Black Red and White variation of 'Charlotte Johnson'. (U.S. 1980 - Alfter).

name: Charlotte Jones
description: Medium Pink with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Large, semidouble to rose form double. Average, upright, spreading growth. M-L. (U.S. 2018 - Frank Pursel & Hulyn Smith).

name: Charlotte Walker
description: White. Medium, semidouble. Average, compact, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1962 - H. C. Wilson, Fresno, CA).

name: Charter
description: White. Large, anemone form. (U.S. 1950 - Mrs. R. Flournoy, Columbus, GA).

name: Cheerleader
description: Deep Rose Pink blotched White variation of "Touchdown'. Large to very large, loose peony form. (U.S. 1965 - Nuccio's).

name: Cherries Jubilee
description: Burgundy Red with Red and White petaloids intermingled with stamens. Medium, full semidouble to rose form double. Average, compact, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1983 - Nuccio's).

name: Cherries Jubilee Variegated
description: Color: Burgundy red blotched white with red and white petaloids intermingled with stamens. Form: Full semidouble to rose form double. Size: Medium. Bloom Season: M. Growth Rate: Average. Growth Characteristics: Compact, upright growth. Leaf Characteristics: Dark green. Originator: Valdosta Camellia Scions. Place of Origination: U.S. - Valdosta, Georgia. Year of Registration / Origination: 1993. Heritage: Virus variegated form of Japonica 'Cherries Jubilee'.

name: Cherry Blossom (Cherry Leaf Camellia)
description: Color: White with pink and red specks and peppering to red streaks with yellow anthers. Form: Single with 7-9 wide, overlapping, crinkled petals and a small, central stamen cluster.. Size: Large. Bloom Season: M. Leaf Characteristics: Foliage resembles that of a cherry tree. Originator: 1867. Place of Origination: Japan. Other Information: Called 'Sakuraba-tsubaki' in Japanese.

name: Cherry Frost
description: Red with Red and White petaloids. Miniature, anemone form. Average growth. M-L. (U.S. 1987 - D. Bergamini, Martinez, CA).

name: Cheryl Hunter Variegated
description: Deep Red with White moiré variegation with Yellow anthers and Red filaments. Very large, semidouble. Average, upright, open growth. M. (Virus variegated form of Japonica 'Cheryl Hunter"). (U.S. 2016 - Thomas Sellers, Bolivia, NC).

name: Chief Slack
description: White blotched and striped Red on one side of flower. Large, peony form. Average, open growth. M. (U.S. 1951 - G. J. Slack, Pensacola, FL).

name: Childe Harold
description: White. Large, semidouble to peony form with satin sheen on petals. (Portugal 1961).

name: China Clay
description: White. Medium, semidouble. Open growth. (Saluenensis × Japonica 'Marjorie Magnificent'). (England 1972 - Carlyon).

name: China Doll
description: Blush White edged Coral. Medium to large, loose high-centered peony form with fluted petals. Average, compact growth. M. (U.S. 1958 - Shackelford).

name: Chiyoda-nishiki
description: Color: Soft pink marbled white and rose pink with yellow anthers and yellow filaments. Form: Semidouble. Size: Medium. Bloom Season: M. Growth Rate: Vigorous. Growth Characteristics: Low, spreading growth. Originator: Chugai. Place of Origination: Japan - ChÃ»bu. Year of Registration / Origination: 1935.

name: Chiyoda-Nishiki (Myraid Brocade)
description: ('Chitose-Nishiki'; 'Mallot Variegated'; 'Nana-Komachi'; 'Princess Bacahachie'; 'Princess Nagasaki'). Soft Pink marbled White and Rose Pink. Medium, semidouble. Vigorous, low, spreading growth. M. (Japan 1935 - Chugai).

name: Cho-No-Hanagata (Butterfly Flower Form)
description: ('Dorothea Blanche'; 'Heart's Desire'; 'Robinson 56'). Pink shaded White. Medium, semidouble to loose peony form. Average, upright, open growth. M. (Japan to U.S. [Star] 1930).

name: Choju-Raku
description: Light Pink. Medium, cup-shaped single with flared White stamens. See Higo.

name: Chow's Han-Ling
description: White with Blush Pink center and occasional petals slightly striped Pink. Large to very large, semidouble to loose peony to formal double. (Sport of Japonica 'Carter's Sunburst'). (For a variation of this cultivar see Japonica 'Han-Ling Snow'). (U.S. 1971 - L E Chow, Bakersfield, CA).

name: Chris Bergamini
description: White, striped with varying shades of Pinks and Reds. Miniature to small, anemone form. Average, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1999 - Don Bergamini, Martinez, CA).

name: Chrissie
description: White. Medium, semidouble with pointed petals. Vigorous, bushy growth. M. (U.S. 1950 - Magnolia).

name: Chrissie's Retic
description: White in center shading to Pale Pink at edge. Very large, peony form. Average, upright, compact growth. M. (Reticulata hybrid). (N.Z. 1987 - Haydon).

name: Christieline
description: Blush Pink with White anthers and White filaments. Medium, rose form double. Vigorous, dense, upright growth. E-L. (U.S. 2004 - Lionel Worthy, Gainesville, FL).

name: Christine Collins
description: Blush Pink with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Medium to large, semidouble to loose peony form. Slow, upright growth. L. (U.S. 2011 - James P. Taylor (deceased), Quitman, GA).

name: Christine Dodd
description: Deep Pink outer petals with Light Pink to White inner petals. Very large, semidouble form; folded outer petals give bloom star shape. Average, upright spreading growth. M. (U.S. 1999 - Bond Nursery Corp., Dallas, TX).

name: Christine Smith Variegated
description: Rose Pink and White variation of 'Christine Smith'. (U.S. 1963 - C. O. Smith, Theodore, AL).

name: Christmas Beauty Variegated
description: Bright Red blotched White variation of 'Christmas Beauty'.

name: Christmas Candles
description: Bright Red. Medium, semidouble. CHRISTMAS CAROL - Formerly Vernalis - Coral Red blooms have highly variable White picotee edges with Yellow anthers and White filaments. 7 mine left in chanter Medium, semidouble. Vigorous, upright, semi-weeping growth. E-M. (Sasanqua 'Egao' x Japonica Tama cultivar). (U.S. 2021 - Bobby Green, Green Nurseries, Fairhope, AL).

name: Christmas Daffodil
description: White tinged Blush Pink at petal tips. Small, anemone form. Vigorous, compact growth. E-M. (Japonica Elizabeth Boardman' × Hybrid 'Tiny Princess'). (U.S. 1971 - Witman).

name: Christmas Day
description: Red with a wide White border on each petal with Yellow anthers and Pink filaments. Medium, loose peony form. Average, upright, open growth. E-M. (Seedling of Japonica 'Merry Christmas'). (U.S. 2019 - Pat Johnson, Cairo, GA).

name: Christmas Star
description: White. Large, semidouble with outer petals producing a star formation and shorter, inner petals and intermingled petaloids and stamens in center. Average growth. M. (U.S. 1951 - Camelliana).

name: Chuck Ritter
description: Medium Pink to Darker Pink with Golden anthers and White filaments. Large, semidouble to full peony; flower form and color varies on the same bush; the placement and number of stamens varies with each bloom; may have petaloids mixed with stamens. Average, upright, open growth. L. (U.S. 2015 - Hulyn Smith)

name: Chuck's Magic
description: White with Red on outer edge of petals. Medium, full peony; the blooms are consistently smaller than the parent plant. Vigorous, upright, open growth. M. (Sport of Japonica 'Magic City'). (U.S. 2015 - Chuck Ritter, Melrose, FL)

name: Chujohaku
description: Pure White with Yellow anthers and Yellow filaments. Large, single occasionally anemone. Slow, spreading growth. E-L. (Japan 1934 - Kansai District). See Higo.

name: Chujoshiro
description: White. Medium, single. (Japan 1912). See Higo.

name: Chung Cho Yang
description: White with central portion Sulphur Yellow and flecked Pink. Medium, formal double. (Sport of Japonica 'Eighteen Scholars'). (U.S. [Peer] from Formosa/Taiwan 1954).

name: Chûjôhaku
description: Pure White with Yellow anthers and Yellow filaments. Large, single occasionally anemone. Slow, spreading growth. E-L. (Japan 1934 - Kansai District). See Higo

name: Chûjôshiro
description: White. Medium, single. (Japan 1912). See Higo.

name: Chūbu-senbazuru
description: Yokoyama & Kirino, 1989, Nihon no Chinka, p.64, description, p.65, colour photo: Small sized, vase-shaped single of 5 rounded petals and a central stamen cylinder with white filaments, joined for lower third, and light yellow anthers. Blooms mid-season. Leaves glossy, dark green, slender, elliptic, apices acuminate, margins serrulate. A bushy plant. Originated in the Chūbu district, Japan.

name: Cile Stanaland
description: White. Large, peony form. Average, spreading growth. M. (U.S. 1970 - C. R. Stanaland, Jr., Jacksonville, FL).

name: Cile Watford
description: White. Medium, rose form double. Vigorous, upright, dense growth. M. (U.S. 2009 - Homeyer).

name: Cilie Sutton
description: White with Blush Pink tinges on outer edges of petals. Medium, single. Vigorous, upright, spreading growth. M. (Japonica 'Rebel Yell' x Japonica 'Omega'). (U.S. 2008 - CamelliaShop, Savannah, GA).

name: Cinderella
description: Predominantly White with streaks and blotches of Rose madder. Medium, semidouble with irregular and somewhat cupped petals, and with petal edges deeply lacinated and wrinkled. (Sport of Japonica 'Fred Sander'). (For a variation of this cultivar see Japonica 'Raspberry Ice'). (U.S. 1955 - Arnesen).

name: Cindy Ann
description: White dashed Pink. Large, semidouble with petals incurved. Vigorous, upright growth. E. (U.S. 1961 - Ashby).

name: Cindy B
description: White striped Rose. Very large, semidouble. Vigorous, open, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1965 - Herrin).

name: Cinnamon Cindy
description: Rose Pink with White center petaloids. Miniature, peony form, Deep cinnamon fragrance. E-M. (Japonica 'Kenyo- Tai' x Lutchuensis). (U.S. 1973 - Ackerman).

name: Cinnamon Scentsation
description: Rose Pink to White. Small, single. Average, open, spreading growth. M-L. Highly fragrant. (Sport of Hybrid 'Cinnamon Cindy'). (U.S. 1995 - Ackerman).

name: Circuit Rider
description: White in center shading to Pale Pink at edge. Medium, loose peony form. Average, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 1972 - E. P. Akin, Shreveport, LA).

name: Circus
description: Strawberry with multiple Red and Pink stripes in variable lengths throughout the bloom with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Large to very large, semidouble to loose peony to occasionally anemone form. Average, upright growth. M. Sport of Japonica 'Ferris Wheel. (U.S. 2020 - Nuccio's).

name: Circus Girl
description: Multicolor of every color combination from Red to Pink and variegations of White, Pink and Red. Large, semidouble. (U.S. 1958 - Shackelford).

name: Claire Bull
description: Light Pink with White flecks. Large, formal double. Average, dense, upright growth. M-L. (Hybrid seedling). (N.Z. 1990 - D. Bull, Auckland).

name: Claire Renee Variegated
description: Dark Pink blotched White variation of 'Claire Renee'.

name: Claire Thompson
description: White slightly Blushed Pink, occasionally blotched Deep Pink. Large, semidouble to anemone form. Vigorous, compact growth. M. (U.S. 1950 - Dr. O. R. Thompson, Macon, GA).

name: Clara Echols Mann
description: Rose Pink with White variegated center petals. Medium, semidouble. Vigorous, upright growth. E-M. (U.S. 1967 - E. A. Mann, Danielsville, GA).

name: Clare Tomlinson
description: Pink with strong Lavender cast with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Large to very large, semidouble to loose peony form. Average, upright, open growth. M-L. (U.S. 2013 - Richard Dodd, Marshallville, GA).

name: Clarise Carleton Variegated
description: Red blotched White variation of 'Clarise Carleton.'.

name: Claritas Alba
description: White. Medium, semidouble. Loose, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. Late 1800's - Magnolia).

name: Clark Hubbs Variegated
description: Dark Red and White variation of 'Clark Hubbs'.

name: Claudia Phelps
description: ('Coral Duchess'; 'Tillie Rice'). Delicate Pink shading to White toward edge and sometimes splashed White. Large, semidouble. (Sport of Japonica 'Duchess of Sutherland'). (U.S. 1948 - Fruitland).

name: Cleopatra White
description: White. Medium, semidouble.

name: Cleve James Variegated
description: Color: Deep rose blotched white with yellow anthers and white filaments. Form: Peony form. Size: Large to very large. Bloom Season: M. Growth Rate: Vigorous. Growth Characteristics: Compact, upright growth. Leaf Characteristics: Dark green. Place of Origination: U.S.. Year of Registration / Origination: 1990. Heritage: Virus variegated form of Japonica 'Cleve James'.

name: Cliff Harris
description: White and Salmon Rose Pink. Medium to large, semidouble to loose peony form. (Variegated form of Japonica 'Her Majesty Queen Elizabeth Ii'). (U.S. 1959 - Longview).

name: Cliveana (Gerbing)
description: Color: Light pink with yellow anthers and white filaments. Form: Anemone form. Size: Medium. Bloom Season: L. Growth Rate: Vigorous. Growth Characteristics: Compact growth. Leaf Characteristics: Dark glossy green. Originator: Gerbing Camellia Nursery. Place of Origination: U.S. - Fernandina, Florida. Year of Registration / Origination: 1939.

name: Cloisonne
description: Soft Pink with opaque heavy petals delicately outlined White. Medium to large, semidouble. Vigorous, upright growth. M- L. (U.S. 1988 - McCaskill).

name: Clower White
description: White with Golden anthers. Medium, loose peony form. Vigorous, open growth. M. (Japonica 'Gloire de Nantes' x Japonica 'Nobilissima'). (U.S. 1949 - Clower).

name: Clown
description: Rose Red, clear Red, Dark Red and White in fine stripes. Medium, full peony form. Average growth. M. (U.S. 1960 - Julington).

name: Coach Mathis
description: Rose Pink, commonly variegated White with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Medium to large, full peony to anemone form. Average, spreading growth. E-L. (U.S. 2019 - Clayton Mathis, Douglas, GA).

name: Cocoanut Ice
description: Pink striped White. Medium, single.

name: Colletii
description: ('Colletii Maculata'; 'Girard Debaillon'; 'Purpliana'; 'Genevieve de Barbier'). Red blotched White, varying from nearly solid Red to nearly Pure White. Small to medium, full peony form. Slow, bushy growth. E-M. (Belgium 1843 - M. Makoy, Liege).

name: Colonial Dame
description: ('Gertrude Murray'; 'Goldlocks'). White, occasionally Blushed Pink. Large, semidouble to peony form to formal double. Open, slender, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1955 - Tick Tock).

name: Colonial Lady
description: ('Crystal Lake'; 'Striped'; 'White Jordan's Pride'). White with Rose Red stripes and flecks. Medium, semidouble. Fragrant. (Sport of Japonica 'Herme'). (For other forms of this cultivar see 'Orchid Pink'; 'Spring Sonnet'). (U.S. 1938 - McCaskill).

name: Commander Mulroy
description: Blush to White edged Pink with Pink bud center. Medium, formal double. Average, compact, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1961 - T. C. Patin, Hammond, LA).

name: Commanding General Fredrickson
description: Red with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Small, semidouble. Average, upright, dense growth. E-L. (U.S. 2013 - Gena Owens Fredrickson, Wilmington, NC).

name: Compton's Brow
description: Pink or White. Medium, single. (England 1953).

name: Comte De Nesselrode
description: Deep Pink striped and splotched Red. Medium, semidouble to loose peony form. Average, upright growth. M-L. (Possibly not same as cultivar listed in old literature, which was Rose Pink tipped and edged White). (Germany [Seidel] to U.S. [McIlhenny] 1937).

name: Comte de Nesselrode (MG)
description: Color: Deep pink striped and spotted red with golden anthers and yellowish-white filaments. Form: Loose peony form. Size: Medium. Bloom Season: M-L. Growth Rate: Medium. Growth Characteristics: Upright growth. Place of Origination: U.S.. Heritage: Believed to be a color sport of Japonica 'Margaret Walker'.

name: Comtesse De Flers
description: Peach Pink shading to White. Large, semidouble with wavy crinkled petals. Average growth. E-L. (U.S. 1973 - H. W. Turner, Ridgeland, SC).

name: Confetti
description: White splotched Red. Miniature to small, formal double to anemone form. Vigorous, bushy, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1971 - McCaskill).

name: Confetti Blush
description: Pink with occasional Red stripe and edged White. (Sport of Japonica 'Confetti'). (U.S. 1971 - McCaskill).

name: Conquistador Variegated
description: Coral Rose and White variation of 'Conquistador.'.

name: Conrad Hilton
description: ('White High Hat"'). White. Medium to large, peony form. (Sport of Japonica 'High Hat'). (U.S. 1955 - E. R. Honn, Arcadia, CA).

name: Cool Breeze
description: White with Yellow anthers and Cream filaments. Medium, rose form double. Vigorous, upright, dense growth. E-M. (U.S. 2016 - Glenn Read, Lucedale, MS).

name: Coolgardie Star
description: White. Miniature, anemone form to rose form double. Fragrant.

name: Coppelia Alba
description: White. Medium, single. Upright growth. M. (Saluenensis x Japonica). (England 1955).

name: Coquetti
description: See 'Glen 40'. ('Coquetti' is reported as priority name for this cultivar but, as 'Glen 40' has been in such common use in the U.S., we do not believe a change is necessary or warranted. Also not same as cultivar listed in old literature, which was an anemone form of Deep Salmon Pink often streaked White).

name: Cora Nelson
description: White. Medium, formal double to rose form double. Average, upright growth. E-M. (U.S. 1957 - C. M. Nelson, Pearl River, LA).

name: Coral Delight Variegated
description: Coral Pink and blotched White variation of 'Coral Delight'. (U.S. 1975 - Kramer).

name: Coral Mist
description: White veined and splotched clear Pink. Medium, loose peony form with dense collar of petaloids and stamens between ruffled petals. Vigorous, compact, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1959 - Shackelford).

name: Coral Pink Lotus Variegated
description: Coral Pink blotched White variation of 'Coral Pink Lotus'.

name: Cornelia White
description: White. Medium, formal double. M. (U.S. 1953 - Florida L and N Co.).

name: Cornelian
description: (Damanao' - Chinese Name). Turkey Red to Deep Rose Pink marbled White. Large to very large, semidouble to peony form with irregular, wavy, crinkled, spiraled petals and a few petaloids in center. Vigorous, compact growth. M. (Variegated form of Reticulata 'Lion Head'). (Yunnan Reticulata). (China to U.S. 1948 - Descanso and Peer)

name: Cornelian Chrysanthemum Petal
description: ('Manao Juban' - (Chinese Name). Pink shaded White. Medium, rose form double. E. (Yunnan Reticulata). (China to U.S. 1980 - U. C. Bot. Gdn, CA)

name: Cornelian Purple Gown
description: ('Manao Zipao' - Chinese Name). Turkey Red marked White. Large to very large, semidouble with irregular, twisted, upright petals. M. (Yunnan Reticulata). (China to U.S. 1980 - U. C. Bot. Gdn, CA).

name: Cornish Snow
description: White with occasional Pink Blush. Small, single. Open, upright growth. M. (Saluenensis x Cuspidata). (England 1950 - Williams).

name: Coronation
description: White. Very large, semidouble. Vigorous, open, spreading growth. M. (U.S. 1954 - McCaskill).

name: Coronation (US)
description: Color: White with golden anthers and yellowish-white filaments. Form: Semidouble. Size: Very large. Bloom Season: M. Growth Rate: Vigorous. Growth Characteristics: Open, spreading growth. Originator: McCaskill Gardens. Place of Origination: U.S. - Pasadena, California. Year of Registration / Origination: 1954. Heritage: Seedling of Japonica 'Lotus'.

name: Corroboree
description: White striped Crimson. Medium, semidouble. M. (Aus. 1962 - Waterhouse).

name: Cottage Queen
description: White with Deeper Rose colored base petals. Medium, single to semidouble. Strong, upright, open growth. M. {(Pitardii var. 'Yunnanica' x Reticulata hybrid 'Forty-Niner') x [(Pitardii var. 'Yunnanica' × Reticulata 'Purple Gown') x (Saluenensis × Transnokoensis)]}. (U.S. 2007 - Charvet).

name: Cottontail
description: White. Miniature, full peony form. Average, compact, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1965 - McCaskill).

name: Countess Of Orkney
description: Cream White striped Rose Pink. Large, peony form. Slow, compact growth. M. (Variations of this cultivar include Japonicas 'Burnham Beeches' and 'Countess of Orkney Rosea'). (England 1847 - Nicholson).

name: Countess of Orkney (England)
description: Color: Cream white striped rose pink with yellow anthers. Form: Peony form. Size: Large. Bloom Season: M. Growth Rate: Slow. Growth Characteristics: Compact growth. Originator: Mr. Nicholson, Gardener to the Earl of Orkney. Place of Origination: England - Orkney. Year of Registration / Origination: 1847.

name: Countess Of Orkney (United States)
description: ('Americana'; 'Maid of Orleans').- White streaked Rose. Medium, formal double to rose form double. Slow, compact growth. (U.S. 1848).

name: Country Doctor Variegated
description: Deep metallic Pink and White variation of 'Country Doctor".

name: Courageous
description: Red. Large, anemone form with petaloids flecked White. Slow growth. M. (U.S. 1965 - J. E. Marscher, Beaufort, SC).

name: Court Yard White
description: White. Medium, semidouble. (U.S. 1956 - Tait).

name: Courtesan
description: Bright Red with Darker Red stripes to Light Pink margined White. Medium, semidouble of bell shape. Average, compact, upright growth. M. (Aus. 1971 - Tuckfield).

name: Courtney Dossett
description: White. Miniature, formal double; the petals are pointed, shape is irregular, never a perfectly round bloom. Average, upright, spreading growth. L. (U.S. 2016 - Howell).

name: Courtney Hall
description: White. Medium, formal double with reverse petals that curl inward. Average, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 1996 - Marion Hall, Dothan AL).

name: Coventry
description: White. Large, semidouble with loose, wavy petals and six clusters of stamens intermingled with petaloids. Average, open growth. M-L. (U.S. 1970 - Haynie).

name: Crane's Feather
description: White. Very large, anemone form. Slow, open growth. M. (N.Z. 1967 - Mrs. T. Durrant, Tirau).

name: Crazy Sue
description: Red to Pink to White blotched Red or Pink and various other combinations of Red and/or Pink and/or White. Medium, peony form. Vigorous, dense, upright growth. E-M. (U.S. 1995 - W. Smith, Gainesville, FL).

name: Cream Puff
description: Chalky White. Medium, peony form. Average, compact growth. M-L. Cold hardy to -5°F. (U.S. 1986 - Ackerman).

name: Creation Variegated
description: White with occasional Pink spot variation of 'Creation'. (U.S. 1959 - McCaskill).

name: Creek
description: White. Medium, single.

name: Creighton Delight
description: Pink with White frosting with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Very large, semidouble. Slow, upright, open growth. L. (Seedling of Reticulata hybrid 'Suzanne Withers'). (U.S. 2016 - Walter Creighton, Semmes, AL).

name: Crepe Rosette
description: Deep Pink veined Red with White margined petals. Medium, semidouble with twisted outer petals intermingled with stamens. Average, compact growth. E-L. (U.S. 1941 - Middleton).

name: Cresta Blanca
description: White. Very large, semidouble. Vigorous, upright growth. M-L. (Japonica × Reticulata 'Crimson Robe'). (U.S. 1961 - Feathers).

name: Crimson Tide

(Sasanqua 'Star Above Star' seedling). (U.S. 2012 - Bobby Green, Green Nurseries, Fairhope, AL). DAWN - Formerly Vernalis - See 'Gin-ryu'. DAWN SONG - Formerly Vernalis - Pink edged White. Small, full irregular semidouble.

name: Crowned Prince

name: Crowning Glory
description: White tipped Lavender Pink. Miniature, anemone form. Average, compact, upright growth. E-L. (U.S. 1977 - Feathers).

name: Cruciata

Leaf Characteristics: Dark green. Originator: Soeurs Guichard. Place of Origination: France - Nantes. Year of Registration / Origination: 1908.

name: Crushed Strawberry
description: Red, Pink and White. Medium, formal double. (U.S. 1953 - Riverbank).

name: Cup Of Creme
description: Creamy White with Gold anthers and Cream filaments. Small, semidouble form. Vigorous, upright, open growth. E-L. (Seedling of Japonica 'Beatrix Hoyt'). (U.S. 2020 - Pat B. Johnson, Cairo, GA).

name: Cutie Buttons
description: White with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Small, full peony to anemone. Average, upright growth. E-M. (U.S. 2016 - John Rumbach, Jacksonville, FL).

name: Cynthia
description: White. Small, semidouble. Average, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1957 - D. W. Davis).

name: D'Iberville
description: White suffused and lined Pink. Small, formal double. (U.S. 1950 - McIlhenny).

name: Daddy Mac
description: White with flecks and streaks of Dark Red, Coral Pink and Light Red to medium Pink mixed in an irregular pattern throughout the bloom; Light Yellow anthers and Yellow filaments. Large, peony to anemone form. Slow, spreading, open growth. E-M. (U.S. 2009 - John M. Davy, Pace, FL).

name: Daijôkan (Large Castle Crown)

name: Daikagura

name: Daintrie Sievers
description: Color: Clear pink with gold anthers and white filaments. Form: Loose peony form. Size: Medium. Bloom Season: L. Originator: Keith Brushfield. Place of Origination: Australia - Kewita, Gosford, NSW. Year of Registration / Origination: 1964.

name: Dainty (California)
description: Blush White striped Red with fringed petals. Medium, semidouble. (Sport of Japonica "Tricolor (Siebold)'). (U.S. 1953 - Carter).

name: Daisy Banks
description: White. Medium, semidouble. Vigorous, spreading growth. M-L. (U.S. 1943 - Gerbing).

name: Daisy Eagleson
description: Light Pink and White having 50 petals. Miniature, rose form double. Vigorous, upright, dense growth. E-M. (Grafted Japonica seedling onto Sasanqua 'Maiden's Blush' under stock). (U.S. 1961 - Tom Eagleson, Port Arthur, TX).

name: Dakota

name: Dalu Jiuqu Variegated

name: Dan Grant

name: Dan Graves Variegated

name: Dana Homeyer Variegated

Leaf Characteristics: Dark green. Originator: Valdosta Camellia Scions. Place of Origination: U.S. - Valdosta, Georgia. Year of Registration / Origination: 1992. Heritage: Virus variegated form of Reticulata hybrid 'Dana Homeyer'.

name: Dante
description: See 'Kagiri'. (Not the same cultivar as listed in old literature which was a White with a few delicate Pink stripes).

name: Daphne T.
description: White. Medium, semidouble. Average, open, spreading growth. M. (U.S. 1997 - C. Elliott, Swainsboro, GA).

name: Darleen Stoner

name: Darling Pink

name: Darsi

name: Daughter's Red

name: David Bellamy

name: David Honour Nelson
description: White occasionally marked or striped Rose Red. Large, semidouble. Average, spreading growth. L. (U.S. 1969 - G. A. Nelson, Florence, SC).

name: David Ratcliff Variegated
description: Bright Red and White variation of 'David Ratcliff'. (U.S. 1995 - J. Ratcliff, Tifton, GA).

name: David Stuart
description: White. Large, semidouble of flat form. Vigorous, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 1955 - Feathers).

name: David Wilson
description: White. Large, peony form. Average, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 1989 - W. Wilson, Augusta, GA).

name: David Wirth Variegated
description: Wine Red blotched White variation of 'David Wirth'.

name: Daydream

name: Daydream Believer

name: Dear Jenny

name: Dearie Mealing

name: Delightful

name: Dixie Belle

name: Dixie Knight Variegated

name: Dixie Red Variegated

name: Do Pozo

name: Doiron's Legacy

name: Dolores Jackson

('For a variation of this cultivar see Japonica 'Ville de Nantes'; there are many named strains of this cultivar e.g., Japonicas 'Tea Garden', 'Middleton No. 15', 'Cantelou, 'English', 'Camellia T', 'Tallahassee', 'Mary Robertson', 'Winnie Davis' and 'Aileen'. However, it would appear that there is only one 'Donckelarii' which varies in degree of variegation). (China to Belgium [Siebold] 1834).

name: Donna Newton

description: White. Medium to large, semidouble. Average, bushy growth. M. (U.S. 1942 - Jones).

name: Enrico Bettoni
description: ('Elata'; 'Haley's Monarch'; 'Red Walker'; 'Hite Pink'; 'June'; 'Lateriatus'; 'Macey Taylor'; 'Maurice Hurst'; 'Venus'). Clear Pink. Medium to large, semidouble. Vigorous, upright growth. (Not the cultivar described in old literature, which was an anemone form of Cherry Red sometimes striped White). (Europe to U.S. [Magnolia] 1848).

name: Envying Sky's Height
description: ('Hentiangao' - Chinese Name). Light Carmine with margin of petals shaded White. Medium, semidouble to rose form double. Very slow growth. L. (Yunnan Reticulata). ('China to N.Z. 1964 - Durrant'; China to U.S. 1980 - U. C. Bot. Gdn, CA).

name: Eos
description: Color: White, lightly edged pink. Form: Semidouble with wavy petals. Size: Medium to large. Bloom Season: E-M. Growth Rate: Vigorous. Growth Characteristics: Upright spreading growth. Originator: Robert Green. Place of Origination: U.S. - Fairhope, Alabama. Year of Registration / Origination: 2005 - ACS Registration #2651 - first bloomed in 1998. Heritage: Seedling of Vernalis 'Dawn' (GinryÃ»).

name: Erin Farmer
description: White washed and shaded Orchid Pink. Large, semidouble to loose peony form with twisted, curled petals. Vigorous, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1962 - Ashby).

name: Ernest F. Beale, Sr.
description: Deep Coral Pink; White very fine edge on or around the Pink petals. Medium to large, semidouble form. Average, spreading growth. E-M. (U.S. 2003 - Melissa Beale and John B. Talley, Wilmington, NC).

name: Ernest Gill
description: White suffused with Pale Blush Pink in bud. Large, semidouble. Average, upright growth. M. (Aus. 1967 - V. A. and V. R. Lambette, East Brighton, Vic.).

name: Ernest J. Palmer
description: Pink to Red. Large, rose form double with White stamens. Slow, compact growth. E-M. (U.S. 1962 - Mrs. E. J. Palmer, Savannah, GA).

name: Ernestine Bowman
description: Salmon Pink and White. Medium, semidouble with fluted petals. Vigorous growth. E. (U.S. 1958 - Fisher).

name: Espana
description: Deep China Rose with White central petaloids. Very large, rose form double. Average, upright, compact growth. M-L. (Reticulata hybrid ‘Nuccio’s Ruby’ seedling). (U.S. 1987 - H. S. Hall, San Anselmo, CA).

name: España
description: Deep China Rose with White central petaloids. Very large, rose form double. Average, upright, compact growth. M-L. (Reticulata hybrid 'Nuccio's Ruby' seedling). (U.S. 1987 - H. S. Hall, San Anselmo, CA).

name: Essie M. Rollinson
description: White. Large, formal double with upright, pointed petals. Compact, upright growth. E-L. (U.S. 1960 - Gerbing).

name: Esther Ann
description: Light Red variegated White. Large, formal double. Average, upright growth. E-M. (U.S. 1977 - Gilley).

name: Esther Moad Variegated
description: Light Rose and White variation of 'Esther Moad.'.

name: Esther Smith
description: White and Pink. Medium, full peony form. Average growth. M. (U.S. 1989 - J. C. Smith, Weirsdale, FL).

name: Esther's Tomorrow
description: Light Pink variegated White and occasional Darker Pink with Yellow anthers and Yellow filaments. Medium to large, full peony form. Average, spreading, open growth. M. (Sport of Japonica 'Leanne's Tomorrow'). (U.S. 2013 - Esther Lawrence, Tallahassee, FL).

name: Ethel Davis Variegated
description: ('Rebecca Jones'). Clear Pink and White form of 'Ethel Davis'.

name: Ethel Rhyne
description: White edged Pink. Medium, formal double. Average, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 1987 - Marshall Rhyne, Belmont, NC).

name: Ethyl Rhyne
description: White edged Pink. Medium, formal double. Average, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 1987 - M. Rhyne, Belmont, NC).

name: Euganea
description: White. Large, peony form. Vigorous, compact, upright growth. E. (Aus. 1958 - G. Waterhouse).

name: Eugene Lize
description: ('Lady Jane Grey'; 'Annie McDonald'). Light Rose Pink marbled and splashed White. Medium, semidouble to loose peony form. Slow, compact growth. M-L. (France 1908 - Guichard).

name: Eugenia Howell
description: Deep Pink to Red splotched and flecked White. Very large, loose peony form. (Sport of Japonica 'Mathotiana Variegated'). (U.S. 1958 - V. T. Howell, Semmes, AL).

name: Eugenie De Massena
description: Light Pink veined deeper Pink edged White. (Sport of Japonica 'Don Pedro'). (England 1877 - W. Bull).

name: Eureka Variegated
description: ('Peppermint Stick'). White lined Rose. Medium, rose form double. Vigorous, bushy growth. M-L. (Japan to U.S. [Star] 1930).

name: Eva Ross
description: Ivory White streaked and splotched Cherry Red with Yellow anthers and Cream filaments. Medium, semidouble. Vigorous, spreading growth. M. Japonica 'Magnoliaeflora' × Japonica 'Ville de Nantes'). (U.S. 1999 - Frank Galloway, Bolivia, NC).

name: Evan Warriner
description: Medium Pink center with Darker Pink edged petals and White petaloids and Medium Pink rabbit ears with Yellow anthers and Yellow filaments. Large to very large, anemone form to semidouble. Vigorous, upright, spreading growth. M. (U.S. 2018 - Tom Warriner, Birmingham, AL).

name: Evelina
description: White. Large, loose peony form with large petals. Average, spreading, open growth. M-L. (U.S. 1959 - Wilson).

name: Evelyn Fulton
description: Soft Pink and White. Medium, loose peony form. Average, compact, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1959 - Fisher).

name: Evelyn Jernigan
description: White. Medium, loose peony form with upright petals. Average, compact growth. M. (U.S. 1953 - Mrs. E. Jernigan, Brewton, AL).

name: Evelyn Poe
description: White with a few Pink splashes. Medium to large, full peony form. Average, spreading, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1968 - Poe).

name: Evelyn Poe Pink Variegated
description: Color: Pink splotched white with golden anthers and pinkish-white filaments. Form: Full peony form. Size: Medium to large. Bloom Season: M. Growth Rate: Average. Growth Characteristics: Spreading, upright growth. Heritage: Virus variegated form of Japonica 'Evelyn Poe Pink'.

name: Evening Glow Pearl
description: ('Ye Ming Zu' - Chinese Name). White washed with Light Pink with Golden stamens. Large to very large, loose peony form with a mixed center of petaloids and stamens. Vigorous, upright, well-branched growth. L. (Reticulata hybrid 'Suzanne Withers' × Japonica 'Kona'). (U.S. 2012 - John Wang, Orinda, CA).

name: Extravaganza
description: White vividly and profusely marked and striped Light Red. Large to very large, anemone form. Average, compact, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1960 - Short).

name: Ezo-nishiki (Portugal)
description: Color: Pink, sprinkled with strong rose markings with yellow anther and white filaments. Form: Peony form with wavy petals. Size: Large. Bloom Season: M. Leaf Characteristics: Dark green. Place of Origination: Originated in Japan. Year of Registration / Origination: 2000. Other Information: This cultivar does not match any of those known in Japan under the name, therefore it is distinguished by the addition of (Portugal)..

name: Fair Jury
description: White with flecks and stripes of Mauve Pink. Large, full peony form. (Sport of Hybrid 'Elsie Jury'). (N.Z. 1982 - O. Blumhardt, Whangarei).

name: Fair Lass
description: Persian Rose shading to White at center. Large, semidouble. Vigorous, upright, compact growth. E-L. (Saluenensis x Reticulata 'Captain Rawes'). (N.Z. 1961 - Doak).

name: Fairest Day
description: White. Medium to large, single of flat form. Average, compact growth. M-L. (U.S. 1955 - Short).

name: Fairhope Jubilee
description: White streaked Pink. Large, peony form. Average, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1964 - R. W. Wilder, Fairhope, AL).

name: Fairweather Favorite
description: White with Pink underside. Small to medium, semidouble form. Average, upright, dense growth. E-M. Cold hardy to - 15°F. (U.S. 1998 - Ackerman).

name: Fairy Fountain
description: White. Small, single with column of White petaloids. Open, spreading growth. M. (U.S. 1959 - Wylam).

name: Fairy Garden
description: White. Miniature, single of cone shape. M. (U.S. 1962 - Short).

name: Fairy Wings
description: White. Medium, semidouble with undulating petals and stamens in broad cylinder. Upright, columnar growth. E-M. (Japonica x Reticulata 'Crimson Robe'). (U.S. 1955 - Feathers).

name: Faith
description: Rose Pink. Large, semidouble with irregular petals to anemone form with slightly variegated White petaloids. Vigorous, sturdy, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1956 - Mrs. R. H. Brodie, Biloxi, MS).

name: Faith Variegated
description: Rose Pink and White variation of 'Faith'.

name: Falling Star
description: Creamy White with an occasional brush of palest Pink on petal edges. Miniature, single form.

name: Fan Henry
description: Salmon Pink margined White. Medium to large, semidouble with irregular petals to full peony form. Vigorous, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1958 - Chiles).

name: Fancy Free
description: White at base shading out through Pale Pink to Deeper Rosy Pink. Medium, semidouble to rose form double with notched petals. Vigorous, compact growth. M. (U.S. 1960 - McCaskill).

name: Fancy Pants
description: White to Blush Pink to Lavender edge. Large, semidouble. Average, upright growth. M. (Reticulata 'Crimson Robe' X Granthamiana seedling). (U.S. 1989 - Homeyer).

name: Fandango
description: White and Pink with bright Red stripes. Large, peony form. Vigorous, bushy, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1969 - McCaskill).

name: Fannie Loughridge Variegated
description: Red blotched White variation of 'Fannie Loughridge'.

name: Fannie Louise Maphis
description: Pink with Golden anthers and White filaments. Very large, semidouble to rose form double. Vigorous, upright, spreading growth. M. (Reticulata hybrid 'Suzanne Withers' x Reticulata hybrid 'Delta Dawn'). (U.S. 1997 - Hulyn Smith).

name: Fantasia
description: Red mottled White form of 'Jarvis Red'. Small, semidouble. (U.S. 1949 - Armstrong).

name: Fantasy (California)
description: White or Shell Pink or Pink and White. Medium, semidouble. Vigorous, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1935 - McCaskill).

name: Fashion Plate
description: White edged Pink to Dark Pink. Medium, single.

name: Fashionata Variegated
description: Color: Apricot pink blotched white with yellow anthers and creamy yellow filaments. Form: semidouble with curled and crepe outer petals and occasional cluster of petaloids. Size: Large. Bloom Season: M. Growth Rate: Vigorous. Growth Characteristics: Open, upright growth. Leaf Characteristics: Dark green. Place of Origination: U.S.. Heritage: Virus variegated form of Japonica 'Fashionata'.

name: Feasti
description: ('Blushing Bride'). White dashed Pink. Medium, formal double. Vigorous, angular, upright growth. M. (Variations of this cultivar include 'Sharon Raye Pearson'; 'Elizabeth Stanton'). (U.S. 1841 - S. Feast, Baltimore, MD).

name: Feathered Edge
description: White to Blush gradually darkening toward the edge and having feathered streaks radiating toward the center. Medium, semidouble to loose peony. Compact, rounded growth. (U.S. 2005 - Bobby Green, Green Nurseries, Fairhope, AL).

name: Feathers Fragrant
description: White blushed with Pink on outer petals with Gold anthers and White filaments. Miniature, single form. Average, spreading, medium density growth. E-M. Cold Hardy. Fragrant. (C. cuspidata x C. fraterna). (U.S. 2020 - Feathers).

name: Feathery Touch
description: White with faint Blush at center and on back of petals and highly ruffled petals. Medium, semidouble. (Sport of Japonica 'Frizzle White'). (U.S. 1971 - C. R. Butler, Mobile, AL).

name: Federation
description: Deep Pink at edge of petals, shading to White at base of petals. Large, loose peony form. Average, bushy, upright growth. M. (Aus. 2003 - Camellias Victoria Inc., Mount Waverley, Vic.).

name: Fenyu (Shanghai)
description: White center with Pink edge with Yellow anthers and Light Yellow filaments. Small to medium, semidouble. Average, dense growth. E. (Sasanqua 'Shishi-Gashira' × Sasanqua 'Shinonome'). (China 2009 - Jian Guo Fei, Yong-hong Hu and Ya-li Zhang, Shanghai Botanical Garden of Shanghai, China).

name: Ferol Ilene
description: White. Medium, formal double. Average, compact, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1971 - Tammia).

name: Ferris Wheel
description: White with multiple Red and Pink streaks in various lengths throughout the flower with Yellow anthers and White filaments; at times there may be Pink shading on the petals. Large to very large, semidouble to loose peony with an occasional anemone flower. Average, upright growth. E-M. (U.S. 2016 - Nuccio's).

name: Fiesta Grande Variegated
description: Light Lavender Pink and White variation of 'Fiesta Grande'. (U.S. 1990 - Piet and Gaeta).

name: Fiesta Variegated
description: Light Coral Red blotched White variation of 'Fiesta'.

name: Fifth Avenue
description: White. Medium to large, full peony form. Average, compact growth. M. (U.S. 1967 - Shackelford).

name: Fimbriata
description: ('Alba Fimbriata'; 'Fimbriata Alba'). White fringed petals. Medium, formal double. (Sport of Japonica 'Alba Plena'). (China to England [Chandler] 1816).

name: Fine Pure
description: ('Miaojie' - Chinese Name). Light Pink petals with Darker Pink edges, sometimes frosted White with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Very large, semidouble; petals in about three whorls, the inner incurved, the outer flat with emarginated apex. Vigorous, upright growth. M-L. (Reticulata hybrid 'Suzanne Withers' x Japonica 'Kona'). (U.S. 2007 - John Wang, Orinda, CA).

name: Finlandia
description: ('Dearest'; 'Nellie White'). White. Medium, semidouble with swirled and fluted petals. Average, compact growth. E-M. (For other forms of this cultivar see below and 'King Lear', 'Monte Carlo'; 'Sunset Oaks'). (Japan to U.S. [Covina, CA]. 1910).

name: Finlandia Variegated
description: ('Margaret Jack'; 'Aurora Borealis'; 'Speckles'). Variegated variation of 'Finlandia' - White streaked Crimson. (Japan to U.S. - CA, 1910).

name: Fiona Capp
description: White. Large, single. Average, dense, spreading growth. E-M. (Aus. 1971 - R. Wilkins, Rosanna, Vic.).

name: Fircone Variegated
description: Blood Red and White variation of 'Fircone'.

name: Fire Chief Variegated
description: Deep Red moired White variation of 'Fire Chief

name: Fire Dance Variegated
description: Deep Red and White variation of 'Firedance'. (U.S. 1993 - Nuccio's).

name: Fire Frost
description: Center Soft Frosted Pink with White overtones blending to Dark to Medium Pink edges with Yellow anthers and White filaments. 15 ming left in chanter Small, loose peony form. Vigorous, upright, dense growth. M-L. (U.S. 2019 - Jill & Glenn Read, Lucedale, MS).

name: Firebird Variegated
description: Satin Red blotched White variation of 'Firebird'.

name: Firebrand Variegated
description: Scarlet blotched White form of 'Firebrand'.

name: First Lady
description: White. Large, semidouble with heavy textured nens and some petaloids. Vigorous, upright growth. M (U.S. 1964 - J. L. Sparkman, Jacksonville, FL).

name: First Prom
description: White with Blush undertone. Medium, formal double. Vigorous, compact, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 1963 - Marshall).

name: Firstborn Variegated
description: Color: Bright red blotched white with yellow anthers and white filaments. Form: Semidouble with petals standing apart. Size: Medium. Bloom Season: M. Growth Characteristics: Open, upright growth. Leaf Characteristics: Dark green. Place of Origination: U.S.. Year of Registration / Origination: 1963. Heritage: Virus variegated form of Japonica 'Firstborn'.

name: Flame Variegated
description: Deep Flame Red spotted White variation of 'Flame'.

name: Flaming Star
description: Bright flame Pink with Yellow stamens and White filaments. Large, single with wavy petals of irregular length that are twisted and fluted. Slow, sturdy, compact growth attaining pyramidal shape. (U.S. 1950 - McCaskill Gardens, East Pasadena, CA).

name: Flashdance
description: White with Red and Pink stripes. Large, anemone form to peony form. Average, open, upright growth. E-M. (Aus. 1989 - D. Coe, Glenroy, Albury, NSW).

name: Flip
description: White or Pink spotted Red. Medium, formal double. Average, upright growth. L. (U.S. 1980 - Habel).

name: Flo-Adrian
description: White. Medium, formal double. Compact growth. M-L. (U.S. 1976 - C. V. Bozeman, Hattiesburg, MS).

name: Flora Ellen
description: White. Large, semidouble. Vigorous, open, pendulous growth. E-M. (U.S. 1952 - A. J. Parsons, Norfolk, VA).

name: Flora Eyster
description: White. Large, anemone form. Average, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1986 - Tammia).

name: Floradora Girl
description: Light Blush Pink. Large, semidouble with wavy petals and trumpet of White stamens in center. Vigorous, open, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1961 - J. M. Hull, Mobile, AL).

name: Florence Coker Rogers
description: Pure White. Medium, formal double. E-M. (U.S. 1930's - Kalmia Gardens, Hartsville, SC).

name: Florence Hollis
description: China White with White filaments. Medium, semidouble. Vigorous, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 1949 - J. L. Hand, Pelman, GA).

name: Florence Jane
description: Blush Pink to White. Large, peony form. Vigorous, spreading growth. M-L. (Aus. 1973 - F. S. Spencer, Cheltenham, Vic.).

name: Florence Stratton
description: White with some petals solid Pink. Medium to large, formal double to rose form double with cupped inner petals. Vigorous, bushy growth. L. (For another form of this cultivar see 'Sieur De Bienville'). (U.S. 1941 - Mcllhenny).

name: Florence's Debutante
description: Rose Pink bordered with a Darker Rose Pink with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Medium, semidouble to full peony form. Vigorous, spreading growth. E-L. (Japonica 'Debutante' × Japonica 'Mathotiana Supreme'). (U.S. 2013 - Florence Crowder, Denham Springs, LA).

name: Florita
description: White with Pink on outer petals. Medium, anemone form.

name: Flowerwood Variegated
description: Crimson spotted White variation of 'Flowerwood'.

name: Floyd Magee
description: White. Large, peony form to anemone form. Average, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1977 - R. S. Magee, Bogalusa, LA).

name: Fluff
description: Cream White blended with soft Pink. Medium, full peony form. Vigorous, compact, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1954 - Short).

name: Fluted Silk
description: Pink with center petaloids tinged White. Very large, semidouble with irregular, wavy petals. Vigorous, upright growth. M. (Reticulata 'Shot Silk' seedling). (U.S. 1988 - Hall).

name: Fluted White
description: See 'Setsugekka'.

name: Fly Fly
description: ('Pian Pian' - Chinese Name). White center with outer petals Pink with White Picotee edges and sometimes petal veining can be observed. Large to very large, semidouble. Very bushy growth. M- L. (Reticulata hybrid 'Suzanne Withers' × Japonica 'Kona'). (U.S. 2012 - John Wang, Orinda, CA).

name: Forrest's Royal Pearl
description: Blush Pink with Dark Pink freckles and flecks with Yellow anthers and Creamy White filaments. Large, semidouble form. Vigorous, upright, open growth. M-L. (Seedling of Japonica 'Beatrix Hoyt'). (U.S. 2023 - Pat B. Johnson, Cairo, GA).

name: Forty-Niner Variegated
description: Color: Brilliant red blotched white with golden anthers. Form: Full peony form. Size: Large. Bloom Season: E-M. Growth Rate: Vigorous. Growth Characteristics: Spreading growth. Place of Origination: U.S.. Year of Registration / Origination: 1969. Heritage: Virus variegated form of Reticulata hybrid - 'Forty-Niner'.

name: Fragrant Fairies
description: Pale Blush Pink tinged Blush Mauve with an occasional stripe. Miniature, single with crepe and notched petals. Vigorous, upright growth. E-L. Fragrant. (Hybrid 'Snow Drop' seedling). (Aus. 1994 - M. Baker, Macleod, Vic.).

name: Fragrant Frill
description: White, Blush or Pink. Large, anemone form. Compact growth. M. Fragrant. (U.S. 1960 - E. Luce, Half Moon Bay, CA).

name: Fragrant Jonquil
description: White. Medium, anemone form resembling a jonquil. Vigorous, open growth. M. Fragrant. (U.S. 1953 - Short).

name: Fragrant Lady
description: Ivory White with Pink flush on outer petals. Large, semidouble. M-L. Fragrant. (Japonica 'Mrs. Bertha A. Harms' × Hybrid 'Salab' × Japonica 'Erin Farmer'). (N.Z. 1996 - J. Finlay).

name: Fragrant One
description: Light Pink with Darker Pink petal tips shading to White in center. Large, single. Average, open, spreading growth. E-L. Fragrant. (Japonica 'Mrs. Bertha A. Harms' × Hybrid 'Salab'] x Japonica Tamaikari'). (N.Z. 1998 - J. Finlay).

name: Fragrant Ripple
description: White to very Light Pink, marked with Light Pink stripes and Darker Pink stripes. Medium, anemone form. Average, upright growth. M. Very light gardenia fragrance. (Japonica 'Mrs. Bertha A. Harms' × Hybrid 'Salab') x Japonica "Tama-Ikari'] x Japonica 'Kramer's Supreme'. (N.Z. 1999 - J. Finlay).

name: Fragrant Star
description: White. Large, semidouble with long, narrow, upright center petals. Average, upright growth. M. Fragrant. (U.S. 1956 - B D. Colombo, Martinez, CA).

name: Fragrant Sweet
description: White. Medium, single. Fragrant.

name: Fran Mathis Variegated
description: Champagne Pink and White variation of 'Fran Mathis'. (U.S. 1965 - Dr. W. F. Mathis, Moultrie, GA).

name: Frances
description: White. Small, semidouble. Compact, pendulous growth. M. (U.S. 1950 - Miss H. Brandon, Thomasville, GA).

name: Frances Black
description: Pink to Pink and White. Large, rose form double. Average, spreading growth. E. (U.S. 1980 - Tammia).

name: Frances Council
description: White. Miniature to small, formal double. Average, upright growth. E-M. (U.S. 1971 - Tammia).

name: Frances Garoni Supreme
description: Soft Rose Pink blotched White with Golden anthers and Creamy Yellow filaments. Medium to large, loose peony form with twisted, fluted petals. Average, slightly pendulous growth. M-L. (Virus variegated form of Japonica 'Frances Garoni'). (U.S. 1963).

name: Frances Lunsford
description: White. Large, semidouble. Vigorous, spreading, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 1989 - Jernigan).

name: Frances Sessions Hicks
description: White. Large, semidouble of fairly flat form with prominent stamens to full peony form. Average growth. M. (U.S. 1962 - R. D. Hicks, Troutville, VA).

name: Franceska Lawson
description: Blush White. Very large, rose form double. Average, open, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 1968 - Camelliana).

name: Francie L. Surprise
description: Rose Pink variegated White. Large to very large, peony form. (Sport of Reticulata hybrid 'Francie L'). (U.S. 1980 - C. X. Copeland, Jackson, MS).

name: Francie L. Variegated
description: Rose Pink and blotched White form of 'Francie L.. (U.S. 1970).

name: Francie L. White
description: White. Large to very large, semidouble. M- L. (Sport of Reticulata hybrid 'Francie L'.). (U.S. 1998 - Dr. Dan Nathan, Fort Valley, GA).

name: Francis Eugene Phillips
description: Soft Pink, bordered White. Large to very large, peony form. Vigorous, upright growth; very unusual holly- like foliage with heavy serrations. M-L. (U.S. - 1999, Gene Phillips, Gene's Nursery, Savannah, GA).

name: Francis Hanger
description: White. Medium, single with blooms up and down stems. Upright growth. (Saluenensis x Japonica). (England 1953 - Hanger).

name: Frank Baker
description: White. Medium, semidouble with irregular petals. Average, open growth. E-M. (U.S. 1950 - F. F. Baker, Jr., Macon, GA).

name: Frank Bisbee
description: White. Large, peony form to formal double. Average growth. E-M. (U.S. 1961 - F. Bisbee, Jacksonville, FL).

name: Frank Eidson
description: White splashed Pink and Red. Large, peony form. E. (For other forms of this cultivar see 'Blush Eidson', 'Rose Eidson'). (U.S. 1953 - F. V. Eidson, Thomasville, GA).

name: Frank Gibson
description: White with mass of White petaloids edged Yellow. Medium, anemone form. Vigorous, open, upright growth. E-M. (U.S. 1951 - Mrs. F. L. Gibson, Thomasville, GA).

name: Frank Houser Variegated
description: Red and White form of 'Frank Houser'. (U.S. 1990 - Jernigan).

name: Frank Persons
description: White. Medium, peony form.

name: Frankie Winn Variegated
description: Pastel Pink, blotched White variation of 'Frankie Winn'. (U.S. - 2000, W. C. Sutton, Savannah, GA).

name: Frau Geheimrat Oldevig
description: ('Mme. Chiang Kai-Shek'; 'Thomas Plant'). Deep Pink, sometimes mottled White. Medium, semidouble. Slow, compact growth. M. (For a variation of this cultivar see 'Frances M. Solomon'). (Germany 1911 - Seidel).

name: Freckles
description: White with Pink dots and flecks. Miniature, formal double. Average, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1997 - Feathers).

name: Fred Gerald
description: Red and White. Large, loose peony form with serrated petals. Slow, compact, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 1968 - K. G. Durio, Jr., Opelousas, LA).

name: Fred Sander Variegated
description: ('Fimbriata Superba Variegated'). Crimson and White variation of 'Fred Sander'.

name: Freda Howell Variegated
description: Deep Pink variegated White with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Medium, semidouble. Average, upright, spreading growth. M-L. (U.S. 2016 - Howell).

name: Freedom Bell Variegated
description: Color: Bright red blotched white with yellow anthers and pinkish filaments. Form: Bell-shaped semidouble. Size: Small. Bloom Season: E-M. Growth Rate: Vigorous. Growth Characteristics: Compact, upright growth. Leaf Characteristics: Dark green. Originator: Ray Gentry. Place of Origination: U.S. - Jackson, Mississippi. Year of Registration / Origination: 1968.

name: French Vanilla
description: Creamy White. Large, single.

name: Freyda
description: Light Rose Pink with some petaloids showing White. Medium, rose form double with some outside notched petals.

name: Friendship
description: White. Large, tiered semidouble. Vigorous growth. M-L. (U.S. 1960 - Feathers).

name: Frilled White
description: White. Medium, semidouble with frilled petals.

name: Frilly One
description: Creamy White with Gold anthers and Yellow filaments. Medium, full peony to loose peony form. Average, upright, open growth. M-L. (Seedling of Non-reticulata hybrid 'Jury's Yellow'). (U.S. 2019 - Pat Johnson, Cairo, GA).

name: Fritz Taylor
description: White graduating to Deep Pink on outer petals. Medium, formal double with small, cupped petals. Slow, upright growth. L. (U.S. 1955 - Mrs. A. M. Nickerson, Norfolk, VA).

name: Frizzle White
description: ('Susan Carter'). White. Large, semidouble with wavy, crinkled petals. Vigorous, spreading growth. M. (A form sport of this cultivar is 'Feathery Touch'). (U.S. 1935 - Overlook).

name: Frolic
description: White with touch of Pink. Miniature, single. Slow, spreading growth. E-L. (Rosaeflora seedling). (N.Z. 1990 - P. Austin).

name: Frost Queen
description: White. Large, semidouble. Average, upright growth. E-L. Cold hardy to -5°F. (U.S. 1971 - U.S. Dept. of Agr., Glenn Dale, MD).

name: Frosted Star
description: Color: White toned light pink with golden anthers and white filaments. Form: Semidouble. Size: Small. Bloom Season: E. Growth Rate: Average. Growth Characteristics: Bushy, upright, somewhat spreading growth. Leaf Characteristics: Dark green. Originator: Nuccio's Nurseries. Place of Origination: U.S. - Altadena, California. Year of Registration / Origination: 1993.

name: Frosty
description: White. Medium, semidouble with irregular petals.

name: Frosty Morn
description: White. Large, anemone form. Average, open growth. M-L. (U.S. 1950 - Short).

name: Fuji
description: White changing to Soft Pink. Large, single with flared Pale Yellow stamens. See Higo.

name: Fuji-No-Mine
description: White. Medium, rose form double.

name: Fuji-No-Yuki
description: (Snow on Mt. Fujiyama). White. Medium, single. See 'Higo'.

name: Fujimusume
description: Large semidouble, white with occasional clear crimson streaks; 15–17 round petals in rows with central stamens; blooms mid-season.

name: Fukurin Wabisuke
description: Light Pink bordered White. Small, single. Medium, upright growth. E-M.

name: Fukurin-Ikkyu
description: White or Pale Pink, shaded Deeper Pink with a White border, or Red in various degrees up to Solid Red with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Miniature, semidouble with foliage bordered Light Green. L. (Sport of Rusticana 'Komomiji'). (Japan - Kantô area to U.S. 1930 - Star Nursery, Sierra Madre, CA).

name: Fukutsuzumi
description: Color: Dark red petals mottled white with yellow anthers and red filaments. Form: Single. Size: Small. Bloom Season: M. Growth Rate: Average. Growth Characteristics: Somewhat open growth. Leaf Characteristics: dark green, elliptic. Originator: Minoru Sat??. Place of Origination: Japan - Chûbu area. Year of Registration / Origination: 1969. Heritage: Variegated form of Japonica  'Kuro-wabisuke'. Other Information: Means 'Fortune's Hand Drum' in Japanese.

name: Fukutsuzumi (Fortune's Hand Drum)
description: Dark Red petals mottled White with Yellow anthers and Red filaments. Small, single. Medium, somewhat open growth. M. (A variegated form of Japonica 'Kuro- wabisuke'). (Japan 1971-Andoh).

name: Fukuzutsumi
description: White shaded Rose Pink. Medium, single to semidouble.

name: Full House
description: White occasionally streaked or splotched Pink. Large, rose form double, often showing tuft of small petaloids in center. Willowy growth. M. (U.S. 1960 - Ashby).

name: G. W. Ellis
description: Rose Pink flecked White. Large, formal double to rose form double. Average, compact growth. M-L. (Similar to 'Rosea Superba'"). (U.S. 1945 - Huested).

name: Gabriel Maphis
description: Bright Pink with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Large to very large, loose peony form. Vigorous, upright, open growth. E-M. (Seedling of Reticulata Hybrid 'Curtain Call'). (U.S. 2020 - Pat B. Johnson, Cairo, GA).

name: Gabriel's Blush
description: Blushing White. Large, formal double to rose form double. Average, upright, open growth. E-L. (Seedling of Japonica 'Snow Lady'). (U.S. 2016 - Pat Johnson, Cairo, GA).

name: Gaiety
description: See 'Gigantea'. (Not the cultivar listed in old literature, which was an anemone form of White striped Pink).

name: Galaxie
description: White striped Reddish Pink. Medium, semidouble sometimes with cupped o r twisted upright petals. Average, upright growth. M-L. (Saluenensis x Japonica 'Finlandia Variegated'). (U.S. 1963 - Metcalf).

name: Galilee Variegated
description: Pink and White form of 'Galilee'.

name: Gallic Moods
description: Varicolored changing from White to Pink to Red. Large, semidouble. Vigorous, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1973 - Witman).

name: Gardenia (Bradford)
description: White. Miniature, full peony form. Compact, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1935 - Bradford).

name: Garnett Avant
description: White. Medium, formal double. Average, spreading, dense growth. E-M. (U.S. 2017 - Randolph Maphis, Tallahassee FL).

name: Gary's Red Variegated
description: Turkey Red and White variation of 'Gary's Red'. (U.S. 1970).

name: Gate Of Heaven
description: Pink with White radiating from center. Medium, semidouble. M. (U.S. 1952 - Baker).

name: Gay
description: White. Medium, single.

name: Gay Chieftain
description: Vivid Red and White. Large, semidouble with upright petals. Vigorous, open growth. M-L. (U.S. 1960 - Short).

name: Gay Magnolia
description: White with Pinkish Lavender throat; sports solid Pink. Small, semidouble. Bushy growth. M. (Aus. 1968 - Hawthorne, Vic.).

name: Gay Sue
description: White with Cream anthers. Medium, semidouble with frilled petals.

name: Gay Time
description: White washed and shaded Orchid Pink. Medium, semidouble to formal double. Average, upright growth. M. (Saluenensis x Japonica 'Mathotiana'). (N.Z. 1970 - Jury).

name: Gayle Lawrence
description: Red with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Large to very large, loose peony form. Average, upright, open growth. M-L. (Seedling of Reticulata Hybrid 'Frank Houser Variegated"). (U.S. 2020 - Pat B. Johnson, Cairo, GA).

name: Gayle's Mona
description: White with Pink undertones looking like a watermark with Darker Pink veins with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Large, semidouble to loose peony form. Slow, upright, spreading growth. L. (Sport of Non-reticulata hybrid 'Mona Jury'). (U.S. 2019 - Steve & Gayle Lawrence, Tallahassee, FL).

name: Gena Owens Fredrickson
description: Light Pink in center to Dark Pink at edges with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Medium, semidouble to loose peony form. Average, upright growth. M. (U.S. 2012 - Edward W. Fredrickson, Wilmington, NC).

name: General Douglas MacArthur
description: Color: Rose pink veined red with red petaloids grading to white at edges. Form: Semidouble of cupped form. Size: Large. Originator: E. A. McIlhenny - Jungle gardens. Place of Origination: U.S. - Avery island, Louisiana. Year of Registration / Origination: 1950.

name: General Douglas MacARTHUR Ii
description: Rose Pink veined Red with Red petaloids grading to White at edges. Large, semidouble of cupped form. (U.S. 1950 - Mcilhenny).

name: General George Patton Variegated
description: Bright Pink blotched White variation of 'General George Patton'.

name: General Leclerc Variegated
description: Dark Red and White variation of 'General Leclerc'. • form. Vigorous, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1959).

name: General Washington
description: White with some petals lined Pink. Medium, rose form double. Average, upright, spreading growth. M. (U.S. 1844 - J. B. Smith, Philadelphia, PA).

name: Geoff Hamilton
description: Pale Pink edged White. Medium, formal double. (Sport of Japonica 'Yirgella'). (Aus. 1981 - Dr. R M Withers, Hawthorn, Vic.).

name: George B. Barrett
description: White. Medium, semidouble with large, fluted outer petals to full peony form. Average, compact growth. E-M. (Plant Patent No. 866). (U.S. 1948 - Mrs. G. Barrett, Augusta, GA).

name: George Counts
description: White. Large, semidouble. Vigorous, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1969 - Roadside Nsy., Savannah, GA).

name: George Lumsden
description: Light Pink with Whiter center with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Small, loose peony to rose form double. Average, upright, dense growth. M-L. (U.S. 2016 - Hulyn Smith).

name: George O. Anderson
description: Pink. Large, rose form double to semidouble with petaloids streaked Pinkish White. Average, open growth. M-L. (U.S. 1959 - Dr. J. B. Anderson, Edgewater Park, MS).

name: George Priest
description: Red and White. Large, semidouble. Vigorous, spreading, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1984 - Alfter).

name: George Stewart Variegated
description: Color: Red with deeper red veining blotched white with yellow anthers and light yellow filaments. Form: Semidouble. Size: Large. Bloom Season: M. Growth Rate: Vigorous. Growth Characteristics: Compact, upright growth. Place of Origination: U.S.. Year of Registration / Origination: 1982. Heritage: Virus variegated form of Japonica 'George Stewart'.

name: Georgia Belle Variegated
description: Rose Pink blotched White variation of 'Georgia Belle'.

name: Georgia National Fair
description: Red with a large amount of White. Large to very large, semidouble to tight peony form. Vigorous, dense, upright growth. E-L. (U.S. 1995 - Rupert E. Drews, Charleston, SC).

name: Georgia National Fair Blush
description: Blush Pink with a large amount of White with Yellow anthers and Yellow filaments. Large to very large, semidouble to full peony form. Vigorous, upright, dense growth. E-L. (Sport of Japonica 'Georgia National Fair'). (U.S. 2009 - Hulyn Smith).

name: Georgie Van De Kamp
description: White occasionally striped Pink. Medium to large, semidouble to anemone form. Compact, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 1983 - Gentry).

name: Ghentry J.
description: White and Blush White. Large, rose form double with twisted and curled petals. Average growth. E-M. (U.S. 1989 - O. L. Jacobson, Jacksonville Beach, FL).

name: Gibson Girl
description: White striped Red. Medium, semidouble. Vigorous, compact, upright growth. M. (For a variation of this cultivar see 'John Clairmont'). (U.S. 1947 - Descanso).

name: Gigantea
description: ('Emperor Wilhelm'; 'Emperor Frederick Wilhelm'; 'Magnolia King'; 'Mary Bell Glennan'; 'Fanny Davenport', 'Kilvintoniana'; 'Monstruoso Rubra'; 'Jolly Roger'; 'Gaiety'). Red marbled White. Large to very large, semidouble to anemone form to peony form. Vigorous, open growth. M. (For a variation of this cultivar see 'Jacksoni'). (Europe to U.S. [Magnolia] 1840's).

name: Gigantea Alba
description: White. Large, single. Vigorous, upright growth. M. (Japan to U.S. [McIlhenny] 1942).

name: Gilbert Fisher
description: Light Rose Red, sometimes spotted White. Large, semidouble with large petaloids. Vigorous, sturdy, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1950 - Malbis).

name: Gillette's Fluffy White
description: See 'Palmer Gillette'.

name: Gilley's Forever
description: Dark Pink with Golden anthers and White filaments. Large, semidouble. Average, upright, spreading growth. M-L. (Seedling of Reticulata hybrid 'Valley Knudsen'). (U.S. 2016 - Gilley).

name: Gin Yô-Tsubaki (Silver Leaf Camellia)
description: Peach Red with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Small, single. E-M. (Japan 1979 - Shu Ôta, Izu Ohshima).

name: Gin'Yo-Tsubaki (Silver Leaf Camellia)
description: Peach Red with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Small, single. E-M. (Japan 1979 - Shu Ota, Izu Ohshima).

name: Gin-No-Zai (Silver Baton)
description: White edged Lavender with splayed Yellow anthers and Yellow filaments. Medium, single. E. (Japan 1789 - Kansai District). • GIN-RYO - Formerly Vernalis - See 'Gin-ryu'. GIN-RYU - Formerly Vernalis - ('Dawn'; 'Gin-ryo'). White, sometimes diffused Pink. Unknown size, semidouble. GLAD TIDINGS - Formerly Vernalis - Deep Rose Pink to Red with Golden anthers and Yellow filaments. Small, semidouble. Rapid, erect, dense growth. E-L. (Seedling of Sasanqua 'Yuletide'). (U.S. 1985 - Houghton S. Hall, San Anselmo, CA).

name: Ginette Walters
description: White splashed Pink. Large, peony form to rose form double. Vigorous, compact, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1984 - Tammia).

name: Ginga
description: White, slightly striped Pink. Medium, single. See Higo.

name: Ginger
description: Ivory White. Miniature, full peony form. Average, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 1958 - Hartman).

name: Gingetsu (Silver Moon)
description: Color: White with petal tips tinged with red with golden anthers and yellowish-white filaments. Form: Single of 6-7 petals; creped and frilled. Size: Large. Bloom Season: E. Growth Rate: Vigorous. Growth Characteristics: Upright, dense growth. Leaf Characteristics: Yellowish-green. Place of Origination: Japan - Kumamoto Prefecture. Year of Registration / Origination: 1967. Other Information: Means 'Silver Moon' in English; authorized by the Higo Sasanqua Society in 1970s.

name: Gladys Darling
description: Rose Red spotted White. Large, semidouble with upright center petals. Average upright growth. E-M. (U.S. 1972 - J. H. Morton, Waycross, GA).

name: Gladys Menard
description: Pink. Large, semidouble with some upright petals and White stamens. Average, spreading growth. M. (U.S. 1962 - Judice).

name: Gladys Wilmot
description: White flushed Pink, light at center deepening to margins. Small, semidouble. Average, spreading, upright growth. E-M. (U.S. 1962 - R. J. Wilmot, Gainesville, FL).

name: Glen 40 Variegated
description: ('Thunderbolt'). Deep Red Blotched White variation of 'Glen 40'.

name: Glen Blackwell Johnson
description: Pink and White with Red stripes varying in width. Large, semidouble with ruffled, upright petals. Average, open, upright growth. E-M. (U.S. 1978 - Mrs. H. Johnson, Madison, FL).

name: Glenn Allan Variegated
description: Deep Rose Red spotted White form of 'Glenn Allan'.

name: Glenn Hanchey
description: White. Medium, formal double. Average, open growth. E-L. (U.S. 1989 - Stone).

name: Glenn-Ella
description: Coral Pink petals shading to White at base. Medium to large, loose peony form. Average, upright growth. E-L. (N.Z. 1990 - A. Gamlin, Manaia).

name: Glenwood
description: Red heavily variegated White in center. Medium, full peony form. Vigorous, upright growth. E-M. (U.S. 1965 - J. U. Smith, Columbia, SC).

name: Gloria del Verbano
description: Color: Vivid red and lined with white. Form: Formal double. Size: Medium. Bloom Season: E-L. Leaf Characteristics: Dark green. Place of Origination: Italy. Year of Registration / Origination: 1845.

name: Gloria Stuart
description: White. Large, semidouble with fluted petals. Vigorous, upright growth. E. (U.S. 1961 - A. P. Barry and O. G. Thomas, Macon, GA).

name: Glory B
description: Bright Pink with Gold anthers and White filaments. Medium, semidouble. Average, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 2017 - Gordy).

name: Goggy
description: Light Pink to Shell Pink, almost White with translucent petals. Medium, formal double. Vigorous, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1979 - R. Bond, Dallas, TX).

name: Gold Tone
description: White. Large, formal double to semidouble to anemone form with many bright Yellow stamens and petaloids having a distinct Yellow cast. Average, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1961 - Wilson).

name: Golden Dome
description: White with heavy Golden stamens. Very large, semidouble. Average, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 1981 - Mrs. M. O'Malley, Woodside, CA').

name: Golden Fleece
description: White with Golden anthers and Light Yellow filaments. Large, loose peony form. Average, compact growth. M-L. Japonica 'Frank Gibson' × C.x williamsii 'Feathers Red Peony'). (U.S. 1970 - David L. Feathers, Lafayette, CA).

name: Golden Gate
description: White outer guard petals and Golden-Yellow center petaloids. Large, anemone form. Vigorous, open, upright growth. M- L. (U.S. 1976 - H. Hall).

name: Golden Glimmer
description: White with the faintest Pink Blush and light shading of Yellow at base of petals. Very large, semidouble form. Average, upright, spreading growth. M. (Reticulata hybrid 'Wild Form' × Reticulata hybrid 'Suzanne Withers' x Nitidissima). (Aus. 2003 - Mr. Geoffrey and Mrs. Colleen Sherrington, Balwyn North, Vic.)

name: Golden Glow
description: Creamy White deepening to Light Yellow at base with edge and back of petals slightly toned Pink. Medium, semidouble. Average, dense, upright growth. L. (Japonica 'Guilio Nuccio' × Pitardii] * Chrysantha). (U.S. 1994 - Nuccio's).

name: Golden Phoenix
description: White with Dark Pink edges. Unknown size, single. (U.S. - Camellia Forest Nursery, Chapel Hill, NC).

name: Goldwater Variegated
description: Red blotched White variation of 'Goldwater'.

name: Gone Again
description: White with few Pink dashes and stripes. Medium, formal double. Bushy, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 1974 - Haynie).

name: Gone Again Silver
description: Light Pink bordered White. (Sport of Japonica 'Gone Again'). (U.S. 1978 - Haynie).

name: Goshozakura
description: Color: Light pink with white base with flared gold anthers and light yellow filaments. Form: Single with petals curled when fully open. Size: Medium. Bloom Season: E-M. Place of Origination: Japan - Kumamoto Prefecture. Year of Registration / Origination: Early 1900's. Other Information: Means 'Cherry Blossom in the Royal Garden' in English.

name: Gossamer Wings
description: Light Pink. Small, single. GRADY's EGAO - Formerly Vernalis - Light Pink, veined, with fine White edge.

name: Governor Earl Warren Variegated
description: Rose Pink and White form of 'Governor Earl Warren'.

name: Governor Hugh White
description: Magenta Red. Medium, semidouble to loose peony form. Vigorous, open, upright growth. E. (U.S. 1954 - Clower).

name: Governor Mouton
description: ('Aunt Jetty'; 'Angelica'). Oriental Red, sometimes splotched White. Medium, semidouble to loose peony form. Vigorous, upright growth. M. Cold hardy. (U.S. Early 1900's - Lafayette, LA [Lost label]).

name: Governor William Bradford
description: Deep Orange Red blotched White variation of 'Prince of Orange'. Large, loose to full peony form to anemone form. (U.S. 1950 - Bradford).

name: Grace Albritton
