description: Light Pink deeper at edge to White with Pink center and border. Miniature to small, formal double. Average, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1970 - A. D. Albritton, Tallahassee, FL).

name: Grace Albritton Flair
description: White with a few flakes of Pink. (Sport of Japonica 'Grace Albritton'). (U.S. 1980 - Gentry).

name: Grace Albritton White
description: White. (Sport of Japonica 'Grace Albritton'). (U.S. 1980 - Gentry).

name: Grace Bunton
description: Deep Rose Pink edging to Cream White at base with underside slightly deeper. Medium, semidouble. Vigorous, compact, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1950 - W. J. Robinson and C. J. Hayes, Norfolk, VA).

name: Grace Caple
description: Faint Blush Pink fading to White. Large, semidouble to peony form. Slow, compact growth. E-L. (Pitardii x Japonica). (N.Z. 1974 - Durrant).

name: Grace Gordon
description: White with occasional Pink stripe. Small, formal double. Vigorous, upright growth. M-L. (China to U.S. [Piet] 1978).

name: Grace Marie
description: White heavily dashed and flecked Light Red. Small, formal double with round petals. Average, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1960 - Julington).

name: Grace's Sweet Pea
description: White with Pink under petals. Miniature to small, formal double. (Sport of Japonica 'Grace Albritton'). (U.S. 1982 - Gentry).

name: Grady's Egao
description: Color: Light pink, veined, with fine white edge with golden anthers and light yellow filaments. Form: Semidouble. Size: Medium. Bloom Season: M. Leaf Characteristics: Mid-green. Originator: Grady Perigan. Place of Origination: U.S. - San Marino, California. Year of Registration / Origination: 1995. Heritage: Sport of Vernalis 'Egao'. Other Information: Flowers and foliage smaller than Egao, more compact with a heavier bud set.

name: Graem Yates Variegated
description: Pink blotched White form of 'Graem Yates. (U.S. 1989).

name: Granada Variegated
description: Color: Vivid red blotched white. Form: Semidouble to full peony form. Size: Large to very large. Bloom Season: M. Growth Rate: Vigorous. Growth Characteristics: Upright growth. Originator: Ray Gentry. Place of Origination: U.S. - Jackson, Mississippi. Year of Registration / Origination: 1969. Heritage: Virus variegated form of Japonica 'Granada'.

name: Grand Finale
description: White. Large to very large, semidouble with irregular, fan-shaped petals fluted at edges and spray of stamens in center. Vigorous, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1957 - Short).

name: Grand Marshal Variegated
description: Rich Deep Red and White variation of 'Grand Marshal'. (U.S. 1993 - Nuccio's).

name: Grand Prix Variegated
description: Color: Brilliant red blotched white with yellow anthers and white filaments. Form: Semidouble with irregular petals. Size: Very large. Bloom Season: M. Growth Rate: Vigorous. Growth Characteristics: Upright growth. Place of Origination: U.S.. Heritage: Virus variegated form of Japonica 'Grand prix'.

name: Grand Slam Variegated
description: Dark Red blotched White variation of 'Grand Slam'.

name: Grandandy
description: Red with distinctive White frosting with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Very large, semidouble. Average, upright growth. L. [(Reticulata 'Crimson Robe' × Lutchuensis) × Reticulata hybrid 'Lilette Witman'). (U.S. 2013 - Hulyn Smith).

name: Grandiflora Rosea Variegated
description: ('Louise Maclay Variegated').- Deep Pink and White form of 'Grandiflora Rosea'.

name: Grandma's Garden
description: Dark Purple Pink with some variegation of central petals with Yellow anthers and Whitish filaments. Large, semidouble to loose peony form. Average, upright, open growth. M-L. (Seedling of Japonica 'Snow Lady'). (U.S. 2016 - Pat Johnson, Cairo, GA).

name: Grant Campbell Variegated
description: Medium Red and White variation of 'Grant Campbell'. (U.S. 2019 - Tommy Weeks, Conroe, TX).

name: Grantham's Fragrant
description: White. Large, semidouble with notched and fluted petals. Average, upright growth. E-M. Fragrant. (U.S. 1961 - T. T. Grantham, Gulfport, MS).

name: Gregory Conway
description: White. Medium, formal double. Average, compact, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 1968 - Mrs. W. Hathorn, Monroe, LA).

name: Greysen James Seaton
description: White with Gold anthers and Yellow filaments. Small to medium, single. Average, upright growth. E-L. (U.S. 2017 - Edward W. Fredrickson, Wilmington, NC).

name: Guest Of Honor Variegated
description: Salmon Pink blotched White variation of 'Guest of Honor'.

name: Guest Star
description: Pink shading to White. Medium, formal double. Average, compact growth. M-L. (U.S. 1974 - J. R. Moore, Hampton, VA).

name: Guilio Nuccio Variegated
description: Coral Rose Pink and White variation of 'Guilio Nuccio'. (For a variation of this cultivar see 'McVey's Guilio Nuccio').

name: Gulf Glory
description: Color: Pure white with dark yellow anthers and light yellow filaments. Form: Single, petals wedge shaped, creped and notched at the apex; stamens flare out in a sunburst fashion. Size: Very large. Bloom Season: E. Growth Characteristics: Upright but somewhat spreading. Leaf Characteristics: Dark green. Originator: K. Sawada. Place of Origination: U.S. - Mobile, Alabama. Year of Registration / Origination: 1948. Heritage: Chance seedling.

name: Gulf Park
description: White splashed Red. Large, semidouble with wide crinkled petals. Slow, open, spreading growth. E-M. (U.S. 1951 - Clower).

name: Gungah
description: White. Medium, anemone form. Slow, compact growth. E-M. (Aus. 1992 - A. and J. Wilson, Monterey, NSW).

name: Gunsmoke Variegated
description: Red blotched White variation of 'Gunsmoke'.

name: Gus Menard
description: White with Canary Yellow petaloids. Large, anemone form with center petals divided by petaloids. Average, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1962 - Judice).

name: Guy Merry
description: White. Medium, semidouble. M-L. (U.S. 1959 - Mayo's Nsy., Augusta, GA).

name: Gwenneth Morey
description: White outer petals and Deep Cream to Pale Primrose Yellow petaloids. Medium, anemone form. Average, upright growth. M-L. See also 'Brushfield's Yellow' which is similar but not identical. (Aus. 1965 - Dr. B. R. Morey, Carlingford).

name: Gwynne Walker
description: Red. Large, single with five large petals and White stamens. Slow, compact growth. E-M. (U.S. 1961 - Mrs. M. H. Walker, Lafayette, LA).

name: Gypsy Lights
description: White with Pink Blush. Small, single. Strong, upright, well-branched growth; flower sheds by itself. M. {Pitardii var. 'Yunnanica' × [Reticulata 'Crimson Robe' x (Reticulata 'Crimson Robe' x Fraterna)]}. (U.S. 1999 - Charvet).

name: Gypsy Variegated
description: Dark Red and White form of 'Gypsy.

name: H. A. Downing Variegated
description: ('Mardi Gras'). Rose Red marbled White form of 'H. A. Downing'.

name: Hagler
description: Deep Red and White. Large, anemone form of variable form. (U.S. 1961 - Mrs. Hagler, Augusta, GA).

name: Haku-Rakuten
description: ('Refugee'). White. Medium to large, semidouble to loose peony form with curved and fluted petals. Vigorous, upright growth. M. (Name of Famous Poet in Old China). (Japan to U.S. [Domoto] 1929).

name: Haku-Taka
description: White. Large, single with flared White stamens. See Higo.

name: Haku-Tsuru
description: ('White Crane'; 'White Stork'). White. Medium, single to semidouble with large, rounded, crinkled petals. Vigorous, upright growth. M. (Japan 1934 - Chugai).

name: Hakuhan-kujaku
description: Color: Carmine red spotted white. Form: Semidouble. Size: Medium. Bloom Season: M. Growth Characteristics: Weeping growth. Leaf Characteristics: Light green. Place of Origination: Japan - Mikawa, Aichi Prefecture. Year of Registration / Origination: 1966. Heritage: White blotched or marbled form of Japonica 'Kujaku'.. Other Information: Means White Spotted Peacock in Japanese.

name: Hakusen
description: Color: White with gold anthers and yellow filaments. Form: Single with five broad outer petals, crimped and waved with a burst of pale yellow stamens in the center. Size: Large. Leaf Characteristics: Dark green. Originator: Shinohara family. Place of Origination: Japan - Kumamoto City. Year of Registration / Origination: 1964. Other Information: Means White fan in Japanese.

name: Hakutaka
description: Color: Snow white with flared yellowish anthers and white filaments. Form: Single with irregular crimped margins. Size: Large. Place of Origination: Japan - Kumamoto Prefecture. Year of Registration / Origination: Late 1800's. Other Information: Means 'White Hawk' in English.

name: Hall's Pride Variegated
description: Salmon Pink heavily blotched White variation of 'Hall's Pride'.

name: Hallelujah
description: White. Very large, semidouble. Vigorous, compact, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 1986 - Piet and Gaeta).

name: Hallmark
description: White. Large, semidouble with crinkled petals. Vigorous, upright growth. E-M. (U.S. 1958 - Ashby).

name: Han-Ling Raspberry
description: Raspberry on White. Large to very large, semidouble to peony form to formal double. (Sport of Japonica 'Carter's Sunburst'). (U.S. 1977 - L E Chow, Bakersfield, CA).

name: Han-Ling Snow
description: Pure White. Large to very large, semidouble to peony form to formal double. (Sport of Japonica 'Chow's Han-Ling'). (U.S. 1977 - LE Chow, Bakersfield, CA).

name: Hana-Fuki
description: ('Chalice'; 'Mrs. Howard Asper'). Soft Pink, sometimes blotched White. Medium to large, semidouble of cupped form. Average, compact growth. M. (Japan to U.S. [Star] 1930).

name: Hana-Fuki Variegated
description: Soft Pink blotched White variation of 'Hana-Fuki.'.

name: Hana-Jiman
description: White edged Pink. Medium, semidouble. Cold hardy.

name: Hana-Nana
description: White bordered with intense Deep Pink. Unknown size, single. Compact growth. (Sasanqua 'Hana-Jiman' seedling). (U.S. 1996 - Bobby Green, Green Nurseries, Fairhope, AL).

name: Hana-no-yuki (Flowers Strewn Like Snow)
description: Color: Very pale pink, with deeper pink margins with light yellow anthers and light yellow filaments. Form: Semidouble. Size: Large. Place of Origination: Japan. Year of Registration / Origination: 1898.

name: Hana-No-Yuki (Snow On Flower)
description: Pink flushed White. Medium, semidouble.

name: Hanajiman (Flower to Boast of)
description: Color: White edged pink. Form: Semidouble. Size: Medium. Bloom Season: Fall. Originator: Sôjiro Saitô, raised at Minagawa Chinka'en Nursery. Place of Origination: Japan - Saitama Prefecture. Year of Registration / Origination: 1905. Heritage: Seedling of Sasanqua 'Eikyû-shibori'.

name: Hanami Gasa
description: White heavily striped Rose Red. Medium to large, rose form double. Vigorous, bushy, upright growth. M-L.

name: Hanamigasa
description: Color: Blush white ground having soft pink patches and crimson streaks with yellow anthers. Form: Rose form double to formal double with 5-7 rows of petals. Size: Medium. Leaf Characteristics: Dark green, glossy. Originator: Tokuji Ôoka. Place of Origination: Japan - Kubiki-gun, Niigata Prefecture. Year of Registration / Origination: 1976.

name: Happy Memories
description: Soft Pink edged White with one outer petal having a Red streak and Ivory to Blush petaloids. Medium, anemone form. (Sport of Japonica 'Elegans Supreme'). (U.S. 1981 - Stone).

name: Happy Times
description: Soft Red and White. Small to medium, semidouble form. Average, open, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 2005 - Gordy).

name: Harbor Lights
description: Red with White frosting on petaloids. Medium to large, anemone form. Average, upright growth. E-M. Very cold hardy. (U.S. 1980's - Mark Cannon, Dothan, AL).

name: Harriet Knapp
description: Rose variegated White. Large, peony form. Average, upright growth. E-M. (U.S. 1967 - A. D. Albritton, Tallahassee, FL).

name: Harriett Moughon
description: Red variegated White. Large, formal double. Average, spreading growth. M-L. (U.S. 1968 - G. W. Moughon, Birmingham, AL).

name: Harriette Ruster
description: White tipped Pink. Medium, anemone form. Medium, spreading average density growth. E-L. (U.S. 1959 - Marvin Ruster, Pasadena, CA).

name: Harrison Jones
description: Light Salmon Pink, usually variegated with White pin stripes and White sometimes suffused with Pink. Large, anemone form with very large outer petals. Vigorous, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1955 - Longview).

name: Harry Bettes
description: White. Large, anemone form to full peony form and occasional formal double to semidouble. Vigorous, compact, upright growth. E-M. (U.S. 1977 - Tammia).

name: Haru-No-Utena
description: White spotted or striped Rose. Medium, semidouble. Slow, bushy growth. M-L. (Japan).

name: Harvey Short's Finale
description: White. Very large, semidouble. Vigorous, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 1980 - Short).

name: Hatchett
description: Pink and White. Medium, peony form with loose petals. (U.S. 1950 - Pfingstl).

name: Hawaiian Bride
description: White. Very large, peony form with fimbriated petals. Average, upright growth. M-L. (Sport of Japonica 'Hawaii"'). (N.Z. 1992 - T. and L. Armstrong, Christchurch).

name: Hazel E. Herrin Variegated
description: Old Rose blotched White form of 'Hazel E. Herrin'.

name: Heartwood Salute
description: White to Blush Pink. Miniature to small, unknown form. Strong, upright, open, weeping growth. E-M. Fragrant. (Saluenensis x Lutchuensis). (U.S. 1981 - Charvet).

name: Heavenly
description: Cream White. Large, peony form to rose form double. Average, compact, upright growth. L. (U.S. 1953 - Councilman).

name: Heavenly Daze
description: Pale Pink with Blush White tones. Large, anemone form with crinkled center petaloids varying from Pale Pink to Blush Pink in center. Average, open growth. E-L. (U.S. 1966 - C. W. Lattin, Lauderdale, MS).

name: Helen B.
description: Bright Pink with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Miniature, rose form double. Vigorous, open, upright growth. M-L. (Lutchuensis x Japonica). (U.S. 1985 - Jernigan).

name: Helen Beach
description: Pink with Rose Red splotches variegated White (Moired). Medium, loose peony to rose form double. Average, open growth. M. (Sport of Japonica 'Mary Edna Curlee'). (U.S. 2004 - Miles A. Beach, Mt. Pleasant, SC).

name: Helen Bower Variegated
description: Rose Red with Purple shading and White blotched or moired variation of 'Helen Bower'.

name: Helen Butler
description: White to Pink on edge. Medium, semidouble. Average, compact, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 1975 - Haynie).

name: Helen Calcutt
description: White striped Pink and Carmine. Large, single to semidouble with cylinder of stamens and occasional petaloid in center. Upright, open growth. M. (Sports Carmine and Pink edged White with occasional Carmine stripe). (Aus. 1960 - Brushfield).

name: Helen Dorn
description: White. Medium, semidouble. Vigorous, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1957).

name: Helen Glenn Variegated
description: Color: Red blotched white with yellow anthers and pinkish-white filaments. Form: Semidouble. Size: Medium to large. Bloom Season: E-M. Growth Rate: Average. Growth Characteristics: Spreading growth. Leaf Characteristics: Dark green. Originator: Bob Wines Nursery. Place of Origination: U.S. - Florida. Year of Registration / Origination: 1985. Heritage: Virus variegated form of Japonica 'Helen Glenn'.

name: Helen Sharpe
description: Pink highlighted White. Large to very large, semidouble to anemone form to peony form. Average, compact, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1984 - Tammia).

name: Heningham Smith
description: White. Medium, rose form double. Vigorous, spreading growth. M. (U.S. 1942 - Middleton).

name: Henny Drews
description: Pale Pink and White. Medium, peony form. Average, upright growth. E-M. (U.S. 2006 - Rupert E. Drews, Charleston, SC).

name: Henrietta L. Bradford
description: White. Medium to large, semidouble with wavy, upright petals and White stamens with Golden anthers. Vigorous, compact, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1961 - J. W. Bradford, San Diego, CA).

name: Henry E. Huntington Variegated
description: Rich Light Pink and White variegated form of 'Henry E. Huntington'. (U.S. 2013 - Nuccio's).

name: Henry Lunsford Variegated
description: Color: Bright red blotched white with yellow anthers and creamy white filaments. Form: Semidouble to peony form. Size: Large to very large. Bloom Season: M-L. Growth Rate: Vigorous. Growth Characteristics: Upright growth. Leaf Characteristics: Dark green. Place of Origination: U.S.. Year of Registration / Origination: 1992. Heritage: Virus variegated form of Japonica 'Henry Lunsford'.

name: Henry Middleton Variegated
description: Dark Red and White to predominately White form of 'Henry Middleton'.

name: Henry Turnbull
description: White. Large, single to semidouble. E. (Aus. 1950 - Turnbull).

name: Henry Viii
description: Deep Rose-Pink (also has variegated form) with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Medium, semidouble to full peony; petaloids and stamens within lower petals are also common. Vigorous, upright growth. E. (U.S. 1938-1939 - G. Gerbing, Fernandina, FL).

name: Herbert Earl Gatch
description: White with Red streaks and flecks. Large, formal double to rose form double. Vigorous, upright growth. M-L. (Japonica 'Lady Laura' seedling). (U.S. 2013 - Jerry Conrad Plymouth, FL).

name: Herme
description: ('Hikaru-Genji'; 'Jordan's Pride'; 'Souv. D' Henri Guichard'). Pink petals with irregular White border and streaked Deep Pink. Medium, semidouble. Vigorous, upright growth. M. Cold hardy. (Variations of this cultivar include 'Beauty of Holland'; 'Colonial Lady'; 'The Mikado'; 'Look-Away'; 'Quaintance'; 'Orchid Pink'; 'Spring Sonnet'). (Japan to U.S. 1875 - Sacramento, CA).

name: Herme White
description: White variation of 'Herme'.

name: Hi-Jinx Variegated
description: Pink blotched White variation of 'Hi-Jinx'.

name: Hi-No-Hakama
description: Scarlet. Large, single with flared White stamens. See Higo.

name: Hidden Treasure
description: White with occasional Rose streak. Small, anemone form. Slow, spreading growth. M-L. (U.S. 1959 - Wylam).

name: High Fragrance
description: Pale Ivory Pink with Deeper Pink shading at edge. Medium, peony form. Vigorous, open growth. M. Fragrant. (Japonica 'Mrs. Bertha A. Harms' × [Hybrid 'Scentuous' × Hybrid 'Salab'l). (N.Z. 1986 - J. Finlay).

name: High Hills
description: White. Medium to large, full peony form. Average, compact growth. M. (U.S. 1953 - Short).

name: High Jinks
description: White blotched Red. Medium to large, full peony form. (Sport of Japonica 'Helenor'). (Aus. 1945).

name: High Society Variegated
description: Clear Pink and White variation of 'High Society'.

name: High Wide 'n Handsome
description: Luminous Pink shading to Pink tones in heart of flower. Large, semidouble with very large, fluted, separated and erect petals and White stamens. Vigorous, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1960 - McCaskill).

name: Higo
description: Name used as general name for special garden form of Camellia Japonica, developed in Kumamoto, Japan, located in district formerly called Higo Province; form is generally single (a few cultivars are slightly semidouble), with thick, round, broad petals and prominent stamens of White, soft Pink and Pale Yellow which are generally flared (a few cultivars having circular or tubular stamens), standing out independently from the base. Some cultivars have stamens topped with petaloids. Cultivars are listed by name with description as to color, size and form with reference to Higo. (Japan 1800's - Kumamoto).

name: Higo-Ozeki
description: Crimson with spreading Gold anthers and White filaments. Very large, single. (Japan - Kumamoto Prefecture). See Higo.

name: Hikarugenji
description: Color: Dark rosy broadly margined white, with a few streaks of deeper crimson reaching to petal edge and a veining of deeper crimson in the colored area of the petals. Form: Peony form. Size: Medium. Bloom Season: M-L. Growth Characteristics: Upright growth. Leaf Characteristics: Medium green. Place of Origination: Japan - Kanto area. Year of Registration / Origination: 1859.

name: Hilda Jamieson
description: Deep Pink shading to White at base. Large, semidouble with ruffled petals. Vigorous, upright growth. M-L. (Aus. 1977 - Miss H. B. Jamieson, Wahroonga, NSW).

name: Hill
description: White spotted Pink, sporting to solid Pink. Medium, semidouble. (U.S. 1953).

name: Himatsuri
description: Rose Red blotched White. Miniature, loose anemone form.

name: Hime-Shira-Yuki
description: White. Miniature, semidouble.

name: Hime-wabisuke
description: Yokoyama, 1975, Gendai Tsubaki Meikan, p.200, black and white photo; Encyclopedia of Camellias in Colour, vol.II, 1978, pl.349, p.144; Seibundō Shinkōsha, 1979, Senchinshū, p.128 colour photo, p.243, description: Small single, 6-7 petals, wabisuke type flower, soft pink with streaks of deeper pink. Three outer petals, notched at the apex and a compact, central column of stamens with white degenerate anthers. Leaves small, elliptic, coriaceous, long acuminate, dark green, finely serrate. See colour photo, p.277, Yokoyama & Kirino, 1989, Nihon no Chinka. Discovered at Ama-gun, Aichi Prefecture. Discovered by Shinichi Asai, named and released in 1974. For botanical drawing, colour photo and description see pp.51, 52, 54; Kyōto Engei Kurabu, 1971, Tsubaki Tokushū`, No.11. Chinese synonym ‘Jituozhu’.

name: Himegoto
description: White. Medium, rose form double.

name: Hinode-Gumo
description: White shaded Pink. Medium, single with fluted petals.

name: Hiodoshi
description: Scarlet. Medium to large, single with flared White stamens. See Higo.

name: Hiryû (Australian)
description: See 'Kanjiro'. 5 mine left in chanter HIRYÜ (FLYING DRAGON) - Formerly Vernalis - ('Hiryo'; 'Red Bird'). Deep Crimson Red. Small, rose form double. HIRYU-NISHIKI (FLYING DRAGON BROCADE) - Formerly Vernalis - ('Hiryo- Nishiki'). Crimson splashed White. Unknown size, rose form double. HOSHI-HIRYÜ - Formerly Vernalis - Crimson dotted White. (Sport of Sasanqua 'Hiryû").

name: Holly Bright Variegated
description: Color: Glossy salmon red blotched white with yellow anthers and white filaments. Form: Semidouble with crepe petals. Size: Large. Bloom Season: M. Growth Rate: Average. Growth Characteristics: Compact, upright growth. Leaf Characteristics: Dark green unique crinkled holly-like foliage. Heritage: Virus variegated form of Japonica 'Holly Bright'.

name: Holy Pure (Shengjie)
description: ('Sheng Jie'). Inner two whorls White lightly tinged Pink, outer whorls slightly Darker Pink with Light Purple margins, sometimes frosted White with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Large to very large, rose form double to semidouble. Vigorous, upright growth. M-L. (Reticulata hybrid 'Suzanne Withers' × Japonica 'Kona'). (U.S. 2007 - John Wang, Orinda, CA).

name: Holy White
description: White with Yellow anthers and Light Cream filaments. Large, semidouble to loose peony form. Average, upright, open growth. M-L. (Seedling of Japonica 'Snow Lady'). (U.S. 2019 - Pat Johnson, Cairo, GA).

name: Homer Fritchie
description: White spotted Pink. Miniature, tiered formal double. Vigorous, compact, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1975 - Tammia).

name: Honey Bee
description: Red to Red and White. Medium, formal double to loose peony form. Average, compact growth. M-L. (U.S. 1970 - Alfter).

name: Honey Chile
description: Deep Red. Large, semidouble with upright petals interspersed with White petaloids, and narrow, twisted petaloids interspersed with crown of stamens in center. (U.S. 1959 - W. D. Pleasant, Beaumont, TX).

name: Honeyglow
description: Deep Honey color to Ivory center petals and faint Blush under petals. Medium, formal double with incurved center petals resembling six pointed star. Vigorous, open, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 1974 - Stone).

name: Honeymoon
description: Coral Pink bud opening to a very Creamy White with Yellow petals at the base. Medium to large, semidouble. Vigorous, open, upright growth. L. (Japonica 'Guilio Nuccio' × Pitardii x Chrysantha). (U.S. 1992 - Nuccio's).

name: Hope Griffin
description: Ivory White. Large to very large, semidouble to anemone form with rabbit ears and stamens. Average, upright growth. M- L. (Sport of Japonica 'Nuccio's Pink Lace'). (U.S. 1999 - G. Griffin, Nashville, TN).

name: Hopkin's Pink Dawn
description: Soft Pink to Light Pink shading to White at edge. (Sport of Japonica 'Hopkin's Pink'). (U.S. 1969 - McCaskill).

name: Hopkin's White
description: White. Medium, single. (U.S. 1953 - Lindo).

name: Horkan
description: ('Mille Beaux'; 'Variabilis'; 'Missima'; 'Pearl Marginata'; 'Dan McCarthy'). White striped Red, Pink and Rose and in solid colors. Medium, variform. Vigorous, angular growth. L. (Not the same variety as Japonica Rena Campbell' - 2014). (England from Orient 1816).

name: Horry Frost
description: Red and White. Small, formal double. E-M. (U.S. 1942 - Magnolia).

name: Houltonwood
description: Dark Pink with occasional Darker Pink stripes with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Medium, semidouble. Average, bushy growth. M. (U.S. 2019 - Morris Marlborough, Hammond, LA).

name: Howard Asper Variegated
description: Color: Salmon pink blotched white with yellow anthers and light green filaments. Form: Pony form with loose, upright petals. Size: Very large. Bloom Season: M-L. Growth Rate: Vigorous. Growth Characteristics: Upright, spreading growth. Leaf Characteristics: Very large, heavy foliage - dark green. Place of Origination: U.S.. Year of Registration / Origination: 1965. Heritage: Virus variegated form of Reticulata hybrid 'Howard Asper'.

name: Huanghainan Baozhu
description: Huang Nursery Catalogue, 1949. No description. Originated in China. Different reading: 'Huang Hai Nan Pao Chu'. Gao, Jiyin, 1998, The World's Best Camellia Cultivars, p.155, and Gao, Jiyin, 2007, The Identification....Outstanding Camellias, p.297 with colour photos; A crossing of C.sasanqua and C.oleifera. The first yellowish hybrid successfully bred in China. Flowers white with yellowish centre, occasionally with pink tips to petals. Anemone form, miniature to small, outside petals 7-10, small petals and petaloids in a ball-shaped centre. Leaves mid-green, small and narrow. Upright, average groawth. Flowers early.

name: Hugh Evans Blush
description: Very Light Blush Pink, almost White. Medium, single. Upright, somewhat lacy growth; profuse bloomer. (Sport of Sasanqua 'Hugh Evans').

name: Hulyn Smith Variegated
description: Color: Soft pink blotched white with yellow anthers. Form: Semidouble. Size: Large. Bloom Season: M-L. Growth Rate: Average. Growth Characteristics: Upright growth. Leaf Characteristics: Dark green. Place of Origination: 1982. Year of Registration / Origination: U.S.. Heritage: Virus variegated of Reticulata hybrid 'Hulyn Smith'.

name: Hulyn's Meadowbrook
description: Medium Pink, sometimes frosted White with Gold anthers and White filaments; the bloom is often fluorescent. Very large, semidouble to full peony. Vigorous, upright, spreading growth. M-L. (Reticulata hybrid 'Suzanne Withers' × Reticulata hybrid 'Frank Houser'). (U.S. 2014 - Hulyn Smith).

name: Hulyn's Sweet Emily
description: Dark Pink with White anthers and Red filaments. Very large, peony form. Average, dense growth. M. (Reticulata hybrid 'Hulyn Smith' x Reticulata hybrid 'Delta Dawn'). (U.S. 2010 - Hulyn Smith).

name: I Bird Variegated
description: Dark Red with White variegation. Large, peony form. Average, open, upright growth. E-L. (Formerly called JAY BIRD). (U.S. 1999 - Jay Ellis Jr., Keystone Hts., FL).

name: Ice Cream Smoothie
description: Deep Pink, shading from a near White center through Pale Pink. Medium, peony form. Slow, dense and spreading growth. M-L. (Pitardii seedling). (N.Z. 2004 - Haydon).

name: Ice Follies
description: Bright Pink. Large, semidouble. Average, dense, upright growth. L. Cold hardy to - 5°F. (Hybrid 'November Pink' × Oleifera 'Lu Shan Snow'). (U.S. 1992 - Ackerman).

name: Ice Queen
description: White. Large, semidouble. Compact growth. M. (Aus. 1965).

name: Iced Fragrance
description: White. Large, semidouble to peony form. Slow, dense, upright growth. E-L. Fragrant. (Aus. 1990 - Mildorrie Camellias, Jasper's Brush, NSW).

name: Iceland
description: White. Large, semidouble with oval petals standing apart. Average, compact growth. M-L. (U.S. 1965 - Julington).

name: Ichiraku
description: White. Miniature, bell shaped single.

name: Ida May Johnston
description: White striped Pink. Large, semidouble to loose peony form. M. (U.S. 1961 - J. W. Bradford, San Diego, CA).

name: Ida Weisner
description: White. Large, semidouble with tight cluster of stamens in center. Vigorous, open, pendulous growth. E-M. (U.S. 1965 - Weisner).

name: Idalia
description: White with Pale Pink halo through center of each petal. Large, rose form double. Average, upright growth. E. (U.S. 1971 - J. Moon, Lake Charles, LA).

name: Igloo
description: White. Large, full peony form. Average, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1965 - Breschini).

name: Ikari-shibori
description: Large semidouble white with crimson streaks, 13–15 petals; blooms mid-season to late.

name: Illie Rountree
description: White striped Red. Medium to large, loose peony form. Average, open, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 1997 - C. Elliott, Swainsboro, GA).

name: Im Smelley
description: Dark Pink with bright Yellow anthers and White filaments. Very large, semidouble. Vigorous, upright, open growth. M-L. (Reticulata hybrid ‘Curtain Call’ x Reticulata hybrid ‘Pleasant Memories’). (U.S. 2013 - James and Elaine Smelley, Moss Point, MS).

name: Imagination
description: Pure White. Medium to large, semidouble form with deeply fluted petals, and a waxy sheen. Vigorous, open, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 2005 - Gordy).

name: Imbricata Rubra
description: Color: Carmine rose, often with central petals streaked white. Form: Formal double - fully imbricated. Size: Medium. Leaf Characteristics: Dark green. Originator: John Dampier Parks for the Royal Horticultural Society of London. Place of Origination: China to England. Year of Registration / Origination: 1824 - first bloomed in England in 1827.

name: Imura
description: White. Medium to large, semidouble. Vigorous, open, willowy growth. M. (Reported to be same as 'Miyakodori'). (U.S. 1939 - Overlook).

name: In The Pink Variegated
description: White striped Pink variation of 'In the Pink'. (U.S. 1977 - Kramer).

name: In The Pink Variegated (Weeks)
description: Rose Pink variegated White. Medium, rose form double to formal double. Vigorous, compact, upright growth. M-L. ('Virus variegated form of Japonica 'In the Pink'; 'Not to be confused with 'In the Pink Variegated' - Pink with White stripes'). (U.S. 2019 - Tommy Weeks, Conroe, TX).

name: Indian Chief Variegated
description: Red blotched White variation of 'Indian Chief'.

name: Indian Summer Variegated
description: Deep Rose Red blotched White variation of 'Indian Summer'.

name: Indiscreet
description: White penciled Red with Red petaloids to Cream White penciled Pink with Pink petaloids to solid Red. Medium, peony form. Slow, compact, upright growth. E-M. (U.S. 1949 - S. W. Miller, El Cajon, CA).

name: Insula Oya
description: Color: Red with yellow anthers and white filaments. Form: Semidouble. Size: Medium. Leaf Characteristics: Medium green. Originator: Botanic Nursery Thoby. Place of Origination: France - Thoby. Year of Registration / Origination: 2009.

name: Irene
description: Pink with White mixed with Red stripes. Small, formal double. Average, spreading growth. M-L. (U.S. 1983 - A. Gonos, Fresno, CA).

name: Irene Bergamini
description: Vibrant medium Pink with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Medium to large, semidouble. Average, upright, spreading growth. M. (U.S. 2015 - Don Bergamini, Martinez, CA).

name: Irene Coker
description: White, lightly striped Pink, occasionally solid Pink. Medium, rose form double. Average, compact growth. E-M. (U.S. 1955 - B. F. Coker, Pensacola, FL).

name: Irene Rester Variegated
description: Currant Red blotched White variation of 'Irene Rester'.

name: Iris
description: White with a few Pink strips. Medium, formal double. Upright growth. M. (Aus. 1848 - Baptiste & Son Nursery, Sydney).

name: Iris Shealy
description: Clear Pink with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Large to very large, anemone to semidouble to loose peony form. Average, bushy, dense growth. M. (U.S. 2022 - Gordy).

name: Irrational Exuberance
description: White (typically 50-80%) with extensive Red veining at center with Yellow anthers and Flesh colored filaments. Medium to large, rose form double. Slow, spreading growth. M. (Japonica "Tama-no-ura' seedling). (U.S. 2014 - Bobby Green, Green Nurseries, Fairhope, AL).

name: Isabel
description: White. Medium, formal double. M-L. (For other forms of this cultivar see 'Yirgella' and 'Geoff Hamilton'). (Aus. 1868 - Macarthur).

name: Isabel Cordelia
description: Medium Pink and White with White anthers and White filaments. Miniature, single. Vigorous, upright growth. E. (Seedling of Hybrid 'Yume'). (U.S. 2016 - Tsubaki Camellias, Savannah, GA).

name: Isabel's Birthday
description: Light Pink shading to Hot Pink on edges with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Medium, semidouble to anemone to loose peony form. Average, upright, open growth. M-L. (Seedling of Japonica 'Snow Lady'). (U.S. 2022 - Pat B. Johnson, Cairo, GA).

name: Isabel's Discovery
description: Dark Rich Red with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Medium, full peony form. Slow, upright, open growth. M- L. (Seedling of Reticulata hybrid 'Frank Houser Variegated'). (U.S. 2022 - Pat B. Johnson, Cairo, GA).

name: Isabel's Surprise
description: White to Light Pink with Darker Pink streaks. Miniature, bell shaped single. Average, open, spreading, upright growth. E- M. (Hybrid seedling). (N.Z. 1992 - J. White, Whakatane).

name: Isla Warriner
description: Bright Red with occasional White variegation with Yellow anthers and Pale Pink filaments. Medium to large, semidouble form with almost Higo form stamens. Average, upright, medium density growth. M-L. (U.S. 2022 - Tom Warriner, Birmingham, AL).

name: Itch
description: White with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Large, semidouble. Average, upright growth. E-M. (U.S. 2009 - William Brierly, Mobile, AL).

name: Itsukamachi Variegated
description: Red and White variation of ' Itsukamachi.'.

name: Ivory Tower
description: White. Large, formal double with high center to peony form. Average, compact growth. M. (U.S. 1966 - Shackelford).

name: Iwane
description: ('Iwane-Shibori'; 'F. G. #2'). Rose Red mottled White. Medium, semidouble. Slow, compact growth. M. (Japan 1891 - Yokohama).

name: Iza Houser
description: White. Large, semidouble. Vigorous, compact growth. M. (U.S. 1956 - M. R. Murray, Fort Valley, GA).

name: Izma
description: White with Pink on reverse side. Medium, semidouble.

name: J. C. Williams
description: Color: Phlox pink with golden anthers and white filaments. Form: Single. Size: Medium. Bloom Season: E-L. Growth Rate: Vigorous. Growth Characteristics: Upright, pendulous growth. Originator: J .C. Williams. Place of Origination: England - Caerhays Castle, Cornwall. Year of Registration / Origination: 1940. Heritage: Saluenensis x Japonica.

name: J. J. Whitfield Variegated
description: Dark Red blotched White variation of JJ Whitfield'.

name: J. Stewart Howard Variegated
description: Bright Red blotched or moired White variation of 'J Stewart Howard'. (U.S. 1983 - R. Bond, Dallas, TX).

name: J.J. Pringle Smith Variegated
description: Bright Red and White form of JJ Pringle Smith'.

name: Jack Frost
description: White. Large, semidouble with frosted appearance on upper surface of petals. M-L. (U.S. 1957 - Bowman).

name: Jack Grimm
description: Pink with White filaments and Yellow anthers, Very large, semidouble. Average, upright growth. L. (U.S. 2019 - Camellia Heaven - John Grimm, Bush, LA).

name: Jack I. Crocker
description: White with Dark Pink stripes and flecks. Small, semidouble form with rabbit ears. Vigorous, upright growth. M. (U.S. 2001 - Eileen C. Hart, Odessa, FL).

name: Jack Wilson Variegated

Hulyn Smith of Valdosta, Georgia, and propagated by Ed Northcutt of Tallahassee, Florida, E. Hulyn Smith of Valdosta, Georgia, and Mark Crawford of Valdosta, Georgia.. Year of Registration / Origination: 1968 - ACRS Registration #97 - first bloomed in 1966. Heritage: Seedling of Reticulata 'Crimson Robe'.

name: Myken

name: Myra Wadsworth

Rose Pink marbled White in varying degrees. Large, semidouble with large, outer petals and a few smaller center petals lying flat. Slow, spreading growth. M. (Japan to England 1889 - G. Waller).

name: Nancy Mandarich

name: Nancy Schmoe

name: Nancy Weems

(U.S. 2009 - John Wang, Orinda, CA).

Heritage: Seedling of Hiemalis 'Shishigashira' x Sasanqua. Other Information: â€˜Green 02-004â€™ PP27553; cold hardy.

name: Ogi-No-Mine

name: Oh-Zora

description: Color: Pink edged white with dark red stripes with golden anthers and pinkish filaments. Form: Semidouble. Size: Large. Bloom Season: M. Place of Origination: Japan - Kantï¿½ Area. name: Olive Lee Shepp

name: Olivia

name: Ollie Mack

name: One Second

name: Oniji

name: Oninski

name: Orandakô (Holland Red)

(Reticulata 'Crimson Robe' seedling). (U.S. 1968 - C. W. Lattin, Lauderdale, MS)

name: Osaki

name: Oscar B. Elmer Variegated

Heritage: Virus variegated form of Japonica 'Oscar B. Elmer'.

name: Ota-Haku

name: Otohime

name: Otome White

name: Our Betty Variegated

name: Our Bill

name: Our Nancy

Flowers mid-season. Leaves lanceolate. dark green, venation prominent, apex acuminate, margins shallowly serrate. See p.205, Seibundō Shinkōsha, 1979, Senchinshū; Colour pl.306, Encyclopedia of Camellias in Colour, vol.II, 1978. Originated in Chūbu, Japan. A chance seedling of unknown parentage, originated and released by Yoshiyasu Wakayama in 1974. Resembles Kayoiji.

name: Oyler's Christina Marie

name: Oyler's Sydney

description: Light Pink with Golden anthers and Pinkish- White filaments. Medium to large, loose peony form, stacked with undulating petals. Average, full growth. E-M. (U.S. 2019 - Tommy Weeks, Conroe, TX).

name: Rachel Tarpy
description: White shading to Blush Pink with occasional Red lines or dashes. Medium to large, anemone form with wavy, concave, ruffled outer petals. Average growth. E-M. (U.S. 1981 - G. F. Abendroth, Shreveport, LA).

name: Rachel Wilkes
description: White. Large, semidouble with stamens in circle of petals. Average, upright growth. E-M. (U.S. 1979 - Mrs. H. Johnson, Madison, FL).

name: Radiant Star
description: White with Gold anthers and White filaments. Large, semidouble. Slow, upright, dense growth. M-L. (Seedling of Japonica 'Owen Henry'). (U.S. 2019 - Pat Johnson, Cairo, GA).

name: Ragland Supreme
description: Milk White. Medium, formal double. Average, spreading growth. M. (U.S. 1968 - Ragland).

name: Rainbow
description: White with each petal bordered Red. Medium, single.

name: Rampey E. Thomas
description: White. Medium to large, semidouble form. Average, upright growth. M. Cold hardy. (U.S. 1999 - L. Baxter, Seneca, SC).

name: Randell Corbett
description: White edged Pink. Medium, irregular semidouble to rose form double. RED BIRD - Formerly Vernalis - See 'Hiryû'.

name: Randolph
description: White. Large, formal double to rose form double. Average, upright, open growth. E-M. (U.S. 2019 - Pat Johnson, Cairo, GA).

name: Rasen-Zome
description: ('Printed Woolen'). Light Rose Pink, sometimes mottled White. Medium, semidouble to peony form. Vigorous, compact growth. M-L. (Japan to U.S. [Star] 1915).

name: Raspberry Ice
description: Light Rose Pink with wide White petal edges and streaks of Raspberry on each petal. Medium, semidouble. (Sport of Japonica 'Cinderella'). (U.S. 1987 - Monrovia Nsy. Co., Azusa, CA).

name: Raspberry Parfait
description: Raspberry Red with touches of Creamy White. Miniature, formal double. Average, upright growth. L. (U.S. 1973 - Witman).

name: Raspberry Ripple
description: White with Pink stripes. Very large, semidouble. Average growth. E-L. (N.Z. 1989 - R. MacDonald, Waiuku).

name: Raspberry Ripple Picotee
description: White, with a defined Pink edge, which sometimes bleeds back into the White with Yellow anthers and Cream filaments. Very large, semidouble. Average growth. E-L. (Sport of Japonica 'Raspberry Ripple'). (N.Z. 1996 - Haydon).

name: Raspberry Sherbet
description: Light Fuchsia with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Large, semidouble. M-L. (U.S. 2003 - Gordon E. Eade, Pensacola, FL).

name: Ray Gentry Variegated
description: Color: Red variegated white with golden anthers and golden filaments. Form: Semidouble to peony form with prominent rabbit ears. Size: Very large. Bloom Season: M-L. Growth Rate: Vigorous. Growth Characteristics: Upright grow. Leaf Characteristics: Dark green. Heritage: Virus variegated form of Reticulata hybrid 'Ray Gentry'.

name: Raymond Beales
description: Color: Dark pink with gold anthers and white filaments. Form: Semidouble. Size: Medium. Bloom Season: M. Growth Rate: Vigorous. Growth Characteristics: Upright growth. Originator: Shepp's Shade Garden. Place of Origination: U.S. - Pasadena, California. Year of Registration / Origination: 1950. Heritage: Sport of Japonica  'Olive Lee Shepp'.

name: Rayna Simone
description: Coral Rose with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Miniature, anemone. Average, upright growth. E-L. (U.S. 2017 - Don Bergamini, Martinez, CA).

name: Razzle-Dazzle
description: Red with Pink and White stripes. Medium, anemone form. Average, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 1992 - Piet and Gaeta, CA).

name: Rebecca
description: Color: Vivid pink streaked with white with yellow anthers and white filaments. Form: Single. Size: Very large. Bloom Season: E. Leaf Characteristics: Dark green. Place of Origination: Belgium. Year of Registration / Origination: 1858.

name: Rebel Yell
description: White striped, speckled and moired Red. Large, semidouble to peony form with twisted, curled, and crepe petals. Average, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1961 - Wheeler).

name: Rebel Yell Blush Variegated
description: Blush Pink blotched White variation of 'Rebel Yell Blush'.

name: Rebel Yell Pink Variegated
description: Pink blotched White variation of 'Rebel Yell Pink'.

name: Rebel Yell Red Variegated
description: Red blotched White variation of 'Rebel Yell Red'.

name: Rebel Yell White
description: White. (Sport of Japonica 'Rebel Yell').

name: Red Cap Variegated
description: Rose Pink blotched White variation of 'Red Cap'.

name: Red Coral
description: Bight Coral Red at the edges fading to Deep Rose Pink towards the center with darker veining with bright Yellow anthers and Light Pink fading to White filaments. Large, semidouble to loose peony. Vigorous, upright, dense growth. M-L. (U.S. 2017 - Jerry Hogsette, Gainesville, FL).

name: Red Devil Variegated
description: Red variegated White variation of 'Red Devil.'.

name: Red Elephant Variegated
description: Color: Dark red blotched white. Form: Peony form. Size: Large. Bloom Season: M. Growth Rate: Vigorous. Growth Characteristics: Upright growth. Place of Origination: U.S.. Year of Registration / Origination: 1962. Heritage: Virus variegated form of Japonica 'Red Elephant. Other Information: Also called 'King Size Variegated'.

name: Red Garnet Variegated
description: Garnet Red and White variation of 'Red Garnet'. (U.S. 1983 - Kramer).

name: Red Hot
description: Bright Red occasionally with one or two small White petals in center. Large, full peony form. Upright, compact growth. M. (U.S. 1959 - Hudson).

name: Red Mountain
description: Dark Red with Yellow Anthers and White filaments. Small, full peony. Average, upright growth. E-M. (Japonica 'Man Size' seedling). (U.S. 2013 - James and Elaine Smelley, Moss Point, MS).

name: Red Wonder Variegated
description: Dark Red blotched White variation of 'Red Wonder'.

name: Reda Scott
description: White. Medium, formal double. Vigorous, compact, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 1987 - Elizabeth R. Scott, Aiken, SC).

name: Reg Ragland Variegated
description: Red blotched White variation of 'Reg Ragland'.

name: Regal Rose
description: Bright clear Pink. Large, rose form double with wide undulating petals and crinkly center petals around White stamens. Vigorous, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 1963 - Ragland).

name: Regan Dove
description: Deep Rose Red with bright Yellow anthers and White to Light Pink filaments. Medium, semidouble form. Slow, bushy, dense growth. M-L. Cold hardy to - 10° F. (U.S. 2023 - Tony Dove, Harwood, MD).

name: Reiho
description: Snow White. Large, single. (Sport of Japonica 'Oaso'). See Higo.

name: Reilly's Reflection
description: Rose with White moiré and frosting with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Miniature, semidouble. Average, upright, spreading growth. E-M. (U.S. 2019 - Thomas Sellers, Bolivia NC).

name: Reine Marie Henriette
description: Rose Pink striped White to Rose Pink variegated White. Medium, formal double. Average, compact growth. M. (France 1908 - Guichard).

name: Rena Campbell
description: Variable: Rose to solid Red, White, White with Red stripes, and medium Pink with stripes and flecks of Deeper Pink. Medium, loose peony form. Vigorous, upright, bushy growth. M-L. (Seed parent of 'Mrs. F. L. Gibson' and seed grandparent of 'Betty Sheffield'). (U.S. 1890-1900 - Loui Lauraine Campbell, Quitman, GA).

name: Rena Swick Variegated
description: Bright Pink and White variation of 'Rena Swick'.

name: Reuel Adams
description: Rose variegated White. Large, semidouble with wavy, crinkled petals. Vigorous, open, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1969 - R. T. Adams, Jacksonville, FL).

name: Revelation
description: Pink and White. Medium, semidouble to peony form. (U.S. 1942 - Magnolia).

name: Reverend John Bowman Variegated
description: Shell Pink and White variation of 'Reverend John Bowman'.

name: Reverend John Bryant
description: White turning to delicate Light Orchid. Large, formal double. Vigorous, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1966 - J. R. Bryant, Shreveport, LA).

name: Rhoda Gonzalez
description: White. Medium, full peony form. Vigorous, open growth. E. (U.S. 1952 - Mrs. C. F. Gonzalez, Pensacola, FL).

name: Rhysa Johnson
description: White, Pink and Red streaks. Large, loose peony to full peony. Average, upright, spreading growth. E-L. (Chance Japonica seedling). (U.S. 2016 - Pat Johnson, Cairo, GA).

name: Richard Mims
description: Light Pink with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Large, semidouble to loose peony form. Average, upright, spreading growth. M-L. (U.S. 2016 - Hulyn Smith).

name: Richard Nixon
description: White shaded Pink and striped Rose Pink. Large, anemone form with outer row of upright, crinkled petals. Compact growth. E-L. (For a variation of this cultivar see 'Pat Nixon'). (U.S. 1954 - U. B. Stair, Whittier, CA).

name: Rilyn Boueres
description: Lavender variegated White with bright Yellow anthers and White filaments. Medium to large, semidouble. Vigorous, upright, dense growth. M-L. (U.S. 2013 - Howard and Mary Rhodes, Tallahassee, FL).

name: Rish Plant
description: White. Small, single.

name: Rita Blythe
description: White. Large, anemone form. Slow, bushy growth. M. (Aus. 1996 - D. Blythe, Kojonup, W. Aus.).

name: Rivers Yerger
description: White occasionally striped Rose Pink to Red. Medium to large, full peony form. Average, compact, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1955 - Wheeler).

name: Rivers Yerger Pink Variegated
description: Rose Pink blotched White variation of 'Rivers Yerger Pink'.

name: Riverside
description: White. Medium, single with crepe petals.

name: Robbie Anne
description: Old Rose blotched and spotted White. Large, semidouble with Yellow stamens occasionally mixed with inner petals and petaloids. M-L. (U.S. 1961).

name: Robert Casamajor Variegated
description: Turkey Red blotched White form of 'Robert Casamajor'.

name: Robert Henty
description: White with Pink stripes and markings. Large, semidouble to loose peony form. (Sport of Japonica 'Charles Henty'). (Aus. 1975 - Henty).

name: Robert Hills
description: Dark Pink splotched White. Medium, semidouble and formal double with some imbricated petals. Average, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1980 - Tammia).

name: Robert Norton
description: White striped Pink. Medium to large, semidouble. Average, compact growth. E. (For another form of this cultivar, see 'Jean Norton'). (U.S. 1944 - Overlook).

name: Robert Train Variegated
description: Dark Red and White variation of 'Robert Train'.

name: Robert's Tina
description: White with fine Red stripes, sometimes Solid Red with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Medium, semidouble to anemone to loose peony form with burst of stamens and petaloids in center. Average, upright, open growth. E-M. (U.S. 2021 - Pat B. Johnson, Cairo, GA).

name: Robin Ann Cross
description: Medium Pink with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Large, semidouble to rose form double. Vigorous, upright, dense growth. M. (U.S. 2016 - Gordy).

name: Robin Ruth Ray
description: White at center blending to Pink at petal edges. Medium, semidouble. Average, open growth. M. (U.S. 1998 - W. and M. A. Ray, Fresno, CA).

name: Robyn Dunne
description: Soft Mauve tipped White.

name: Rochelle Marrinan
description: White center shading to Pink stripes with Gold anthers and Yellow filaments. Medium, semidouble to loose peony form. Average, spreading, open growth. E-L. (Seedling of Japonica ' China Doll'). (U.S. 2018 - Pat Johnson, Cairo, GA).

name: Rodney Lawrence
description: Bright Red that really shines in the light with Golden anthers and White filaments. Very large, semidouble form. Vigorous, bushy, medium density growth. M. (Seedling of Japonica 'Mary Ann Lawrence'). (U.S. 2022 - Steve & Gayle Lawrence, Tallahassee, FL).

name: Roi des Belges
description: Color: Different colors on the same plant, sometimes self red (Lavinia Maggi Rosea), sometimes red with splashes of white. Form: Peony form. Size: Large. Bloom Season: M. Growth Rate: Vigorous. Growth Characteristics: Upright growth. Leaf Characteristics: Medium green. Originator: Dominique Vervaene. Place of Origination: Belgium. Year of Registration / Origination: 1864. Heritage: Virus variegated form of Japonica 'Lavinia Maggi Rosea'.

name: Roi Leopold
description: Bright Rose Red splashed White. Medium, formal double. Compact, upright growth. M. (Belgium 1851 - Emile Defresne, Liege).

name: Rokukasen
description: JCS., Japan Camellia, 1986, No.22, p.4, colour photo: Originated in a garden in Hamakita City, selected by Muramatsu and named by Hakoda. Single, small to medium size. A spring flowering sasanqua, white variegation on peach-red background. Petals at intervals having a dappled pattern and size variation. Hakoda, JCS, 1987, Tsubaki, No.26, p.51 as 'Rokkasen': Red ground with white spots. Blooms early to mid-season. Different reading 'Rokkasen'. Discovered by Tsoyushi Matsumura in Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture, Japan, late 1970s, named by Naotoshi Hakoda. International Camellia Society, 2023. Splendid Sasanquas, an International Ornament, p.71.

name: Roll-In' Toomer
description: White with Yellow anthers and Yellow filaments. Very large, semidouble. Average, upright growth. E. (Japonica 'Moonlight Bay' x Japonica 'Joshua Youtz'). (U.S. 2017 - Kenneth Rogers, Auburn, AL).

name: Roman Soldier Variegated
description: Bright Red blotched White variation of 'Roman Soldier'.

name: Roncharbar
description: Cream White. Medium, semidouble with irregular petals to rose form double. Average, open growth. M. (U.S. 1956 - Miss E. W. Boorman, Temple City, CA).

name: Roosevelt Blues Variegated
description: Purplish Red blotched White form of 'Roosevelt Blues'.

name: Rosa Mundi
description: Red blotched White. Medium, formal double. M. (Two other cultivars are listed under this name in old literature, one a rose form double of pale Pink streaked deep Cerise Pink and the other a rose form double of Pink streaked White). (England 1832 - Press).

name: Rosalie Deneen Fendig
description: Shell Pink margined White. Large, semidouble. (Sport of Japonica 'Gladys Fendig'). (U.S. 1957 - A. Fendig, Sea Island, GA).

name: Rosalynn Carter
description: Pink shading to Lighter Pink edges with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Large, rose form double to loose peony form; rarely formal double. Average, upright, dense growth. M. (First Lady of Georgia Series). (U.S. 2017 - Gordy).

name: Rosary Variegated
description: ('Pink Glory Variegated'). Light Rose Pink and White form of 'Rosary'.

name: Rose 'n Bloom Variegated
description: Coral Rose marked White variation of 'Rose 'N Bloom'.

name: Rose And Snow
description: ('Emma Ladd'). Rose Pink spotted White. Medium, semidouble with petaloids in center. Vigorous, compact, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 1942 - Middleton).

name: Rose Of Kastellorizo
description: White. Small, formal double. Average, dense, upright growth. M-L. (Aus. 1998 - F. Abbott, Rossmoyne, W. Aus.).

name: Rose Queen Variegated
description: Rose Pink spotted White variation of 'Rose Queen'.

name: Rosea Mundi
description: Deep Pink. Medium, semidouble with irregular, wavy petals. Average, compact growth. M-L. (Not cultivar listed in old literature, which was formal double of White striped Pink). (U.S. 1946 - Magnolia).

name: Rosea Superba Variegated
description: ('Margaret Sandusky'). Deep Rose Pink spotted White variation of 'Rosea Superba'.

name: Roselle Bell
description: White. Large, anemone form. Average, open, upright growth. E-M. (U.S. 1965 - Mrs. R. A. Bell, Cairo, GA).

name: Rosemarie Ostberg
description: Very Dark Pink with Gold anthers and White filaments. Medium, semidouble to anemone. Average, upright growth. M. (U.S. 2013 - Gordy).

name: Rosemary Kinzer Variegated
description: Pale Pink blotched White variation of 'Rosemary Kinser'.

name: Rosemary Mcgeoch
description: Deep Pink, occasionally shading to Blush White. Large, single.

name: Rosenlee
description: White with tiny Pink markings on some of the petals. Large, formal double. Vigorous, compact, upright growth. L. (U.S. 1955 - L. Smith, Altadena, CA).

name: Rosie O'Grady Variegated
description: Deep Pink heavily moired White variation of 'Rosie O'Grady'. (U.S. 1968 - Haynie).

name: Rosina Sobeck
description: Light Pink, slightly darker at margins, with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Medium, semidouble - cup-shaped with loosely arranged stamens and occasional small twisted central petals. Average, spreading growth. M-L. (U.S. 1967 - John E. Sobeck, California).

name: Rosularis
description: White blotched Rose Pink. Large, peony form. (Reported to be the same as - 'Marchioness of Exeter Variegated'). (U.S. 1941 - Mcllhenny).

name: Rouge Baiser
description: Color: Bright red with yellow anthers and white filaments. Form: Rose form double. Size: Medium. Bloom Season: M. Growth Rate: Slow. Growth Characteristics: Upright growth. Leaf Characteristics: Dark green. Originator: Roger SalaÃ¼n. Place of Origination: France. Year of Registration / Origination: First bloomed in 2009. Heritage: Chance seedling.

name: Rouge Variegated
description: Rose Red and White form of 'Rouge'.

name: Round To It
description: White with occasional Red streaks with Gold anthers and Cream filaments. Large, semidouble form. Slow, upright, open growth. E-M. (Seedling of Japonica 'Snow Lady'). (U.S. 2021 - Pat Johnson, Cairo, GA).

name: Rouse's Big Red
description: Red (RHS 46B) with Yellow anthers and White with Red base filaments. Large to very large, semidouble. Average, upright, spreading, open growth. L. (U.S. 2013 - Dennis Rouse, Auburn, AL).

name: Rowena Craig
description: White with one petal having Carmine stripe or flecks. Large, single of tulip shape opening to cup shape. Vigorous, open, upright growth. E-M. (Aus. 1983 - E. Craig, Warrawee, NSW).

name: Rox Cowley
description: White to Cream suffused with Pink specks or small blotches. Medium, full peony form. Vigorous, compact growth. M. (U.S. 1952 - Wilkinson).

name: Roxanne
description: White. Large, peony form with heavy China-like petals and Blonde stamens. Average, compact growth. M. (U.S. 1953 - Pierates Cruz Gardens, Mt. Pleasant, SC).

name: Royal Trumpeteer
description: White opening from Blush Pink bud. Medium, semidouble with center three petals forming a trumpet. Average, compact growth. M-L. (U.S. 1955 - Marshall).

name: Royal Trumpeter
description: Color: White opening from blush pink bud. Form: Semidouble with center three petals forming a trumpet with 16 petals. Size: Medium. Bloom Season: M-L. Growth Rate: Average. Growth Characteristics: Compact growth. Leaf Characteristics: Dark green. Originator: Marshall's Camellia Nursery. Place of Origination: U.S. - San Gabriel, California. Year of Registration / Origination: 1955. Heritage: Seedling of 'Magnoliaeflora' (Hagoromo).

name: Royal Velvet Variegated
description: Dark Velvet Red variegated White variation of 'Royal Velvet'.

name: Royal White
description: White. Medium, semidouble, rose form double to formal double. Spreading growth. L. (U.S. 1944 - Overlook).

name: Royalty Variegated
description: Bright Pink and White form of 'Royalty'. (U.S. 1974).

name: Royston
description: Off White. Large, rose form double. Compact, bushy growth. E-M. (Aus. 2007 - Roy H. Campbell, Maddington).

name: Rubens
description: Color: Deep rose with white stripes. Form: Formal double. Size: Miniature. Bloom Season: L. Growth Rate: Vigorous. Growth Characteristics: Compact growth. Leaf Characteristics: Dark green. Place of Origination: Belgium. Year of Registration / Origination: 1845-1846.

name: Rubescens Major Variegated
description: ('Princess Anne').- Red and White variation of 'Rubescens Major'). (Aus. 1962).

name: Ruby Anniversary
description: Ruby Red with darker veining and some White stripes. Medium to large, formal double. M-L. (N.Z. 1997 - R. Young).

name: Ruby Davidson
description: White center, with Blush edges with deep Yellow anthers. Miniature to small, semidouble form. Average, spreading growth.

name: Ruby Irene
description: Dark Red with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Large, semidouble. Average, upright, dense growth. M. (U.S. 2019 - Gordy).

name: Ruby Lips
description: Ruby Red with White Picotee border with Gold anthers and Cream filaments. Medium, single form. Average, upright, open growth. M-L. (U.S. 2021 - Pat Johnson, Cairo, GA).

name: Ruchi Rhodes
description: Bright Red and White with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Large, semidouble. Vigorous, upright, dense growth. M. (U.S. 2013 - Howard and Mary Rhodes, Tallahassee, FL).

name: Rudolph Variegated
description: Deep Red mottled White variation of 'Rudolph'. (U.S. 1995 - Nuccio's).

name: Ruffian
description: White with Yellowish tinge. Large, semidouble with irregular petals to peony form. Average, compact, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 1978 - Homeyer).

name: Ruffled Princess Variegated
description: Pink blotched White variation of 'Ruffled Princess.'.

name: Ruffles
description: White. Large, loose peony form with undulated petals. Vigorous, bushy growth. M. (U.S. 1960 - Marshall).

name: Rulona Holland
description: White. Large, semidouble to loose peony form. Vigorous, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 1975 - Haynie).

name: Rupie
description: White with stripes or spots of Dark Pink sometimes producing a flower Pink on one half and White on the other half with Gold anthers and Yellow filaments. Medium to large, semidouble to loose peony. Average, upright growth. M. (U.S. 2013 - Rupert E. Drews, Charleston, SC).

name: Rupie's Charleston
description: Pink variegated White. M. (Sport of Japonica 'Miss Charleston Variegated'). (U.S. 2004 - Rupert E. Drews, Charleston, SC).

name: Russian Snow
description: White with small, Red flecks. Large, formal double. (U.S. 1952 - Carleton).

name: Russian White
description: White with Cream center. Large, peony form. Average, compact, upright growth. M. (N.Z. 1973 - Mrs. I. Berg, Whakatane).

name: Ruth Anderson
description: White. Large, semidouble with alternate rows of sweet pea shaped petals forming flared cup-shaped bloom with loose center of White stamens and petaloids. Vigorous, open, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1964 - Julington).

name: Ruth Brooks
description: Rose with Snowy White center. Medium, formal double. M. (U.S. 1973 - Haynie).

name: Ruth C. McNAIR
description: White with bright Yellow anthers with White filaments in the middle. Large, peony form having 3 to 4 rows of large, broad, layered outer petals which are slightly down curved; inner petals are upward and fluted and sometimes crinkled and irregular, mixed with many petaloids. Average, spreading, dense growth. M-L. (U.S. 2009 - John M. Davy, Pace, FL).

name: Ruth Royer
description: ('Thelma Sanford'). Pink with various amounts of White variegation. Large, semidouble. (Sport of Japonica 'Duchess of Sutherland'). (U.S. 1949 - Flowerwood).

name: Ruth Seibels
description: White. Large, semidouble. Average, open, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1975 - Haynie).

name: Ruth Tinkle
description: Red with faint White streaks on innermost petals and White anthers. Medium, semidouble to rose form double. Average, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 2010 - Oscar Tinkle, Portland, OR).

name: Ruth Vaughn
description: Porcelain White. Large, peony form to rose form double. Vigorous, open, willowy, upright growth. E-L. (U.S. 1965 - Dr. J. W. Vaughn, Lakeland, FL).

name: Ruthana Sellers
description: Light Red with White variegation. Medium, formal double form with incurved petals. Average, upright growth. M-L. (Sport of Japonica 'Miss Margaret') (U.S. 2020 - Thomas Sellers, Bolivia, NC).

name: Ruthie Pope
description: Bright Pink and White. Large, loose peony form. Average, open growth. E-M. (U.S. 1981 - C. R. Pope, Lafayette, LA).

name: Ryan Gainey
description: Pale to Blush Pink on the center petals progressing to Deep Pink on the outer petals with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Large, semidouble; the bloom has pointed petals in the center and outer petals are notched. Average, upright growth. M-L. (Japonica 'Magnoliaeflora' seedling). (U.S. 2014 - Ryan Gainey, Decatur, GA).

name: Sabiniana
description: Color: White with rare, red stripes. Form: Loose peony form; outer 4-5 rows of petals are close, notched, rose form; those of the interior, irregular. Size: Large. Leaf Characteristics: Dark green. Originator: D .J. Parks. Place of Origination: China - Canton. Year of Registration / Origination: ~1824.

name: Sabrina
description: Light Coral Pink with few Red stripes and each petal bordered White. Medium, formal double. M-L. (U.S. 1980 - Gentry).

name: Sacco
description: ('Sacco Nova'; 'Sacco Vera'). Rose Pink to Rose Pink spotted White to deep Red. Medium, formal double. Slow growth. E. (Spelled 'Saccoi' in certain old literature). (Italy 1851 - Sacco, Milan).

name: Sadaharu Oh
description: White to Blush with Pink veining and Red highlights with Golden anthers and White filaments. Medium to large, rose form to anemone to peony form. Vigorous, upright, semi-weeping growth. E-M. ('Seedling of Japonica 'Tama-no-ura'; named for Japanese baseball hero known as "The Babe Ruth of Japan"). (U.S. 2004 - Bobby Green, Green Nurseries, Fairhope, AL).

name: Sadie Mancill Dawn
description: Deep soft Pink veined Rose and edged White. (Sport of Japonica 'Sadie Mancill'). (U.S. 1964 - K. G. Durio, Jr., Opelousas, LA).

name: Sainty's Special
description: White. Large, peony form. M. (Aus. 1962 - Pymble, NSW).

name: Sajin Baozhu
description: Gao & Zhuang, 1989, The Camellia in China, p.53, No.343, colour photo No.201: Small size, 7-8 cm across, anemone form, white with red streaks, 5 large guard petals and a compact centre of white petaloids and golden stamens. Leaves dark green, ovate to broad-ovate, apices acute. Originated in China. Camellias, Y.C. Shen, 2009, p.188, colour photo.

name: Sakuraba-Tsubaki
description: Color: Pale pink with a few pale red markings sometimes sporting pale pink with gold anthers and white filaments. Form: Semidouble with foliage resembling that of a cherry. Size: Large. Bloom Season: M. Leaf Characteristics: Dark green. Place of Origination: Japan. Year of Registration / Origination: 1867. Other Information: Known in the U.S. as 'Cherry Blossom'.

name: Sally Arnold
description: White with Pink stripes and markings with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Very large, semidouble form. Average, upright, open growth. L. (U.S. 2021 - Don Bergamini, Martinez, CA).

name: Sally Harrell
description: White. Medium, full peony form. Compact growth. E-M. (U.S. 1956 - E. H. Harrell, Thomasville, GA).

name: Sally Jane
description: White shading to Blush Shell Pink.

name: Sam Barranco
description: White dashed clear Pink. Small, formal double. Slow growth. E. (U.S. 1952 - S. Barranco, Beaumont, TX).

name: Sam Barranco Pink
description: Deep Pink sometimes flecked White. (Sport of Japonica 'Sam Barranco'). (U.S. 1959 - F. Barranco, Beaumont, TX).

name: Sam Harn
description: Cherry Red with White stamens. Medium, anemone form. Compact, upright growth. E-M. (U.S. 1956 - Julington).

name: Sam Wellborn
description: Medium Pink with Golden anthers and White filaments. Very large, rose form double to semidouble. Vigorous, upright, dense growth. E-L. (Seedling of Reticulata hybrid 'Frank Houser'). (U.S. 2018 - James Smelley, Moss Point, Mississippi).

name: Sam Zerkowsky
