name: Small Slam
description: Red with Red and White petaloids. Miniature to small, anemone form. Average, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1999 - Northern California Camellia Research Committee, Martinez, CA).

name: Snippet
description: Soft Pink to almost White center petals and Light Pink outer petals. Small, semidouble with long, narrow, notched petals. Dwarf, compact growth. M. (Pitardii seedling). (N.Z. 1971 - Mrs. A. B. Durrant, Rotorua).

name: Snooty Beauty
description: Glowing Rose Pink. Large, semidouble with White tipped petaloids intermixed with Golden stamens. (U.S. 1961 - H. Mura, Augusta, GA).

name: Snow
description: See 'Mine-No-Yuki'.

name: Snow Baby
description: White. Miniature, anemone form. Average, compact, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1965 - McCaskill).

name: Snow Bell
description: White with Dark Yellow anthers and Creamy filaments. Large, semidouble with petals firm, broad-ovate, inner crêped, apices notched, sometimes fluted. Average, spreading dense growth. M. (Japan to U.S. [Huntington Gardens]).

name: Snow Chan
description: ('White Elegans').- Pure White. Large to very large, anemone form. (Sport of Japonica 'Shiro Chan'). (U.S. 1957 - Nuccio's).

name: Snow Cloud
description: White. Large, semidouble to loose peony form with fluted petals. Average, compact growth. M. (U.S. 1953 - Jones).

name: Snow Cone
description: White. Medium, formal double with wide, heavy petals. Bushy, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1973 - T. E. Croson, Paso Robles, CA).

name: Snow Doll
description: See 'Pax'.

name: Snow Drop
description: White edged Pink. Miniature, single. Open, upright growth. E-L. (Pitardii x Fraterna). (Aus. 1979 - Sebire).

name: Snow Drop Cascade
description: White shading to Pink on petal edges. Miniature, single to semidouble. Spreading growth. E-M. (Hybrid 'Snow Drop' seedling). (Aus. 2005 - Steve Campbell).

name: Snow Fairy
description: White. Small, formal double to rose form double. Average, compact growth. M. (U.S. 1963 - McCaskill).

name: Snow Flame
description: White. Medium to large, formal double. Average, compact, upright growth. L. (U.S. 1963 - Short).

name: Snow Flurry
description: White. Small, anemone form. Slow, spreading growth. E. Cold hardy to -10°F. (Oleifera 'Plain Jane' × Hybrid 'Frost Princess'). (U.S. 1986 - Ackerman).

name: Snow Lady
description: Pure White. Large, loose peony form to rose form double. Vigorous, open, upright growth. E-L. Cold hardy. (U.S. 1995 - A. Chastain, Thomasville, GA).

name: Snow Maiden
description: White. Small, semidouble. Vigorous, upright, open growth. M. (U.S. 1937 - Coolidge).

name: Snow Mitt
description: White. Large, formal double. Compact, upright growth. M-L. (Aus. 1979 - J. G. Baxter, Dorchester East, Vic.).

name: Snow Nymph
description: White. Small, full peony form, occasionally showing stamens. Average, compact growth. E-M. (U.S. 1958 - Mrs. W. E. Milligan, Thomasville, GA).

name: Snow Palace
description: White. Large, peony form to anemone form with large, undulated outer guard petals. Average, compact growth. M. (U.S. 1958 - Short).

name: Snow Princess
description: White. Medium, formal double. Vigorous, compact, upright growth. L. (U.S. 1953 - Shackelford).

name: Snow Swirl
description: Pure White. Medium to large, formal double with lightly ruffled petals at times. (U.S. 2005 - Gordy).

name: Snow Tulip
description: White. Medium, single to semidouble. Vigorous, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1945 - Carter).

name: Snow White (Rosedale)
description: See 'Thomas D'. Pitts'.

name: Snow White (Star)
description: White. Large, semidouble with central mass of Golden stamens and occasional petaloids. M. (U.S. 1959 - Star).

name: Snowdrift
description: White with Gold anthers and White filaments. Medium, semidouble. Slow, upright growth. M. (Japan to U.S. 1932 - Toichi Domoto, Hayward, CA).

name: Snowfall
description: White. Small, single.

name: Snowflake
description: Color: White with golden anthers. Form: Single. Size: M. Place of Origination: U.K.. Year of Registration / Origination: Early 1900's.

name: Snowflake (Sawada)
description: White. Medium, single

name: Snowie
description: White with some flowers Pale Blush. Miniature, rose form double. Average, compact, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1971 - M. A. and N. Cox, Georgetown, SC).

name: Snowman
description: White. Large, semidouble with curled and twisted inner petals and notched and incurved outer petals. Vigorous, spreading, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1964 - W. Stewart, Savannah, GA).

name: Snowmist
description: White. Miniature, single. Slow, dense growth. E-M. (U.S. 1997 - Domoto).

name: Snowstorm
description: White. Miniature, single with notched petals. Vigorous, spreading, weeping growth. E-M. (Hybrid 'Snow Drop' seedling). (Aus. 1994 - A. Raper, The Patch, Vic.).

name: Snowy Egrets
description: White. Medium to large, semidouble with long, narrow, twisted, fluted petals, a loose bunch of stamens, and very narrow petaloids. Average, pendulous growth. M-L. (U.S. 1972 - Witman).

name: Solitaire
description: White. Medium, formal double. Average, bushy growth. M-L. (U.S. 1953 - Short).

name: Sophie Ducker
description: Pink, shading to White at base of petals with Pale Pink petaloids at center. Miniature, anemone form. Slow, open growth. E. (Hybrid 'Our Melissa' seedling). (Aus. 1998 - Dr. R. Withers, Donvale, Vic.).

name: South Georgia Ballet
description: Light Apricot-Pink shading to Creamy White center with Yellow anthers and Creamy White filaments. Very large, semidouble to anemone to loose peony form. Average, upright, open growth. E-M. (Seedling of Japonica 'Omega'). (U.S. 2021 - Pat B. Johnson, Cairo, GA).

name: Southern Charm
description: ('Queen Juliana').- White. Large, semidouble. Average, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1955 - Fisher).

name: Southern Snowball
description: White. Medium, anemone (with 12 guard petals) to full peony form. Vigorous, dense, upright growth. E-M. (U.S. 2012 - Jill Read, Lucedale, MS).

name: Southern Temptation
description: White with Cream Yellow center bud. Medium to large, formal double. Vigorous, upright, dense growth. M-L. (U.S. 2016 - Jill Read, Lucedale, MS).

name: Special Delivery
description: White. Large, semidouble with wide, sweeping Golden stamens interspersed with clusters of petaloids and upright center petals. Vigorous, open, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 1962 - D. B. Brockman, Greer, SC).

name: Spencer Sargent
description: White with Pale Pink towards center with Yellow filaments. Small to Medium, anemone form. Slow, bushy, open growth. M-L. (U.S. 2021 - John L. Spencer, Lakeland, FL).

name: Spindletop Centennial
description: White. Small, formal double; fully imbricated petals, with very small petaloids at center. Average, upright, 1 minute left in chapter dense growth. E-M. [Japonica 'Yukibotan' x (Japonica 'Elizabeth Boardman' x Japonica 'Mary Lilla Dumas')]. (U.S. 1999 - H. R. Norsworthy, Beaumont, TX).

name: Spindrift
description: White with Light Pink stripes. Miniature, semidouble. Average, dense growth. M-L. (U.S. 1992 - H. Hall).

name: Spink
description: Rose Pink. Small, single. Open, upright growth. E-M. (Hybrid 'Snow Drop' seedling). (Aus. 1986 - Sebire).

name: Splash
description: Red splashes on White with Pink undertones with speckled Red dots as if it was sprayed with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Large to very large, semidouble form. Average, spreading, open growth. E. (U.S. 2021 - Howard Rhodes, Tallahassee, FL).

name: Splash Of Pink
description: White with splash of Pink. Medium, peony form. Average, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1977 - Kramer).

name: Splash-O-White
description: Red splotched White on several petals. Small to medium, semidouble. Slow growth. E-L. (U.S. 1960 - E. Pieri, San Gabriel, CA).

name: Sporting Class
description: White with flecks, streaks and petals of Dark Coral Pink with White Yellow tinted anthers and Yellow filaments. Medium, peony form. Average, upright, spreading, dense growth. E. (U.S. 2008 - John M. Davy, Pace, FL).

name: Spring Call
description: White. Large, formal double. Vigorous, open, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1953 - Short).

name: Spring Mist
description: Blush Pink. Miniature, semidouble. Vigorous, spreading growth. E-M. Fragrant. (Japonica Snow Bell' x Lutchuensis). (U.S. 1982 - A. E. Longley and Parks).

name: Spring Wind
description: White tinged Pink. Miniature, single. Average, open, spreading growth. M. (Japonica x Lutchuensis). (U.S. 1983 - Ackerman).

name: Squadron Leader Astin
description: White. Miniature, semidouble. Vigorous, dense, upright growth. L. Cold hardy. (U.S. 1997 - P. Astin, Carrollton, GA).

name: St. Ives
description: White dotted and spotted Carmine. Large, loose peony form. M. (Aus. 1962 - Waterhouse).

name: Stan Jones
description: Pale Pink edged Deep Rose Pink. Medium, semidouble. STAR ABOVE STAR - Formerly Vernalis - White shading to Lavender Pink at edge. Medium, semidouble. STARMAN - Formerly Vernalis - White with Dark Pink edges with Golden anthers and White filament. Large, semidouble. Average to vigorous, upright, spreading growth. E-L. (Seedling of Sasanqua 'Star Above Star'). (U.S. 2021 - Bobby Green, Green Nurseries, Fairhope, AL).

name: Stangate Ruby
description: Dark Red with darker veining and White markings. Small, formal double. Vigorous, compact growth. M-L. (Aus. 1996 - Aldgate, S. Aus.).

name: Star Above Star
description: Color: White center shading to lavender pink at edge with yellow stamens. Form: Semidouble in the form of one star superimposed on another star. Size: Medium. Bloom Season: M. Growth Rate: Vigorous. Growth Characteristics: Upright, bushy growth. Originator: McCaskill Gardens. Place of Origination: U.S. - Pasadena, California. Year of Registration / Origination: 1964. Heritage: Chance seedling.

name: Stars 'n Stripes
description: White striped Rose Red, often with Rose Red border. Medium, single. Average, upright, spreading growth. E-M. (Hybrid 'Christmas Rose' seedling). (U.S. 1999 - Nuccio's).

name: Stars And Bars
description: Blood Red with Cream White bar down center of each petal and tipped White. Miniature, formal double with petals pinched at top. Slow, open, willowy growth. M. (U.S. 1963 - Mrs. J. V. Knapp, Tallahassee, FL).

name: Stella Sewell
description: Color: White with creamy overcast with golden anthers and light yellow filaments. Form: Semidouble to loose peony form. Size: Large. Bloom Season: M. Growth Rate: Average. Growth Characteristics: Compact growth. Leaf Characteristics: Light green. Originator: Julington Nurseries. Place of Origination: U.S. - Jacksonville, Florida. Year of Registration / Origination: 1954 - ACS Registration #214 - first bloomed in 1953.

name: Stephanie
description: White with Cream center. Medium, formal double. E-M. (U.S. 1954 - Mrs. F. W. Fitzpatrick, San Diego, CA).

name: Stephanie Cassagne Grimm
description: Mauve Pink with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Medium, irregular semidouble form with interspersed stamens. Slow, upright, wispy growth. M. (U.S. 2019 - R. J. Despeaux, Covington, LA).

name: Stephanie Cassagne Grimm Variegated
description: Mauve Pink blotched White with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Medium, irregular semidouble form with interspersed stamens. Slow, upright, wispy growth. M. (U.S. 2019 - Camellia Heaven - John Grimm, Bush, LA).

name: Stephanie Stanley
description: Dark Pink with White spots. Large to very large, semidouble with ruffled petals. Vigorous, open, spreading growth. M. (U.S. 1995 - E. Atkins, Shalimar, FL).

name: Stephanie's Super Nova
description: White with Pink edges with Golden anthers and White filaments. Large, single. Average, bushy growth. E. (U.S. 2019 - Bobby Green, Green Nurseries, Fairhope, AL).

name: Stephanie's Tiny Star
description: Red with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Miniature, single. Average, bushy growth. E. (U.S. 2019 - Bobby Green, Green Nurseries, Fairhope, AL).

name: Stephen Louis Cassagne
description: Pink shading to White on edges. Small, formal double. Average, bushy growth. (U.S. 2019 - Camellia Heaven - John Grimm, Bush, LA).

name: Steve Blount Variegated
description: Color: Light red blotched white with yellow anthers and white filaments. Form: Semidouble. Size: Large. Bloom Season: E-M. Growth Rate: Average. Growth Characteristics: Spreading, upright growth. Leaf Characteristics: Dark green. Place of Origination: U.S.. Year of Registration / Origination: 1983. Heritage: Virus variegated form of Japonica 'Steve Blount'.

name: Stevie Cockrell
description: White heavily flecked and dashed bright Red. Small, semidouble with rounded petals, small White stamens and a few small petaloids. Average, compact growth. M. (U.S. 1958 - Julington).

name: Stewart's White Supreme
description: White. Large, semidouble to loose peony form with upright petals. Vigorous, compact, upright growth. E-M. (U.S. 1960 - W. Stewart, Savannah, GA).

name: Still Hope
description: White with Pink cast. Miniature, semidouble to anemone form. Average, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 1961 - Metcalf).

name: Stop!
description: Red with White petaloids with Gold anthers and White filaments. Large, anemone form. Vigorous, upright, open growth. M-L. (U.S. 2018 - Nuccio's).

name: Strawberry Moon
description: Medium Pink and White with White anthers and White filaments. Small, semidouble to peony form. Vigorous, upright growth. E. (Seedling of Hybrid 'Yume'). (U.S. 2016 - Tsubaki Camellias, Savannah, GA).

name: Strawberry Pizzazz
description: Vivid Magenta and White markings. Miniature to small, formal double form. Vigorous, upright, dense growth. E- M. Cold hardy to 14° F. (U.S. 2022 - Glenn & Jill Read, Lucedale, MS). SUNBEAM - Formerly Vernalis - Rose Pink with lighter tones on the petals with Yellow anthers and Yellow filaments. Large, semidouble. Low, spreading growth . (U.S. 2010 - Nuccio's).

name: Strawberry Soda
description: Light Pink and White. Large, anemone form. Vigorous, upright growth. E. (U.S. 1966 - A. Leger, Handsboro, MS).

name: Strawberry Swirl
description: Soft Pink margined White with occasional Rose Stripe. Medium to large, peony form. Average, upright growth. E-M. (N.Z. 1989 - Jury).

name: Strawberry Vanilla
description: White with Strawberry Pink stripes with Yellow anthers and Creamy Yellow filaments. Medium to large, semidouble. Average, upright, open growth. E-L. (U.S. 2018 - Pat Johnson, Cairo, GA).

name: Streamliner
description: White, finely striped Deep Pink. Large, semidouble. Vigorous, upright growth. L. (U.S. 1959 - Short).

name: Sudie Blanchard
description: Chalk White. Large, semidouble. Average, compact growth. M. (U.S. 1989 - J. K. Blanchard, Wallace, NC).

name: Sue Green
description: White shading to Pink. Medium to large, anemone form. Vigorous, upright growth. E-M. (U.S. 1997 - Habel).

name: Sue Kendall Variegated
description: Medium Pink variegated White variation of 'Sue Kendall.'.

name: Sukiya
description: Pinkish White. Small, single. Vigorous, open growth. E- M.

name: Sulphurous
description: Rosy Salmon Pink edged Darker Pink with occasional White stripe. Medium, formal double. Bushy growth. M-L. (Aus. 1880 - Shepherd).

name: Summer Pearl
description: Very Light Pink with Red or Pink stripes with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Small to medium, semidouble to loose peony to rose form double. Vigorous, upright, spreading growth. E-L. (U.S. 2013 - James and Elaine Smelley).

name: Sun Dial
description: Creamy White at base to Flesh Pink striped Red. Medium, anemone form. Slow, compact growth. M. (U.S. 1950 - Short).

name: Sun Kissed
description: Soft Pink with two outer petals and one center petal marked White. Medium, anemone form. M-L. (U.S. 1965 - Mrs. M. M. Hitchcock, Little Rock, AR).

name: Sun-Up Variegated
description: Coral Pink blotched White variation of 'Sun-Up'.

name: Sundae
description: Crimson with mass of Creamy White petaloids overlaid with Light Carmine. Miniature, anemone form. Average, upright growth. M. (N.Z. 1989 - Jury).

name: Sunny Jim
description: White with peppermint candy stripes. Large, semidouble with irregular stamens. (U.S. 1953 - Fruitland).

name: Sunny Side
description: White at center blending to Pink at edge. Small to medium, single. Average, compact, upright growth. E-M. (U.S. 1990 - Nuccio's).

name: Sunnyvale Carnival
description: Bicolor, White and Pink with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Medium, loose peony to semidouble form with a globular shape, especially if grown in shade. Vigorous, upright, spreading growth. M-L. Fragrant. (Sasanqua 'Kanjiro ?' × Sasanqua 'Bert Jones'). (U.S. 2020 - Yuri Panchul, Sunnyvale, CA).

name: Sunrise Serenade
description: Light Pink shading near White at the center. Medium, loose peony. Fragrant.

name: Sunset Glory Variegated
description: Coral Pink blotched White variation of 'Sunset Glory'.

name: Super Star
description: White. Large, semidouble with White stamens tipped Gold. Slow to average growth. E-L. (Saluenensis x Japonica). (N.Z. 1984 - Mrs. J. E. Shaw, Wairua).

name: Surusumi
description: ('Starlight'). White. Large, single. Average, compact growth. M. (Name of famous horse in old literature). (Japan to U.S. [Rhodellia] 1945).

name: Survivor (Parks)
description: White. Large, single. Upright, compact growth. Cold hardy to - 9°F. (Oleifera 'Narumi-Gata x Oleifera'). (U.S. 1988 - Parks).

name: Susan Carter
description: See 'Frizzle White'.

name: Susan Grooms
description: Blush Pink with highlights of Dark Pink random stripes on a few of the petals; all petals have tiny Pink pixels which provide the overall Light Pink color of the flower with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Medium to large, loose peony form; the bloom has petaloids varying from typical small size to approximately half the size of the primary petals. Average, upright, medium density growth. M-L. (U.S. 2021 - Gordy).

name: Susan Johnson
description: White. Large, semidouble. Bushy, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1975 - Dr. G. R. Johnson, Carthage, TX).

name: Susan Page Skinner
description: White to Light Blush Pink marked Rose. Medium, semidouble with round petals. Vigorous, compact, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1964 - Julington).

name: Susan Pilgrim
description: Deep Rose Pink with a few White stripes. Small, anemone form. L. Cold hardy. (U.S. 1995 - Dr. C. Minarik. West Harwich, MA).

name: Susanna Beard
description: White with Pink outer petal edges. Large to very large, peony form. Vigorous, dense, upright growth. E-M. (U.S. 1996 - Jernigan).

name: Susie Q. Variegated
description: Deep Pink blotched White variation of Susie Q.

name: Suzanne Withers
description: White deepening to Orchid Pink from center to edge. Large, semidouble to peony form with crepe petals. Open, upright growth. M. (Reticulata seedling). (Aus. 1979 - E. D. Kettle, Cheltenham, Vic.).

name: Suzie
description: White. Miniature, semidouble of cupped form with White petaloids in center. Average, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 1958 - J. Audrey, San Fernando, CA).

name: Suzy Edge
description: Soft Coral Pink with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Medium to large, semidouble form. Vigorous, upright, open growth. M-L. (Seedling of Japonica 'Beatrix Hoyt'). (U.S. 2021 - Pat B. Johnson, Cairo, GA).

name: Swamp Buggy
description: White with Pink stripes with Golden anthers and White filaments. Large, semidouble. Average, upright growth. M. (U.S. 2017 - Gilley).

name: Swan Lake
description: Snow White. Medium, semidouble of hose-in-hose form. Bushy growth. M-L. (Name subsequently used for Japonica cultivar by a commercial nursery). (Aus. 1968 - Tuckfield).

name: Swan Lake (Monrovia)
description: White. Large, loose peony form to rose form double to formal double. Vigorous, compact, upright growth. M. (Name previously used for a non-reticulata Hybrid cultivar). (U.S. 1971 - Monrovia Nsy., Monrovia, CA).

name: Sweet Auburn
description: RHSCC 66D, moderate Purplish Pink, with Yellow anthers and Yellow-White filaments. Very large, semidouble. Vigorous, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 2014 - Unknown origin, Auburn, AL).

name: Sweet Bonair
description: Cream White. Medium, semidouble. Vigorous, upright growth. M. Fragrant. (U.S. 1952 - Goletto).

name: Sweet Emma
description: White with outer petals flushed Pink. Medium to large, anemone form. E. Fragrant. (Japonica "Tamaikari' × Japonica Mrs. Bertha A. Harms' × Hybrid 'Salab'). (N.Z. 1996 - J. Finlay).

name: Sweet October
description: White with Golden Yellow anthers and Cream filaments. Small to medium, single. Vigorous, upright growth. E. (Oleifera 'Lu Shan Snow' x Sasanqua 'White Queen'). (U.S. 2009 - Ackerman).

name: Sweet Scentsation
description: Dark Orchid Pink edges fading to Blush Pink with White overtones with a center of a mixture of Dark Orchid Pink 1 minute left in chanter and Blush Pink with White overtones with Golden anthers and Golden filaments. Large, loose peony to semidouble with upright petals. Vigorous, upright, open growth. E-M. Very fragrant. (U.S. 2016 - Jill Read, Lucedale, MS).

name: Sweetheart
description: Soft Apricot Pink, occasionally marbled White. Medium, rose form double. (Sport of Japonica 'Bleichroeder Pink'). (U.S. 1958 - Arnesen).

name: Sweetheart (Huntington)
description: Color: Soft apricot pink, occasionally marbled white. Form: Formal double and occasional semidouble form. Size: Medium. Bloom Season: M. Growth Rate: Slow. Growth Characteristics: Compact growth. Originator: Edward B. Arnesen, Valley Garden Supply Co.. Place of Origination: U.S. - North Hollywood, California. Year of Registration / Origination: 1958. Heritage: Sport of  Japonica 'Bleichroeder Pink'.

name: Sweetii Vera
description: ('Lewellyn Variegated'). White and pale Pink. Medium to large, peony form. Vigorous, upright, open growth. M. (England 1832 - Sweet).

name: Swirling Cloud
description: White. Large, semidouble with loose petals. Average, compact growth. M. (U.S. 1955 - Feathers).

name: Sylvia
description: Color: Carmina red flecked white with golden anthers and white filaments. Form: Semidouble; corolla of 9-12 petals disposed alternatively in 3-4 rows, regular, rounded, opening nearly flat. Size: Medium. Bloom Season: M. Growth Rate: Vigorous. Growth Characteristics: Compact, upright growth. Leaf Characteristics: Dark green. Place of Origination: Italy. Year of Registration / Origination: 1841. Heritage: Variegated from of Japonica 'Kimberley'.

name: Symphonette Variegated
description: Color: Bright red blotched white with yellow anthers and white filaments. Form: Semidouble. Size: Medium. Bloom Season: M. Growth Rate: Vigorous. Growth Characteristics: Upright growth. Leaf Characteristics: Dark green. Place of Origination: U.S.. Year of Registration / Origination: 1963. Heritage: Virus variegated form of Japonica 'Symphonette'.

name: Sōshin-wabisuke
description: from Saitama Pref. Published as the front cover of Japan Camellia, No.78, August 2005: A natural seedling of Shimofusa-wabisuke, clearly a hybrid with Tama-no-ura, growing next to the mother tree. Bloomed first in 1995, miniature, red widely bordered white, single. White filaments with degenerated anthers, slightly hairy ovary. Profuse blooming from late autumn to mid-season. Leaves waved like those of Tama-no-ura. Tree vigorous. Originated by Sōshin Hirai and named for himself.

name: T. C. Patin Variegated
description: Light Red blotched and marked White variation of 'T. C. Patin'. (U.S. 1974 - T. C. Patin, Hammond, LA).

name: T. K. Variegated
description: Color: Light pink edged darker pink blotched white with yellow stamens. Form: Semidouble. Size: Medium. Bloom Season: M. Growth Rate: Vigorous. Growth Characteristics: Compact growth. Leaf Characteristics: Dull green. Originator: T. Kiyono. Place of Origination: U.S. - Crichton, Alabama. Year of Registration / Origination: 1937.

name: T. Morris
description: Medium Pink with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Large, semidouble to rose form double. Vigorous, upright, medium density growth. M. (Seedling of Reticulata hybrid 'Nuccio's Ruby). (U.S. 2023 - Jim Smelley, Moss Point, MS).

name: T. P. Bowen
description: White with Blush Pink iridescent undertone. Miniature, formal double. Bushy growth. M. (U.S. 1977 - Haynie).

name: T. S. Clower, Jr.
description: White with narrow Pink stripe. Medium, formal double. Vigorous, slender, open growth. M. (U.S. 1950 - Clower).

name: Tabbs
description: Crimson marbled and blotched White. Medium to large, full peony form. (Sport of Japonica 'Helenor'). (Aus. 1856 - Guilfoyle).

name: Tabitha
description: Light Pink flushed Rose; sports White streaked Deep Rose or Red, Fuchsia, and Fuchsia with one White petal. Large, semidouble. Spreading, upright growth. L. (U.S. 1963 - Mrs. K. H. McEachern, Wilmington, NC).

name: Tafuku-Benten (Happy Goddess)
description: ('Benten-tsubaki'; 'Otafuku-benten'). Pink blotched and edged White. Medium, single. Vigorous, spreading growth. M. (Japan 1956 - E. Satomi, Tokyo).

name: Tai-shuhai
description: Color: White shading to deep red  on edge with yellow anthers and light yellowish filaments. Form: Single. Size: Medium to large. Bloom Season: E. Leaf Characteristics: Dark green. Originator: Shunsuke Hisatomi. Place of Origination: Japan - Fukuoka Prefecture. Year of Registration / Origination: 1960's. Other Information: Means.

name: Taishuhai
description: White shading to Deep Red on edge. Medium to large, single.

name: Taiyo (The Sun)
description: Claret Rose with Yellow anthers and Creamy filaments. Small, single. Slow, upright growth; bloom has broad, overlapping petals, emarginate and slightly creped, with a compact stamen column; this snow Camellia cultivar has Light Yellow variegation along the midrib and the major veins of the Dark Green, glossy leaves. M-L. (Japan 1966 - Niigata Prefecture).

name: Taiyô (The Sun)
description: Claret Rose with Yellow anthers and Creamy filaments. Small, single. Slow, upright growth; bloom has broad, overlapping petals, emarginate and slightly creped, with a compact stamen column; this snow Camellia cultivar has Light Yellow variegation along the midrib and the major veins of the Dark Green, glossy leaves. M-L. (Japan 1966 - Niigata Prefecture).

name: Takane-No-Yuki
description: Soft Pink petals, veined Deeper Pink with wide, White center, with Gold anthers and White filaments. Medium, single with crinkled edges. (Japan 1931 - Tôbei Yoshida, Kiso River, Chûbu).

name: Takane-no-yuki (Snow on the High Peaks)
description: Color: Soft pink petals, veined deeper pink with wide, white center, with gold anthers and white filaments. Form: Single with crinkled edges. Size: Medium. Leaf Characteristics: Medium to dark green. Originator: TÃ´bei Yoshida. Place of Origination: Japan - Kiso River, ChÃ»bu. Year of Registration / Origination: 1931. Other Information: Means 'Snow on the High Peaks' in English - slightly fragrant.

name: Takara-Awase (Treasure Trove)
description: Pink shaded White and bordered Pink. Unknown size, semidouble.

name: Talmont
description: White. Large, formal double. Average, spreading, upright growth. E-M. (U.S. 1967 - Mrs. W. P. Bevis, Tallahassee, FL).

name: Tama Americana
description: Rose Red with broad White border. Medium, semidouble with occasional petaloids. Average, open, upright growth. E-M. (U.S. 1993 - Nuccio's).

name: Tama Anemone
description: Rose Red with White border, irregular but usually narrow with Golden anthers and White filaments. medium, anemone form. Average, compact, rounded growth. M-L. (Seedling of Japonica "Tama-no-ura'). (U.S. 2021 - Bobby Green, Green Nurseries, Fairhope, AL).

name: Tama Bambino
description: Rose Pink bordered White. Miniature to small, peony form with narrow, pointed petals. Average, upright growth. E-M. (U.S. 1993 - Nuccio's).

name: Tama Beauty
description: Rose Pink bordered White. Medium to large, loose peony form. Average, dense growth. E-M. (U.S. 1993 - Nuccio's).

name: Tama Bell
description: White with Red at base of petals. Miniature to small, bell-shaped single. Vigorous, loose, upright growth. E-M. (U.S. 1993 - Nuccio's).

name: Tama Carousel
description: Pink with a White border. Medium, semidouble with center petals that standup and occasional petaloids. Medium, upright, bushy growth. E-M. (Japonica "Tama-no-ura' × Japonica 'Nuccio's Carousel'). (U.S. 2014 - Bradford King, Arcadia, CA).

name: Tama Electra
description: Brilliant Dark Red bordered White. Small to medium, single. Vigorous, compact, upright growth. E-M. (U.S. 1993 - Nuccio's).

name: Tama Glitters
description: Red bordered White. Medium to large, semidouble to loose peony form. Average, spreading, upright growth. E-M. (U.S. 1993 - Nuccio's).

name: Tama Loch Laurel
description: Red with White border around petals with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Medium, loose peony to semidouble with petals in center of flower are narrow, similar to petaloids. Average, upright growth. M. (Japonica 'Tama Electra' seedling). (U.S. 2016 - Mark Crawford, Valdosta, GA).

name: Tama Peacock
description: Maroonish Red washing to a White border. Small to medium, semidouble form. Upright, pendulous growth. M-L. (U.S. 2000 - Nuccio's).

name: Tama Velvet
description: Dark Red with a White border of varying width with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Medium, semidouble. Average, upright, spreading growth. E-M. (Seedling of Japonica 'Tama-no-ura'). (U.S. 2018 - Nuccio's).

name: Tama Vino
description: Wine Red washing to broad White border. Small to medium, semidouble with long, narrow petals. Average, upright growth. E- M. (U.S. 1993 - Nuccio's).

name: Tama-No-Ura
description: Red bordered White. Small to medium, single. Vigorous, upright growth. M. (Japan 1973 to U.S. 1978 - Nuccio's).

name: Tamagasumi
description: Faint Blush Pink with slender flecks of Red with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Medium, single opening to a spherical/ cupped shape. Average, upright growth. M-L. (Seedling of Japonica 'Tar'an'). (Japan 1968 - Shun'ichi Sakuragi, Chûbu Area).

name: Tammia
description: White with Pink center and border. Miniature to small, formal double with incurved geometric petals. Average, compact, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 1971 - Tammia).

name: Tammia Fire Opal
description: White flecked and streaked radiant Scarlet. (Sport of Japonica 'Tammia'). (U.S. 1978 - J L Carvain, Dallas, TX).

name: Tammia Speckled
description: White with sunken center and border, with Red speckles. Miniature to small, formal double with incurved geometric petals. Average, compact, upright growth. M-L. (Sport of Japonica 'Tammia').

name: Tancho
description: White streaked Crimson. Medium, single. See Higo.

name: Tashiro
description: White. Medium, rose form double.

name: Tata
description: White. Large to very large, semidouble. Vigorous, spreading, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 1991 - Nuccio's).

name: Tatters
description: White. Medium, peony form. M. (Saluenensis x Japonica). (Aus. 1962 - Waterhouse).

name: Teckla Variegated
description: Deep Pink and White variation of "Teckla'.

name: Ted Kohl Variegated
description: Rose Red heavily blotched White variation of "Ted Kohl.'.

name: Telstar
description: White, inside Light Pink to Dark Pink with Rose border. Medium, semidouble with crinkled petals.

name: Temptation
description: White Blushed Pink on petal edges. Medium, single.

name: Teresa Ragland
description: Deep Coral Pink with White stamens. Large, semidouble with six separated fascicles of stamens in a circle spreading over petals and small, folded, swirled petals in center of incurved stamens. Average, open growth. M. (U.S. 1959 - Ragland).

name: Terrell Weaver Variegated
description: Color: Flame to dark red blotched white with white stamens. Form: Semidouble to loose peony form with thick fluted and twisted petals. Size: Large to very large. Bloom Season: M. Growth Rate: Vigorous. Growth Characteristics: Spreading, upright growth. Leaf Characteristics: Dark green. Heritage: Virus variegated form of Reticulata hybrid 'Terrell Weaver'.

name: Terry Gilley Variegated
description: Color: Brilliant red blotched white with yellow anthers and cream filaments. Form: Semidouble with wavy, crinkled petals. Size: Large. Bloom Season: E-M. Growth Rate: Vigorous. Growth Characteristics: Upright growth. Originator: Ray Gentry. Place of Origination: U.S. - Jackson, Mississippi. Year of Registration / Origination: 1977. Heritage: Virus variegated form of Japonica 'Terry Gilley'.

name: Thalia
description: White flower with outer half suffused with Pink. Miniature, loose peony to rose form double. Dense, semi-dwarf growth. E- M. (U.S. 1996 - Bobby Green, Green Nurseries, Fairhope, AL).

name: The Czar Variegated
description: Light Crimson blotched White variation of 'The Czar'.

name: The Flirt
description: Blush Pink. Large, semidouble with undulating, notched petals and two or three large petaloids interspersed with White stamens. Average, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1961 - Ragland).

name: The Hobbit
description: Creamy White. Small to medium, single to semidouble. Average, dense, medium growth. E. (N.Z. 2007 - Haydon).

name: The Mikado
description: Rose Pink with narrow, White border. Medium, semidouble. (Sport of Japonica 'Herme'). (Japan to England 1889 - G Waller).

name: The Pilgrim
description: Cream White. Large, full peony form. Average, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1956 - Short).

name: The Real Mccoy
description: White. Large, semidouble with irregular petals. Vigorous, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1959 - E. McCoy, Fairhope, AL).

name: The Sam Stallings
description: Cream White. Large, semidouble with stamens intermixed with petals. (U.S. 1961 - S.H. Stallings, Smithfield, NC).

name: Theola Clark
description: Rose Red. Large, peony form with White stamens. Vigorous, compact growth. M-L. (U.S. 1962 - Dr. Parker, Lucedale, MS).

name: Theresa Marie
description: White spotted Pink and Red. Medium, rose form double to peony form. Vigorous growth. (U.S. 1951 - Malbis Nursery, Daphne, AL).

name: Thomas D. Pitts
description: ('Snow White' [Rosedale Nursery]). White. Medium, formal double. Vigorous, compact, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 1954 - Nuccio's).

name: Thomasville Beauty
description: Soft Pink. Large, anemone form with some White, Pink and fimbriated petaloids. Average, spreading growth. E- M. (U.S. 1969 - Dr. M. B. Wine, Thomasville, GA).

name: Thompsonii
description: White flecked and penciled Pink; sports Light Pink to Pink. Medium, full peony form. Vigorous, open, upright growth. E-M. (England. 1838 - Thompson).

name: Tiara Variegated
description: Orange Red and White form of 'Tiara'.

name: Tick Tock
description: White striped and flecked Cherry Red. Large, full peony form. Vigorous, upright growth. E-M. (U.S. 1955 - Tick Tock).

name: Tick Tock Red Variegated
description: Red marked White variation of 'Tick Tock Red'.

name: Tick Tock Speckled
description: White speckled and marked Cherry Red. (Sport of Japonica 'Tick Tock'). (U.S. 1965 - Tick Tock).

name: Tickled Pink Variegated
description: Pink blotched White variation of "Tickled Pink'.

name: Tiffany Variegated
description: Color: Light orchid pink to deeper pink at edge blotched white with golden anthers and pinkish filaments. Form: Loose peony form to anemone form. Size: Large to very large. Bloom Season: M. Growth Rate: Vigorous. Growth Characteristics: Upright growth. Leaf Characteristics: Mid-green. Place of Origination: U.S.. Year of Registration / Origination: 1968. Heritage: Virus variegated form of Japonica 'Tiffany'.

name: Tiki
description: Rosy Salmon with petaloids streaked White and edged Rosy Salmon. Small, anemone form. Vigorous, compact, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1962 - McCaskill).

name: Til Andia
description: White. Large, semidouble. (U.S. 1955 - Domoto).

name: Tillie Wirth
description: Pink and White. Large to very large, semidouble to loose peony form. (Sport of Japonica 'Big Beauty'). (U.S. 1954 - H. G. Fritchie, Slidell, LA).

name: Tina
description: White. Large, formal double. Average, compact, upright growth. E. (U.S. 1977 - Gilley).

name: Tini Faces
description: White with Rose and Carmine stripes and specks Miniature, semidouble. Average, spreading growth. M. (U.S. 1997 - D. Mayfield, Baton Rouge, LA).

name: Tinker Bell
description: White striped Pink and Rose Red. Small, anemone form. Vigorous, upright growth. E-M. (For another form of this cultivar see 'Jingle Bells'). (U.S. 1958 - Nuccio's).

name: Tinker Toy
description: White speckled and striped Rose Red. Miniature, anemone form. Average, compact growth. M. (U.S. 1981 - Nuccio's).

name: Tinky Lee Variegated
description: Soft Rose Pink and White form of 'Tinky Lee.

name: Tinsie
description: ('Bokuhan'). Red outer guard petals and White center. Miniature, anemone form. Vigorous, upright growth. E-M. (Japan to U.S. 1930 - Star).

name: Tinsie Gold
description: Red outer guard petals and White center. Miniature, anemone form. Average, dense, upright growth. E-M. (Genetic leaf sport of Japonica "Tinsie'; 'Leaves having a wide Yellow border'). (Aus. 1992 - Dr. G. Downe, Endeavor Hills, Vic.).

name: Tinsley Smith Variegated
description: Dark Rose Red and White variation of "Tinsley Smith'.

name: Tiny Princess
description: White shaded delicate Pink. Miniature, semidouble to peony form with loose petals and small petaloids. Slow growth. E-M. (Japonica 'Akebono' x Fraterna). (U.S. 1961 - Sawada).

name: Tiny Tot
description: White with occasional Pink streak. Miniature, formal double. Average, open, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 1961 - Short).

name: Tip Top
description: White with occasional Pink stripe. Miniature, formal double. Slow, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 1980 - Mealing).

name: Togetherness
description: Pale Pink shading to Deep Pink at outer petal edges. Miniature, single, with fluted and notched petals. Slow, dense, upright growth. E-M. Fragrant. (Hybrid 'Snow Drop' seedling). (Aus. 1995 - M. Baker, Macleod, Vic.).

name: Tokayama
description: White. Medium, formal double. Average, bushy growth. M. (Japan 1956 - Satomi).

name: Toki-no-hagasane
description: Color: White blushed pink with deeper pink under petals with yellow anthers and white filaments. Form: Semidouble. Size: Medium. Bloom Season: L. Growth Rate: Slow. Growth Characteristics: Compact growth. Originator: Chugai. Place of Origination: Japan to U.S.. Year of Registration / Origination: 1934. Heritage: Old variety from Kantô area. Other Information: Japanese for 'Feathers of  wild goose'.

name: Toki-No-Hagasane (Feather Of Wild Goose)
description: ('Bessie Morse Bellingrath'; 'Ubane'; 'Kent Deigaard'; 'Betty Hopfer'). White Blushed 40 mine left in chanter Pink with Deeper Pink under petals. Medium, semidouble. Slow, Compact growth. L. (Japan 1934 - Chugai).

name: Toki-no-mai
description: Encyclopedia of Camellias in Colour, vol.II, 1978, pl.454, p.178, description, p.264: Medium size, full anemone form with one row of outer guard petals, transparent, white with red streaks and a full hemispherical centre of small, erect, com­pact, folded and twisted petals with no stamens visible. Leaves long-elliptic, keeled, thick, dark green, sometimes, apex tapered acuminate, margins irregularly and coarsely serrate, becoming obscure towards the petiole. Originated in Murakami City, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. Named and released by Tokuji Ōoka in 1973. See colour pl.: Seibundō Shinkōsha, 1980, Senchinshū, pp.105, 384, 385, Yokoyama & Kirino, 1989, Nihon no Chinka. Sport: Kifukurin-toki-no-mai.

name: Tom Cat Variegated
description: Light Rose Pink blotched White variation of "Tom Cat'.

name: Tom Hatley
description: Vivid Deep Red with Yellow anthers and White to Pink filaments. Medium to large, classic semidouble to sometimes irregular semidouble. Average, upright, open growth. M. (U.S. 2011 - M. Thomas Hatley, Charlotte, NC).

name: Tom Herrin
description: White marked Red, occasionally sporting Red. Large, semidouble to peony form. Vigorous, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1962 - Herrin).

name: Tom Thumb
description: Pink with each petal edged White. Small to medium, formal double. Average, upright growth. M. (For another form of this cultivar see Thumbellina'). (U.S. 1957 - A. Krueger, Monterey Park, CA).

name: Tomo-no-ura
description: Latest Illustrated Book of Japanese Camellias, (Camellias of Japan), 2010, p.169 with colour photo; Large semi-double, deep red widely bordered white. Flowers mid-season to late. Leaves ovate, medium size. Upright growth with excellent branching. Selected by Tomo’o Ebata from seedlings of unknown parentage. Named by Tora Ebata, released by Soshin Hirai in 2005. From Saitama Pref., Japan.

name: Tomorrow Crown Jewel
description: White brushed Red in throat with occasional streak of Red on one or more petals. Large to very large, semidouble to full peony form. (Sport of Japonica "Tomorrow'). (U.S. 1967 - Tick Tock).

name: Tomorrow Park Hill
description: Light soft Pink generally deepening toward edge with some White variegation. Large to very large, semidouble to full peony form. (Sport of Japonica "Tomorrow Variegated'). (U.S. 1964 - Peer).

name: Tomorrow Variegated
description: ('Maverick'; 'Tomorrow Supreme'; 'Tomorrow Tuxedo'). Strawberry Red blotched White variation of "Tomorrow'. Large to very large, semidouble to full peony form. (For another variation of this cultivar see 'Queen of Tomorrow').

name: Tomorrow White
description: White. Large to very large, semidouble to full peony form. (Sport of Japonica "Tomorrow'). (U.S. 1984 - J. Movich, La Verne, CA).

name: Tomorrow's Dawn
description: Deep soft Pink to Light Pink shading to White at edge with some White petaloids and one or more petals having streak of Red. Large to very large, semidouble to full peony form. (Sport of Japonica "Tomorrow'). (For a variation of this cultivar see 'Tomorrow, Leanne's'). (U.S. 1960 - L Ruffin and R Allums, Ellisville, MS).

name: Tomorrow's Dawn Bessie
description: Same as parent except wide border of White. Large to very large, semidouble to full peony form. (Sport of Japonica 'Tomorrow's Dawn'). (U.S. 1979 - B. Waters, Kilgore, TX).

name: Tomorrow's Dawn Blush
description: Blush Pink with White border. Large to very large, semidouble to full peony form. (Sport of Japonica "Tomorrow's Dawn'). (U.S. 1982 - C. Copeland, MS).

name: Tomorrow's Delight
description: Soft Creamy White with a few flecks and streaks of Red. Large to very large, semidouble to full peony form. (Sport of Japonica 'Tomorrow'). (U.S. 1971 - J. Fuller, Montgomery, AL).

name: Tomorrow's Lisa
description: Blush White with Red flakes and few streaks of Pink turning Red. Large to very large, semidouble to full peony form. (Sport of Japonica "Tomorrow Park Hill'). (U.S. 1977 - A. P. Fatherree, Jackson, MS).

name: Tomorrow's Sweet Image
description: Pale Orchid Pink heavily variegated White. Large to very large, semidouble to full peony form. (Sport of Japonica "Tomorrow Park Hill'). (Reported as similar to "Tomorrow Park Hill'). (U.S. 1972 -J L Carvain, Fort Worth, TX).

name: Tomorrow's Tropic Dawn
description: White with occasional Red line or dash, fading to Blush as flower ages. Large to very large, semidouble to full peony form. (Sport of Japonica 'Tomorrow's Dawn'). (U.S. 1967 - R M Merino, Fresno, CA).

name: Tomorrow, Marbury's Light Pink
description: Light Pink edged Lighter Pink to White. Large to very large, semidouble to full peony form. (Sport of Japonica 'Tomorrow'). (U.S. 1977 - Marbury).

name: Tootsie
description: Chalk White. Miniature, formal double, sometimes in form of five pointed star. Slow, open, spreading growth. M-L. (U.S. 1967 - R. E. Ward, Jr., Birmingham, AL).

name: Tori-No-Ko (Chicken)
description: Pinkish White slightly striped Darker Pink. Large, semidouble of lotus form. Slow, compact growth. M-L. (Japan 1956 - E. Satomi, Tokyo).

name: Totts
description: White to Creamy Yellow. Very large, peony form. Vigorous, dense, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 1993 - R. Ehrhart, Walnut Creek, CA).

name: Tricolor (Siebold)
description: ('Wakanoura Variegated'). Waxy White streaked Carmine. Medium, semidouble of slightly cupped form. Vigorous, compact, upright growth. M. Cold hardy. (Variations of this cultivar include 'Dainty (California)'; 'Fred Sander'; 'Jewel Bowden'; 'Blush Tricolor'; 'Chalk Pink'). (Japan to Germany 1832 - Siebold).

name: Tricolor (Siebold) White
description: See 'Leucantha'.

name: Tricolor California
description: ('Crichton'). White striped Pink to solid color. Medium, semidouble. Average, compact growth. L. (For other forms of this cultivar, see 'Annie Laurie' and 'Lois Hill'). (U.S. 1928 - Armstrong).

name: Tricolor Nova
description: Color: White with pink stripes with yellow anthers and cream filaments. Form: Semidouble. Size: Medium. Leaf Characteristics: Light green. Originator: Obtained from seed by Mr Mathot of Ghent, Belgium and sold to Mr Miellez of Lille. Place of Origination: Belgium. Year of Registration / Origination: 1846.

name: Tricolor Pink
description: Pink with Rose color stripes with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Medium, semidouble of slightly cupped form. Vigorous, compact, upright growth. M. (Sport of Japonica 'Tricolor'). (U.S. 1937 - E. A. Mellhenny (Jungle Gardens), Avery Island, LA).

name: Tricolor Superba
description: Variable colors from nearly White to Solid Red, but majority White striped Red or Light Pink margined White with Golden anthers and White filaments. Large, semidouble. M. (Seedling of Japonica 'Tricolor'). (U.S. 1945 - K. Sawada, Overlook Nurseries, Crichton, AL).

name: Triumph
description: Dark Red with Golden anthers and White filaments. Small, single. (U.S. 1942 - Magnolia Plantation & Gardens, Charleston, SC).

name: Triumphans
description: ('Triomphant'; 'Lady Parker Peony'; 'Allen's Pink'; 'Harmony'). Rose Pink splotched White. Large, peony form. Upright, open growth. M. (Belgium 1834).

name: Trixey
description: Bright Pink shading to White at center. Large, formal double. Slow, compact growth. L. (N.Z. 1998 - F. Upson, Kaponga).

name: True Love
description: Chalk White with faint dash of Pink on some petals. Large, semidouble to peony form shaped like six-pointed star. (U.S. 1959 - True's Camellias, Columbia, SC).

name: Tsubaki's Bona Bella
description: Rose Pink with White marks on the center of the petals. Medium to large, formal double. Vigorous, upright Q Aa 0 : growth. M-L. (Japonica 'Steve Blount' seedling). (U.S. 2013 - Tsubaki Camellias, Savannah, GA).

name: Tsugawa
description: Pink fading to White at edges. Small, rose form double.

name: Tsukasa-Nishiki
description: Pink mottled White. Medium, single. See Higo.

name: Tsuki-no-wa
description: Color: Red with white stripes with gold anthers and white filaments. Form: Single. Size: Large. Bloom Season: M. Growth Rate: Vigorous. Growth Characteristics: Upright growth. Leaf Characteristics: Light green. Originator: Katsumi Miyahara. Place of Origination: Japan - Kurume City, Fuku'oka Prefecture. Year of Registration / Origination: 1975. Heritage: Chance seedling. Other Information: Means Halo Around Moon in Japanese.

name: Tudor Baby Variegated
description: Dark Red, blotched White variation of "Tudor Baby'. (U.S. 2001 - Hulyn Smith).

name: Turkish Delight
description: Light Lavender Pink. Medium, semidouble with long, narrow, fluted, reflexed petals. Bushy growth. M. (Hybrid 'Cornish Snow' seedling). (Aus. 1968 - Tuckfield).

name: Turner Davis
description: White. Medium, formal double with incurved petals. Vigorous, compact, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 1980 - J. Hintermister, Gainesville, FL).

name: Tutti Frutti
description: Multi-colors of White, Pink, and Red with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Medium, anemone form. Slow, upright, dense growth. E-M. (U.S. 2020 - Pat B. Johnson, Cairo, GA).

name: Twinkle
description: White, occasionally dashed Pink. Miniature, semidouble. Average, upright growth. L. (U.S. 1958).

name: Twinkle Twinkle
description: White, sometimes tinted Pink. Miniature, semidouble form with pointed petals, star shaped. (U.S. - Nuccio's)

name: Tyler Studstill
description: Deep Wine Red with Yellow anthers and White filaments. Large, semidouble form with at least one large petaloid mixed with the stamens. Average, upright, open growth. M. (U.S. 2022 - Gordy).

name: Unforgettable White
description: White with Yellow anthers and Light Yellow filaments. Large, anemone form to peony form. Vigorous, upright growth. E-L. (Sport of Japonica 'Unforgettable').

name: Utsukushi-Asaye Variegated
description: Coral and White variation of 'Utsukushi-Asaye'. (U.S. 1979 - Kramer).

name: Vale Beauty
description: White shading Pink in outer petals. Medium, formal double. Vigorous, compact upright growth. M. (Aus. 1977 - Camellia Vale Nsy., Bexley North, NSW).

name: Vale Mist
description: White. Small, semidouble.

name: Valentine Day Variegated
description: Salmon Pink and White form of 'Valentine Day'. (U.S. 1975).

name: Valley Dee
description: White, sometimes streaked faint, soft Pink. Medium, loose peony form. Average, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1959 - Brodies Nsy., Biloxi, MS).

name: Valley Dee Blush
description: Very Light Purple with darker veining and White patches on some petals with Light Yellow anthers. Medium, loose peony form. Average, upright growth. M. (Sport of Japonica 'Valley Dee').

name: Valtevareda Variegated
description: Pink blotched White form of "Valtevareda'.

name: Vanilla Cream
description: Creamy White with Gold anthers and Yellow filaments. Medium to large, semidouble to anemone form. Average, spreading, open growth. E-M. (Seedling of Japonica 'Betty Sheffield Supreme'). (U.S. 2021 - Pat Johnson, Cairo, GA).

name: Vanilla Moon
description: Blush Pink with White anthers and White filaments. Small, single. Vigorous, upright growth. E. (Seedling of Hybrid 'Yume'). (U.S. 2016 - Tsubaki Camellias, Savannah, GA).

name: Vannine
description: White to White striped Pink to Pink. Medium to large, semidouble. Vigorous, bushy growth. M. (Japonica 'Lady Vansittart' × Reticulata 'Crimson Robe'). (U.S. 1968 - Feathers)

name: Variegata
description: Deep Rose Pink mottled White. Medium, semidouble. Spreading growth. M. (China to England [Chandler] 1792).

name: Variety Z
description: Dark Pink (RHS 62A); the petaloids are Light Pink (RHS 62C) with Creamy White anthers and White filaments. Medium, rose form double to formal double. Average, upright growth. M. (U.S. 2009 - P. W. Zimmerman and Boyce Thompson, Yonkers, NY).

name: Vashti
description: White splotched and striped Pink with occasional Red flowers. Medium, rose form double. Average, compact growth. M-L. (U.S. 1945 - F. L. Gibson, Thomasville, GA).

name: Vaughn Drinkard
description: Light Salmon to Dark Rose and White. (Variegated variation of Japonica 'Dona Julia'). (U.S. 1955 - Longview).

name: Velma Grantham
description: White to Blush Pink with Pink flecks and stripes, sporting Blush, Dark Pink, White striped Pink and Blush bordered Deep Rose. Medium to large, semidouble with upright petals. Average, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1962 - A. Grantham, Bogalusa, LA).

name: Velma Grantham Variegated
description: Pink flecked, blotched and moired White variation of 'Velma Grantham'.

name: Velvet Brocade
description: Cranberry Red. Medium, formal double form with tiny incurved petals which sometimes show White tips. Vigorous, upright, open growth. E-M. (Seedling of Japonica 'Beatrix Hoyt'). (U.S. 2020 - Pat B. Johnson, Cairo, GA).

name: Velvet Glow
description: White. Large, anemone form. Vigorous, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1961 - R. W. Wilder, Fairhope, AL).

name: Vergine Di Colle Beato
description: ('Virgine Calubini'; 'Virgin of the Blessed Hill'). White. Medium, formal double with six to seven geometric swirls in either clockwise or counter clockwise formation. M-L. (Belgium [Versch] from Italy 1857; 'To U.S. from Sevesi - mid 1960's').

name: Vernal Breeze
description: White. Small, single. Vigorous, upright growth; the plant has long arching branches. E. Very fragrant. (Japonica × C. lutchuensis). (U.S. 2014 - Camellia Forest, Chapel Hill, NC).

name: Vernon E. Howell, Jr.
description: Rose with White splotches. Large, formal double to rose form double. Vigorous. upright growth. (Seedling of Japonica 'Mary Agnes Patin'). (U.S. 2019 - Vernon E. Howell, Lucedale, MS).

name: Vi Stone
description: Deep bright Pink veined Red and edged White. Large, formal double to rose form double. Vigorous, upright growth. M-L. (Reticulata 'Cornelian' × Reticulata hybrid 'Jean Pursel'). (U.S. 1980 - Pursel).

name: Victory White
description: White. Medium, semidouble to loose peony form. Vigorous, open, upright growth. M. (U.S. 1939 - Overlook).

name: Vida Davis
description: White washed Blush Pink. Large, semidouble with large petals and prominent bunched Ochre tipped stamens. (U.S. 1959).

name: Ville De Nantes
description: Dark Red blotched White. Medium to large, semidouble with upright, fimbriated petals. M-L. (Sport of Japonica 'Donckelarii'). (For a variation of this cultivar see 'Lady Kay'). (France 1910 - Heurtin, Nantes).

name: Virgin's Blush
description: White flushed faintest Pink with Yellow anthers and Yellow filaments. Medium, semidouble to peony form. Vigorous, upright growth. M. (Seedling of Japonica 'Orandagasa'). (U.S. 1944 - E. A. Mellhenny [Jungle Gardens], Avery Island, LA).

name: Virginal
description: Greenish White. Medium, semidouble to anemone form. Open, upright growth. M. (Aus. 1945 - Linton).

name: Virgine Calubini

name: Yutianshi
description: Yutianshi ( C. japonica ). China Regn. # 2014-155 . China Flowers & Horticulture , 2014 , No. 18 , p. 51 . Flowers anemone form, white with red dots, medium size, over 8 cm diameter, outer petals broadly rotund, flat, emarginate, inner petals irregular or wavy, becoming smaller to the center. Leaves elliptic, deep green, thin, 6-9 cm long, 3-6 cm wide. Plant upright, growth fast. Flowers early to mid-season.

name: Yuyake-Fuji
description: Deep Pink veined deeper, fading to White margins. Small, single - 5-6 petals. (Sport of Japonica 'Yuri-shibori'). (Japan 1988).

name: Yvonne Marie
description: White. Very large, peony form. Slow, spreading growth. M-L. (Saluenensis × Japonica). (N.Z. 1984 - Mrs. J. E. Shaw, Wairua).

name: Yvonne Tyson Variegated
description: Phlox Pink and White variation of 'Yvonne Tyson'.

name: Zac Chaffin
description: Red and White streaked. Large, semidouble. Vigorous, spreading, upright growth. M-L. (U.S. 1994 - Dr. O. Lewis, Picayune, MS).

name: Zebulon
description: Soft White petals very lightly tinted with soft Amaranth, chopped and streaked with Vermillion with Yellow anthers and Yellow filaments. Large, semidouble to full peony. Average, upright and spreading growth. M. (U.S. 2014 - Waters, England, imported from Nantes, France).

name: Zhanqun Mingzhu
description: Flowers semi-double to loose peony form, variegated white to light pink, large to very large (13–14 cm), with 22–25 wavy petals and occasional yellow stamens at the center. Leaves elliptic, deep green, glossy and thick (12–17 × 5.5–9 cm) with prominent veining and serrate margins. Plant upright with fast growth; mid-season flowering.

name: Zuiko-Nishiki
description: White splashed Red. Medium, single. See Higo. SPECIES RETICULATA AND HYBRIDS WITH RETICULATA PARENTAGE The predominant opinion is that Reticulata is not a distinct species, but is a hybrid of other species, probably largely Pitardii (which also is believed to be a hybrid) fractionally combined with Japonica. The Yunnan Reticulatas, which were imported into the United States from China in 1948 by Ralph S. Peer of Hollywood, California and Descanso Gardens of La Canada, generally have been listed as Reticulata and their seedlings listed as Reticulata Hybrids. It is the opinion of many that such cultivars represent garden forms of Reticulata, some Pitardii Variety Yunnanica, and some hybrid crosses of both. Many such cultivars are over three hundred years old. Therefore, it is believed advisable to list Reticulatas and Hybrids with Reticulata Parentage in this same section. Cultivars imported directly to the United States from China during the 40's, 50's, 60's and 80's have been marked with an asterisk (*).

name: Ō-zora
description: Taniguchi, 1912, Chinka Kyōkan: Blush pink, medium. Hiratsuka, Taizō, 1964, Higo Camellia, pp.12, 17. Tuyama, 1968, Camellias of Japan, pl.114, p.57, description, p.121 as ‘Oo-zora’: Higo Camellia, pink (Rose Madder 23/3). Leaves ovoid-elliptic to broadly elliptic, rounded at the base; petioles glabrous; petals about 6, incurved and strongly wavy. Staminal tube very broad with stamens in a circle. About 8 cm across with 120 stamens with white filaments. Colour photos and descriptions, see: Adachi, 1960, Camellia. Its Appreciation & Artistic .... p.65; Seibundō Shinkōsha, 1979, Senchinshū, pp.26, 204. Corruption of Japanese name: ‘Ozorah’. Different reading: ‘Oo-zora’, ‘Oh-zora’. Originated in the Kumamoto Prefecture, Japan.
